title: E-TEST — Testing infrastructure (shared harness, model-gated e2e, structured logging, watchdogs, CI)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is a pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for ONE model (Baidu Unlimited-OCR, a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). The whole project lives or dies on the doctrine **G1 > G2: correctness outranks speed, always** (AGENTS.md doctrine #1) — a faster kernel that drifts OCR output is reverted and memorialized in `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`. That doctrine is only enforceable if the test machinery is first-class. The user explicitly wants **comprehensive unit tests AND end-to-end test scripts with great detailed logging**.

This epic is the **cross-cutting testing INFRASTRUCTURE** that every per-phase test bead (P1-*, P2-*, P3-*, P4-*) and the E-VERIFY gauntlet build on top of. It is NOT the parity-ladder logic itself (E-VERIFY owns the L0–L5 ladder, oracle wiring, ULP tables, conformal ratchet, e-processes). E-TEST owns the **plumbing**: the golden-fixture harness + PROVENANCE, the model-gated e2e runner (skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights but PROVE the native path ran), the structured JSON-line test-logging convention every stage emits, per-module unit-test scaffolding standards, the end-to-end CLI script (`focr ocr golden-image → compare --json to frozen reference`), the `many_pages_without_deadlock` concurrency watchdog, the benchmark-guardrail regression gate, and the CI wiring that runs all of it.

## WHAT THIS EPIC DELIVERS (the tasks)
- TEST-fixture-harness — golden corpus + frozen-reference loader + canonicalization + `PROVENANCE.md` (transformers==4.57.1, torch==2.10.0, git ref, exact command).
- TEST-log-conventions — the structured NDJSON test-logging schema: every stage logs `{stage, inputs, shapes, dtype, elapsed_us, parity_delta, seed, simd_tier}`.
- TEST-log-emitter — the reusable Rust `TestLog` emitter crate-internal module wired into all test scaffolds.
- TEST-unit-scaffolding — the per-module unit-test standard (happy/edge/error + structured logging) all module authors follow.
- TEST-model-gated-runner — the harness that skips-with-SUCCESS without weights and PROVES the native path ran (fallbacks pointed at `/nonexistent`).
- TEST-e2e-cli-script — `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` + `scripts/run_e2e.sh`: `focr ocr <golden> --json` → compare to frozen reference, full logging.
- TEST-deadlock-watchdog — `many_pages_without_deadlock` concurrency watchdog (pages ≫ pool, CI timeout on regression).
- TEST-bench-guardrail — benchmark-regression gate vs a frozen baseline, skips-green without fixtures.
- TEST-determinism-gate — shared same-input-twice → byte-identical assertion helper.
- TEST-robot-contract-harness — frozen JSON-schema fixture + validator the robot NDJSON contract test reuses (shared with E-VERIFY surface parity, owned here as plumbing).
- TEST-ci-wiring — GitHub Actions wiring that runs unit + conformance + e2e (model-gated) + deadlock watchdog + bench-guardrail across the 5-target matrix.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Model-gated, not model-required.** The 6.67 GB bf16 single-shard checkpoint (`model-00001-of-000001.safetensors`, 2710 tensors) is NOT in CI. Tests that need it skip-with-SUCCESS so CI stays green, but they MUST prove the native path actually ran when weights ARE present (point any fallback at `/nonexistent` so a silently-skipped native path fails loudly).
- **Logging is a feature, not decoration.** The plan mandates "comprehensive tests with great logging." Every stage logs inputs/shapes/timings/parity-deltas as machine-parseable NDJSON so a failing test is diagnosable from logs alone, and so the gauntlet's artifact graph can ingest test evidence.
- **Fixtures are frozen + provenanced.** `/testing-golden-artifacts` + `/testing-conformance-harnesses` doctrine: fixture provenance is MANDATORY; intentional divergences are `XFAIL` (never `SKIP`), each a `DISC-NNN` in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md`. `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` workflow with mandatory `git diff` review; `*.actual` gitignored; CI never auto-updates.
- **Deadlock is an architectural risk with a named test.** The frankensearch deadlock saga converged after 5 commits on: single OcrModel, SEQUENTIAL outer page loop, forward fans out internally, NEVER nest rayon under a held lock, NEVER nest a 2nd asupersync runtime. The `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (pages ≫ pool) hangs on regression — that hang IS the signal.
- **No mocks where a real path exists.** Per `/testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks`: the e2e script drives the real `focr` binary against real golden images and the real frozen reference JSON.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A model-gated test that silently skips because weights are absent must NOT also silently skip when weights ARE present but the native path is broken — the `/nonexistent` fallback trick is the guard.
- Robot NDJSON has timing/run-id fields → goldens must be SCRUBBED (strip timing, run-id, durations) before comparison or every run diffs.
- Cross-platform line endings/paths → canonicalize before golden compare.
- The deadlock watchdog must run with a HARD wall-clock timeout in CI (a deadlock manifests as a hang, not a panic).
- Bench-guardrail must skip gracefully (exit 0) when the model fixture is absent — never red-flag CI for a missing 6.67 GB file.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (epic-level)
- `cargo test` green on all 5 targets without weights (model-gated tests skip-with-SUCCESS, logged as SKIPPED-NO-MODEL).
- With weights present, model-gated e2e PROVES the native path ran (asserts a native-path sentinel in the structured log) and compares `--json` to the frozen reference.
- Every test emits structured NDJSON test-log lines with the agreed schema; a log-schema validator passes over captured logs.
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` completes within its wall-clock budget; an injected nested-rayon-under-lock regression makes it time out (proven once).
- Bench-guardrail compares to a frozen baseline and fails on >threshold regression, skips-green without fixtures.
- CI runs unit + conformance + e2e + watchdog + bench-guardrail; one workflow, documented.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This epic IS the test infrastructure; its own meta-tests are: (a) a self-test that the model-gated runner correctly skips without weights and fails-loud on a broken native path with weights present; (b) a self-test that the NDJSON test-log schema validator rejects a malformed log line; (c) a self-test that the deadlock watchdog times out under an injected violation.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the scaffold (crate layout, `tests/`, `tests/fixtures/`, `benches/`, robot NDJSON skeleton, `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py`, model resolution / `native_model_available()`) before any harness can be wired.
- depends_on **E-VERIFY**: E-VERIFY owns the parity ladder semantics, oracle wiring, ULP tolerance tables, conformal lower-bound ratchet, and e-processes that the harness EMITS into and the conformance fixtures compare against. E-TEST is the plumbing those verification semantics ride on.

title: Tests: CER/TEDS/CVaR/EVT harness correctness + per-bit-width curve reproducibility
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The accuracy harness gates the entire phase; if CER/CVaR/EVT are computed wrong, every quality decision is wrong. This bead proves the metrics and the curve are correct and reproducible.

## WHAT TO DO
- Unit: CER on synthetic strings with known edit distance; TEDS on synthetic HTML tables; CVaR_0.1 on a synthetic per-doc CER distribution with a known worst-10% mean; EVT generalized-Pareto fit on a synthetic heavy-tailed sample with a known p999.
- Reproducibility: the per-bit-width curve is stable across two runs within the oracle nondeterminism floor.
- Floor: assert the recorded oracle nondeterminism envelope is consumed (thresholds derived from it, not hardcoded).
- NDJSON logging of every metric per doc/config.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Metrics match known answers on synthetic inputs; curve reproducible within floor; thresholds derived from the measured floor.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-dense-corpus`: tests the corpus/harness it depends on.

title: Benchmark-guardrail regression gate (vs frozen baseline; skips-green without fixtures; honest, fairness-controlled)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Perf is gated behind correctness (G1 > G2), but once a perf lever lands it must not silently regress. Plan §9.6: "A benchmark-guardrail gate catches regressions vs a frozen baseline." §8.5 keep-gate: both focused + broad benches from the same git state / same `target/` / same minute; `release-perf` profile only; `cv_pct` reported (>5% = noise, ineligible); pass-over-pass `.bench-history` ratchet. This bead builds the guardrail that compares current Criterion results to a frozen baseline and fails CI (or the perf-loop) on a real regression — while staying HONEST (fairness controls) and skipping-green without the model fixture.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `benches/guardrail.rs` (or a `scripts/bench_guardrail.sh` wrapper around the Criterion benches in `benches/`):
1. **Bench targets** (the §4.1 `benches/` set): `vision_encode`, `decode_token`, `end_to_end`, plus the `gauntlet` head-to-head (§9.3). Measure p50/p95/p99 wall-clock + memory per the §9.1 profiling discipline.
2. **Frozen baseline + ratchet**: store a committed baseline (`benches/.bench-history` / `artifacts/perf/baseline.json`); compare current vs baseline; FAIL on a regression beyond a documented threshold; the ratchet only moves the baseline forward on an explicit, reviewed update (never auto).
3. **Profile across the OUTPUT-LENGTH axis** (§9.1): the corpus spans {sparse page (vision-prefill-dominated), dense page, 10-page, 40-page (decode-dominated)} — rank/compare per regime, because the cost center MOVES with output length.
4. **Honest fairness controls** (§9.3, MANDATORY for any head-to-head): thread parity (pin `OMP_NUM_THREADS`/torch `set_num_threads(N)` == focr's budget; NEVER benchmark torch at @64 — measure @8/@32); allocator fairness (same allocator posture both sides; mimalloc behind a feature if used); best-of-N with warmup discard, report the min + precision; per-stage ratios (preprocess/vision-encode/prefill/decode-per-token); precision annotation per row (focr-int8 vs torch-bf16). Tag each row OK/warn/slower/"focr faster".
5. **Roofline annotation** (§9.1): next to each ratio, record the compute floor (int8/int4 GEMM FLOPs ÷ arch peak int8) and memory floor (bytes ÷ DRAM BW); flag any stage >~1.3× above its floor as a named attackable lever.
6. **Skips-green without fixtures** (§9.6): if the model fixture / reference cmd is absent, benches exit 0 (logged as skipped) — never red-flag CI for a missing 6.67 GB file.
7. **Structured logging**: emit a `TestLog`/NDJSON row per bench: `{bench, regime, simd_tier, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us, cv_pct, baseline_us, ratio, roofline_floor_us, fairness:{threads,allocator,precision}, verdict}`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Frozen baseline + manual ratchet** so a regression is caught but the baseline can only improve under review (no silent drift).
- **`cv_pct` gate** (>5% = noise, ineligible) so a noisy bench never produces a fake win/loss.
- **Fairness controls are mandatory, not optional** — a perf number across different thread/allocator/precision postures is meaningless (the hardened frankentorch lesson: never benchmark torch at @64).
- **Roofline next to every ratio** so a stage sitting >1.3× above its floor is a named lever, not an excuse (§9.1).
- **Per-stage, per-regime** because G2 is per-stage honest: decode-per-token faster is the gating part; vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is acceptable and recorded openly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Wall-clock benches are noisy in CI — use `release-perf` profile, best-of-N, warmup discard, and the `cv_pct` gate; consider running the guardrail as a separate (non-blocking-by-default) job that flags rather than hard-fails until baselines stabilize.
- The gauntlet head-to-head requires the Phase −1 proven CPU reference (`FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD`/`FOCR_REFERENCE_PYTHON`); if that reference isn't available, the gauntlet rows skip-green and only the self-relative ratchet runs.
- Never let a perf regression be reported as a correctness pass — the guardrail re-states the parity receipt ("text exact, max logit diff X, deterministic") alongside the ratio (§9.2).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Guardrail compares current vs frozen baseline per (bench, regime) and FAILS on a regression beyond threshold; ratchet only advances under review.
- Skips-green (exit 0, logged) without the model/reference fixtures.
- Every row carries `cv_pct`, roofline floor, fairness annotations, per-stage ratio, precision annotation; noisy rows (cv>5%) marked ineligible.
- A self-test injecting a deliberate slowdown makes the guardrail FAIL (proves it catches regressions).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Self-test: injected slowdown → guardrail FAIL.
- Self-test: missing fixtures → skip-green.
- Self-test: a cv>5% row is marked ineligible, not a win/loss.
- A structured-log validation over emitted bench rows.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the `benches/` scaffold + `release-perf` profile + the gauntlet reference-cmd env wiring.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the per-bench NDJSON rows.

title: Phase 2 — weight-only int8 decoder (the validated quant recipe)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
`franken_ocr` is a pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for ONE model: Baidu Unlimited-OCR (a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B-MoE VLM with R-SWA). The over-arching goal is *maximum CPU speed for this one model* via int4/int8 weights + fused-at-peak kernels, with **correctness before speed** (G1 > G2), shipped as a portable single binary, agent-ergonomic.

Phase 2 is the **first quantization phase**: it turns the working pure-f32 forward from Phase 1 into a **weight-only int8 decoder** following the recipe already validated by the two community quants (GGUF, NVFP4). Decode is **memory-bandwidth-bound**, so halving the weight bytes (bf16→int8) is the single biggest correctness-safe decode lever before int4. int8 (Q8_0-class) is also our **correctness oracle** for the int4 work that follows (Phase 4): we must prove int8 is OCR-lossless before pushing lower.

## WHAT TO DO (epic scope)
Deliver, behind the `.focrq` custom on-disk format:
1. `.focrq` reader/writer (magic `b"FOCRQ\0"`, header, `arch_target`, MIT notice, tensor directory, inline scales, bf16 high-precision verbatim).
2. `focr convert --quant int8`: per-output-channel symmetric quant (zero-point 0, `scale = max|w_row|/127`) of the decoder linears ONLY; vision tower, projector, `embed_tokens`, MoE **router gate**, and ALL norms kept **bf16 verbatim**.
3. The i32-overflow **proof obligation** + unit test at worst-case K=6848 on every arch.
4. Per-row dynamic activation quant at forward time.
5. **STAGED** quantization, each stage its own parity gate + ledger entry: (2a) FFN/expert GEMMs int8 → (2b) attention q/k/v/o int8 behind `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` → (2c) `lm_head` int8 behind `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`.
6. Round-trip / determinism gate; differential parity int8-vs-f32-vs-reference.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Quant recipe is FIXED and validated (AGENTS doctrine 2): quantize the decoder FFN/expert GEMMs only.** Keep high precision the entire vision tower, projector, `embed_tokens`, the MoE router gate, and ALL norms. Quantizing the vision encoder wrecks OCR — both prior-art quants keep it. int8 on attention q/k/v/o and on `lm_head` go *beyond* the validated set (NVFP4 likely skips them — OQ-14) and are OUR CER risk → each behind a measured-CER kill-switch, never assumed lossless.
- **bf16, NOT f16, for high-precision storage.** The checkpoint is bf16 (1-8-7); narrowing to f16 (1-5-10) is lossy (clips at ±65504, different mantissa) and is NOT a byte-identical round-trip. Both are 2 bytes — no disk reason to prefer the lossy one. f16 storage is only ever a ledgered `DISCREPANCIES.md` divergence, never the silent default.
- **int8 SIMD path is bit-identical to scalar** (integer add is associative/exact) — no tolerance needed on the SIMD-vs-scalar axis. Only the int8-vs-f32 *forward* drifts, and that `max_diff` must be **MEASURED for this model**, not inherited (frankensearch's 0.055 is a BERT-reranker figure on different shapes/depth).
- **i32-overflow is a PROOF, not an assumption (AGENTS doctrine 6).** Do NOT inherit frankensearch's `k≤1536` bound. Worst case here is dense layer-0 `down_proj` at K=6848.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Accuracy under quant for small/dense text is the HIGH risk (numbers, tables, code, sub/superscripts are exact-token-sensitive; perplexity under-predicts this). Gate on **CER over a dense numeric/table corpus**, not perplexity.
- A regression in any of stages 2a/2b/2c reverts *just that stage* (revert source, memorialize in `NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`). Never ship a stage that fails its parity gate.
- The oracle's OWN nondeterminism floor (bf16, 129280-vocab argmax) must already be established (by E-VERIFY) before any int8 tolerance is set; an int8 divergence *inside the oracle's bf16 noise* is not a bug.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- int8 end-to-end CER within the ledgered budget of f32 (target: within noise, matching NVFP4's "identical to BF16" bar).
- L3 logits within the *measured* int8 tolerance; L4 decoded text **exact** where the reference is deterministic.
- Round-trip deterministic (same source → byte-identical `.focrq`; same input twice → identical output under greedy).
- `.focrq` size ≈ Q8_0-class for the int8 decoder set.
- i32 accumulator proven not to overflow at K=6848 on every arch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
The full ladder under this epic's child beads: format round-trip tests, converter determinism tests, the K=6848 overflow unit test on every arch, per-stage differential parity gates (2a/2b/2c) vs the f32 path and the E-VERIFY oracle, and an end-to-end CER gate on the dense corpus with structured logging (per-stage CER, max logit diff, first-divergence token position).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-P1** (Phase 1 fp32 parity): we need a working, parity-proven f32 forward to quantize *against* — int8 is measured as a delta from f32, which is itself a delta from the reference.
- **E-VERIFY** (verification gauntlet/oracle): supplies the reference oracle fixtures, the oracle nondeterminism floor, and the L0–L5 parity ladder machinery that every int8 stage gate runs through.

title: .focrq on-disk format specification + version/provenance contract
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The whole 'transform the bf16 weights into a custom quantized on-disk form' mandate flows through one container format: `.focrq`. Every reader/writer/converter/kernel bead depends on a single frozen spec. Writing the spec down first (before code) prevents drift between the writer and the reader and pins the license-compliance and provenance story. This serves the portable-single-binary goal: the runtime loads a self-describing, dependency-free byte-range index — exactly like franken_whisper's ggml/safetensors loader pattern.

## WHAT TO DO
Produce `docs/focrq-format.md` (the frozen spec). The container is safetensors-like, length-prefixed, self-describing; the reader reads the whole file into ONE `Vec<u8>` blob (or mmap), validates magic, reads the header, and indexes tensors by byte range. The EXACT layout (transcribed from plan §5.2):

```
magic:           b"FOCRQ\0"        # loud rejection on mismatch
format_version:  u32               # bumped on ANY layout change
arch_target:     enum { Generic, Aarch64Smmla, X86Vnni, X86Amx }   # which pre-packing
source_sha256:   [u8;32]           # sha256 of the SOURCE safetensors (provenance)
license_notice:  utf8             # "Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu — MIT License ..." (MUST be present)
model_config:    json             # frozen copy of the relevant config.json fields
tensor_directory: [
  { name,
    dtype: {F32, F16, BF16, QInt8PerChan, QInt4PerGroup},
    shape,
    packing,
    byte_offset, byte_len,
    scales_offset, scales_len,
    group_size?,   # int4 only
    tier?          # int4 only
  }
]
payload: <raw bytes>
```

Spec MUST nail down, in prose + a byte-table:
- Endianness (little-endian), alignment of payload regions, how lengths are encoded (length-prefix widths).
- **Inline scales convention:** int8 carries a per-output-channel `Vec<f32>` of scales in its `scales_offset/scales_len` region (`scale = max|w_row|/127`, zero-point 0). int4 carries per-group scales (group 16–32) + `tier` metadata (this spec defines the fields now; int4 *writing* is Phase 4).
- **High-precision tensors stored BF16 verbatim** (not f16), dequantized BF16→f32 at load.
- `format_version` semantics: loader REFUSES a `.focrq` whose `format_version` exceeds the binary's; loader WARNS (not errors) on `arch_target` mismatch and falls back to the generic packing.
- `license_notice` MUST be non-empty — the MIT/Baidu attribution is a redistribution requirement (the model is MIT, Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Self-describing, version-gated, provenance-stamped** so a stale/wrong `.focrq` is caught at load, not as garbage OCR output. `source_sha256` + `format_version` + frozen config travel with the artifact.
- **One canonical artifact, many packings**: the same source emits per-arch blobs (`--arch`), so the dtype/shape/scales are logically identical across packings; only `arch_target` + the `packing` field differ. CI verifies all packings dequantize to the same logical weights.
- **BF16 verbatim** (not f16) — see the round-trip bead; this is the byte-identical-round-trip guarantee.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If the spec is ambiguous on scales placement or length encoding, the reader and writer will silently disagree → fuzzy logits with no error. The byte-table must be exhaustive.
- `model_config` must be the FROZEN relevant config fields from the E-PM1 truth pack, NOT a live re-read.
- Do not promote any `[OPEN]` shape into the format before E-PM1 resolves it.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/focrq-format.md` exists, fully specifies every field with byte offsets/widths, the magic, the `arch_target` enum, the dtype enum, the inline-scales layout for QInt8PerChan, the version-refusal + arch-warn rules, and the mandatory license_notice.
- Spec is internally consistent with §5.2/§5.3/§5.4 of the plan and references the E-PM1 frozen config.

## TESTS REQUIRED
None directly (this is the spec). The reader/writer beads (P2-focrq-reader, P2-focrq-writer) and their tests depend on this.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1** (Truth Pack): supplies the frozen `config.json` fields for `model_config` and the pinned source provenance for `source_sha256`. The format cannot freeze a config it hasn't pinned.

title: Stage 2b — int8 attention q/k/v/o behind FOCR_INT8_ATTN kill-switch
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The attention q/k/v/o projections are the SECOND int8 stage, and they go **BEYOND the validated set** — NVFP4 likely skips attention (OQ-14: its ~2196 modules don't reconcile with our ~2229 census, ~33 short, suggesting it skips q/k/v/o and/or lm_head and/or shared experts). So int8 attention is OUR CER risk to retire by measurement, gated behind a separate `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` kill-switch. ONNX runs Q·Kᵀ and scores·V in int8 (§3.2 lever 3), so there's a perf reason to want it — but ONLY if accuracy holds.

## WHAT TO DO
Quantize `decoder.layer[i].attn.{q,k,v,o}` (i in 0..11) to int8 (per-output-channel symmetric + per-row dynamic activations), **only when `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` is enabled**. Plain MHA (use_mla=false): q/k/v/o are separate dense GEMMs, 10 heads, head_dim 128, K=1280. With the gate OFF, attention stays high precision (Phase 1 / stage 2a state). The kill-switch is an env var read at load/convert time; the `.focrq` records whether attention is int8 (dtype in the directory).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Behind a kill-switch, never assumed lossless** (AGENTS doctrine 2). If the stage-2b gate regresses CER beyond the ledgered budget, the kill-switch defaults OFF and the stage is reverted — memorialized in NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md.
- **OQ-14 must resolve first** — we must know whether NVFP4 validates attention int8 or not, so we know if this is validated-by-prior-art or pure-our-risk.
- K=1280 → S8S8 ≤ 20.6M, U8S8 ≤ 41.4M — comfortably within i32 (covered by the overflow proof).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Attention is more sensitive than FFN; a small q/k drift can change the softmax distribution and flip tokens. The gate must be strict.
- The R-SWA reference-block attention reads quantized q/k/v — ensure the quant doesn't interact badly with the constant-KV ring buffer (the V_proj is the most sensitive; in Phase 4 it stays one tier higher, but in Phase 2 it's uniform int8).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- With `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` on, q/k/v/o run int8; off, they're high precision.
- Passes the stage-2b parity gate (P2-int8-attn-parity-gate) or the kill-switch defaults OFF with a NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry.
- i32 accumulator safe (K=1280, proven).

## TESTS REQUIRED
P2-int8-attn-parity-gate. Plus a unit test: q/k/v/o int8 vs f32 cosine on a synthetic attention input; assert the softmax distribution shift is within tolerance.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-ffn-parity-gate**: stage 2a must be green first (it's the lower-risk oracle for 2b).
- **OQ-14**: enumerates whether NVFP4 quantizes attention — decides validated-vs-our-risk. Hard rule: no kernel ships against an unresolved OQ that blocks it.

title: TEST — stage 2b parity gate (int8 attention vs f32 vs reference)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Stage 2b (int8 attention) is beyond the validated set, so its gate decides whether the `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` kill-switch is allowed ON by default or must default OFF. This is the differential proof + the measured-CER evidence the kill-switch is keyed on.

## WHAT TO DO
Run the L0–L5 ladder with `FOCR_INT8_ATTN=1` (stage 2a already on), comparing int8(2a+2b) vs f32 vs the reference oracle, on the golden + dense corpus:
- L3 logits within measured tolerance + argmax match; L4 exact where deterministic; L5 CER within ledgered budget; determinism byte-equal.
- **Decision logic:** if CER stays within the budget → record the measured impact in DISCREPANCIES.md and allow the switch; if it regresses → kill-switch defaults OFF, NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry (lever = int8 attention, measured before→after, disposition REVERT, do-not-retry condition).
Structured logging per doc: `{doc, stage='2b', max_logit_diff, argmax_match_rate, softmax_kl_shift, CER_delta_vs_2a, first_divergence_token}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The gate produces a measured CER delta for int8 attention vs stage 2a.
- Either: passes within budget (DISCREPANCIES.md entry, switch allowed) OR fails (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry, switch OFF) — both are acceptable outcomes; what's not acceptable is shipping it unmeasured.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-attn**: the thing under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: oracle + ladder machinery.

title: Stage 2c — int8 lm_head behind FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD kill-switch
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
`lm_head` (1280→129280, untied) is the THIRD int8 stage — **high-value, high-risk**. It's a huge per-token GEMV (the 129280-vocab logits) so int8 here is a real decode-bandwidth + footprint win (~330 MB bf16 → ~half). But it directly produces the logits the sampler argmaxes, so a small drift can flip the chosen token. It's beyond the validated set (OQ-14 — NVFP4 likely keeps the output layer in BF16), so it's gated behind its own `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` kill-switch and never assumed lossless.

## WHAT TO DO
Quantize `decoder.lm_head` (1280→129280) to int8 (per-output-channel symmetric + per-row dynamic activations), **only when `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` is enabled**. With the gate OFF, lm_head stays BF16 verbatim (the default). The int8 GEMV feeds the sampler: argmax/top-k only (no full softmax under greedy), with the no_repeat_ngram_size=35 blocklist over the 129280 vocab. K=1280 → S8S8 ≤ 20.6M, U8S8 ≤ 41.4M (within i32).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Default OFF, kill-switch ON only if the gate proves it** — the output layer is the most token-flip-sensitive tensor; this is the riskiest int8 stage.
- **Footprint motivation:** the < 2.9 GB int4 target may require int8 lm_head (NVFP4 keeping lm_head BF16 already lands at 2.93 GB) — but only behind CER gating.
- **argmax stability is the real metric**, not logit cosine — a tiny logit shift that doesn't change argmax is fine; one that flips the top token is a failure.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 129280-row per-channel scale vector is large; ensure it's stored inline correctly.
- Logit ties (bf16, 129K vocab) are exactly where int8 drift flips tokens — measure against the oracle's nondeterminism floor.
- A regression reverts JUST stage 2c.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- With `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` on, lm_head runs int8; off, BF16 verbatim.
- Passes the stage-2c parity gate (P2-int8-lmhead-parity-gate) or kill-switch defaults OFF with a NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry.
- i32 accumulator safe (K=1280).

## TESTS REQUIRED
P2-int8-lmhead-parity-gate. Plus a unit test: lm_head int8 GEMV vs f32, assert argmax-match rate on a synthetic batch of hidden states.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-ffn-parity-gate**: stage 2a green first.
- **OQ-14**: whether NVFP4 quantizes the output layer — decides validated-vs-our-risk.

title: TEST — stage 2c parity gate (int8 lm_head vs f32 vs reference, argmax-stability)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
lm_head int8 directly produces the sampled tokens, so its gate is argmax-centric: the question is not 'are the logits close' but 'does the argmax flip'. This is the measured-CER evidence the `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` kill-switch is keyed on.

## WHAT TO DO
Run the L3–L5 ladder with `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD=1` (stage 2a on, 2b per its own outcome), comparing int8 lm_head vs f32 vs reference:
- **L3:** logits within measured tolerance; **argmax match rate** vs f32 is the headline metric (must match where the reference is deterministic, accounting for the oracle's tie-level nondeterminism floor).
- **L4:** decoded token sequence exact where deterministic; report first-divergence position.
- **L5:** CER within ledgered budget, especially on dense numeric/table content (where a single flipped digit matters).
- **no_repeat_ngram_size=35 interaction:** confirm the int8 logits don't perturb the n-gram blocklist behavior vs f32.
- **Decision:** within budget → DISCREPANCIES.md entry + switch allowed; regression → NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + switch OFF.
Structured logging: `{doc, stage='2c', argmax_match_rate, flipped_token_positions, CER_delta, max_logit_diff}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Measured argmax-match rate + CER delta for int8 lm_head recorded.
- Either passes within budget (switch allowed, ledgered) or fails (switch OFF, NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) — measured either way.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-lmhead**: the thing under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: oracle + nondeterminism floor + ladder.

title: TEST — convert determinism + dequant determinism gate (source→.focrq→load)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
§5.4 mandates a deterministic round-trip story: same source → same `.focrq`; same `.focrq` → same dequantized weights → same forward. Determinism is a precondition for every parity gate (you can't compare against a moving target) and a non-negotiable goal (same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy).

## WHAT TO DO
A determinism gate with three layers:
1. **Convert determinism:** `focr convert --quant int8` twice from the same source → byte-identical `.focrq` + identical content hash. (The quantizer is a pure function of input bytes — no RNG, no calibration.) Also assert `source_sha256` is stable.
2. **Multi-arch logical equivalence:** `--arch generic` / `--arch aarch64-smmla` / `--arch x86-vnni` from the same source all **dequantize to the same logical weights** (the per-arch packing differs, the logical int8+scales are identical). CI verifies this.
3. **Dequant determinism:** loading the same `.focrq` twice yields byte-identical dequantized f32 weights; the int8 GEMM is bit-identical across SIMD paths (integer add is exact/associative) — assert SIMD path == scalar path bit-identically.
Structured logging: `{run, content_hash, source_sha256, arch, logical_weight_hash}` per run so a non-determinism is pinpointed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Pure-function quant** (data-free, no RNG) is what makes byte-identical convert possible — the test enforces it.
- **SIMD == scalar bit-identical** for int8 (no tolerance on that axis) — this is the e-process invariant E-VERIFY monitors.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- BTreeMap ordering / stable sort of the tensor_directory must be deterministic (no HashMap iteration order leaking into the output).
- The content hash must cover the full artifact (header + directory + payload), not just the payload.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Two converts → byte-identical `.focrq`.
- All `--arch` packings dequantize to the same logical weights.
- Same `.focrq` → identical dequant; SIMD == scalar bit-identical on the int8 GEMM.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the determinism gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-convert-int8-cli**: the converter under test.
- **P2-focrq-reader**: loads back for the dequant-determinism check.
- **P2-quantize-per-channel-i8**: the pure-function quant being asserted deterministic.

title: TEST — int8 end-to-end CER gate (within ledgered budget, NVFP4 bar)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The Phase 2 exit gate: int8 end-to-end OCR accuracy within a documented budget of f32 (target: within noise, matching NVFP4's 'identical to BF16' bar). This is the G1-correctness proof for the whole phase, measured as CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM on the golden + dense corpus. Perplexity under-predicts dense-text accuracy, so this gate uses CER on exact-token-sensitive content (numbers, tables, code, sub/superscripts).

## WHAT TO DO
An end-to-end conformance gate over the full golden corpus + a dense numeric/table/code stress corpus, with whatever stage flags are decided green (2a always; 2b/2c per their own gate outcomes):
- **CER** (character error rate) vs the reference, aggregated and per-doc.
- **TEDS / TEDS-S** for tables, **Formula-CDM** for LaTeX/math, **Reading-Order-Edit** for layout (the metrics the model is benchmarked on).
- **Decoded text exact** where the reference is deterministic (over the oracle-reproducible prefix).
- **Size check:** the int8 `.focrq` decoder set is Q8_0-class.
- Record the ledgered int8 CER budget + the realized CER in `docs/PERF_LEDGER.md` / `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` with the artifact-graph fields (claim_id, evidence_id, model source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, exact command + env, kill-switch state).
Structured logging per doc: `{doc, CER, TEDS, Formula_CDM, reading_order_edit, exact_match, stage_flags}` and an aggregate summary.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **CER on dense content, not perplexity** — the accuracy cliff for OCR is in dense numbers/tables/code; perplexity hides it.
- **NVFP4 'identical to BF16' is the bar** for int8 (int8 should be within noise; the real budget is for int4 in Phase 4).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Multi-page decode is cross-page DEPENDENT under R-SWA (OQ-13) — do NOT assert 'multi-page = sum of single-page parses'; the corpus and metrics must respect that.
- Establish the budget from the measured int8-vs-f32 delta, not an inherited number.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- int8 end-to-end CER within the ledgered budget (target: within noise of f32).
- TEDS/Formula-CDM/Reading-Order within budget; decoded text exact where deterministic.
- `.focrq` size ≈ Q8_0-class for the int8 set.
- Budget + realized CER ledgered with full artifact-graph provenance.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the phase exit gate (model-gated; skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights, prove native path with `/nonexistent` fallback when weights present).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-ffn-parity-gate**: stage 2a (the validated set) must be green; this gate aggregates whichever stages passed.
- **E-VERIFY**: supplies the reference oracle, the CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM harness, and the conformal release ratchet this gate feeds.

title: .focrq writer — serialize header + tensor directory + payload + MIT notice
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The converter (`focr convert`) must emit a `.focrq` artifact. The writer is the serialization half of the format; it takes the remapped + quantized tensors and produces the self-describing container that the runtime loads with zero shuffle. This is the on-disk deliverable of the 'transform into a custom quantized form' mandate.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the `.focrq` writer in `src/native_engine/weights.rs` (the converter side). Given a `BTreeMap`/list of (internal_name, tensor-with-dtype-and-scales), write:
1. `magic = b"FOCRQ\0"`.
2. `format_version: u32` (current).
3. `arch_target` (from `--arch`, default `Generic`).
4. `source_sha256: [u8;32]` (sha256 of the input safetensors blob).
5. `license_notice: utf8` — the MIT/Baidu notice string; the writer ERRORS if it is empty.
6. `model_config: json` — the frozen relevant config fields from E-PM1.
7. `tensor_directory` — one record per tensor with `{name, dtype, shape, packing, byte_offset, byte_len, scales_offset, scales_len, group_size?, tier?}`.
8. `payload` — raw tensor bytes (quantized weights + inline scales for QInt8PerChan; BF16 verbatim bytes for high-precision tensors).

Writer MUST be a **pure function of its inputs** (no RNG, no timestamps in the artifact body) so the same source → byte-identical `.focrq` (the determinism gate, P2-roundtrip-determinism-gate, asserts this). Emit a **content hash** of the output alongside `source_sha256`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Scales inline, adjacent to their weights** (per §5.2), so the reader indexes both by byte range without a second pass.
- **BF16 verbatim** for high-precision tensors — the writer must NOT narrow bf16→f16 (lossy, non-round-trippable). It copies the bf16 bytes straight through.
- **Deterministic ordering** of the tensor_directory (stable sort by internal name) so the output is reproducible.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- An empty/missing `license_notice` is a license-compliance bug → hard error, not a warning.
- Misaligned `byte_offset`/`scales_offset` will make the reader read garbage — assert alignment invariants the spec defines.
- Do NOT write any tensor the manifest census didn't expect (EXTRA tensors are a loud diff upstream in the converter).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Writer produces a `.focrq` that P2-focrq-reader reads back with byte-identical payload and matching directory.
- Same source bytes + same flags → byte-identical `.focrq` (asserted by P2-roundtrip-determinism-gate).
- `license_notice` non-empty enforced.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit tests (in `weights.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`): write a tiny synthetic model (a few int8 + a few bf16 tensors), assert the resulting bytes parse, the directory offsets are correct, and the magic/version/arch_target/license_notice are present. Cross-tested end-to-end by P2-focrq-roundtrip-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-focrq-format-spec**: the writer serializes exactly what the spec defines.
- **E-PM1**: provides the frozen config for `model_config` and the source provenance hash.

title: TEST — .focrq write→load→re-serialize byte-equality (bf16/f32 verbatim)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The format is only trustworthy if a `convert → load → re-serialize` cycle is byte-identical for the high-precision (BF16/F32) tensors. This is the §5.4 'Bit-exact round-trip for high-precision tensors' guarantee. Without it, the bf16-not-f16 decision is unproven and a silent narrowing could slip in.

## WHAT TO DO
Add an integration test (in `tests/` or `weights.rs` `#[cfg(test)]`) that:
1. Builds a synthetic model mix: several BF16 high-precision tensors (a fake vision/norm/router set) + several QInt8PerChan tensors with inline scales.
2. Writes it to a `.focrq` (P2-focrq-writer), reads it back (P2-focrq-reader), and re-serializes.
3. Asserts **byte equality** of the BF16/F32 verbatim regions across the write→load→re-serialize cycle (the bf16 bytes are unchanged).
4. Asserts the int8 weight bytes + inline scales survive the round-trip exactly.
5. Asserts a deliberate bf16→f16 narrowing would BREAK byte-equality (a guard test documenting why f16 is rejected — narrowing is lossy, clips at ±65504).
Structured logging: emit per-tensor `{name, dtype, bytes_in, bytes_out, byte_equal}` as a test artifact line so a failure names the exact tensor.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **BF16 verbatim** is the contract; the test makes it falsifiable. f16 narrowing is only ever an explicitly ledgered `DISCREPANCIES.md` divergence, never a silent storage choice — the guard test encodes that.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must cover both endianness of the bf16 storage and the alignment the spec mandates.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- write→load→re-serialize is byte-identical for all BF16/F32 tensors and for the int8 weights+scales.
- The f16-narrowing guard test fails byte-equality (proving the lossiness), documented as expected.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Structured per-tensor logging required.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-focrq-writer**, **P2-focrq-reader**: the round-trip exercises both halves.

title: .focrq reader — dependency-free byte-range index + manifest census on load
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The runtime loads weights through the `.focrq` reader in `src/native_engine/weights.rs`. It must be a dependency-free byte-range index into one mmap/blob (the franken_whisper loader pattern), and it performs the **WeightsManifest census** on load so wrong/stale weights are caught at load — not as garbage OCR output. This serves portability (no framework, no FFI) and honesty (loud failure over silent corruption).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the reader:
1. Read the whole `.focrq` into one `Vec<u8>` (or mmap). Validate `magic == b"FOCRQ\0"` — loud rejection on mismatch.
2. Parse `format_version`; REFUSE to load if it exceeds the binary's supported version (forward-incompatible).
3. Parse `arch_target`; if it mismatches the running arch, WARN and fall back to the `Generic` packing path (do not error).
4. Read `source_sha256`, `license_notice` (assert non-empty), `model_config`, and the `tensor_directory`.
5. Expose each tensor as a typed view over its byte range: int8 weights + their inline per-output-channel f32 scales; bf16 high-precision weights dequantized BF16→f32 at load.
6. **Manifest census**: validate every expected (name, shape) against a `WeightsManifest` covering all decoder + vision + projector + embedding tensors; MISSING / SHAPE-MISMATCH / EXTRA → a LOUD named diff, refuse to proceed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Census at load** (not at first garbage output) is the cheap insurance the plan mandates for stale/wrong weights.
- **BF16→f32 dequant at load** for high-precision tensors (the kernels consume f32 slices).
- **Version refusal + arch warn-and-fallback** keeps one binary able to load any same-or-older artifact and degrade gracefully on a wrong packing.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A `format_version` newer than the binary MUST be refused, not best-effort parsed.
- The reader must NOT depend on any heavy crate (it is the portable runtime path) — byte-range indexing only.
- Inline scales length must match the output-channel count of the quantized tensor; mismatch → loud error.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Reads any `.focrq` produced by P2-focrq-writer with byte-identical payload recovery.
- Census passes on a correct artifact; fails loudly (named diff) on a tampered/missing/extra/wrong-shape tensor.
- Rejects a higher `format_version`; warns + falls back on `arch_target` mismatch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit tests: round-trip a synthetic `.focrq` (write→read, payload byte-equal); census-failure tests (drop a tensor, mutate a shape, add an extra → assert each loud diff); version-refusal test (bump version → refused); arch-mismatch test (assert warning + generic fallback path chosen). Cross-tested by P2-focrq-roundtrip-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-focrq-format-spec**: the reader parses exactly what the spec defines (and what the writer wrote).

title: Tensor remap — HF dotted state_dict paths → internal .focrq layout
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The converter loads the HF safetensors (`ft-serialize::load_safetensors_from_bytes` decodes BF16→f32) into a `BTreeMap<String, DenseTensor>` keyed by HF dotted paths, then must remap to our internal layout AND tag each tensor with its quant policy (int8 / bf16-verbatim / high-precision). A wrong mapping silently quantizes a tensor that must stay high-precision (or vice versa) → corrupted OCR.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the remap table in the converter (`src/native_engine/weights.rs`), transcribed from plan §5.3. EXACT mapping (HF path → internal name → policy):

- `model.sam_model.*` (patch_embed, pos_embed, blocks.0..11, neck) → `vision.sam.*` — **bf16 verbatim**
- `model.vision_model.*` (embeddings, transformer.layers.0..23) → `vision.clip.*` — **bf16 verbatim**
- `model.projector.layers.{weight,bias}` → `vision.projector` — **bf16 verbatim**
- `model.image_newline`, `model.view_seperator` (sic) → `connector.image_newline`, `connector.view_sep` — **bf16 verbatim**
- `model.embed_tokens.weight` (129280×1280) → `decoder.embed` — **bf16 verbatim**
- `model.layers.{0..11}.self_attn.{q,k,v,o}_proj.weight` → `decoder.layer[i].attn.{q,k,v,o}` — **int8 (stage 2b, FOCR_INT8_ATTN)**
- `model.layers.0.mlp.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` (dense, intermediate 6848) → `decoder.layer[0].dense.{gate,up,down}` — **int8 (stage 2a)**
- `model.layers.{1..11}.mlp.gate.weight` (router 1280→64) → `decoder.layer[i].router` — **high precision (f32/bf16), NEVER quantized**
- `model.layers.{1..11}.mlp.experts.{0..63}.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` → `decoder.layer[i].expert[e].{...}` — **int8 (stage 2a)**
- `model.layers.{1..11}.mlp.shared_experts.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` → `decoder.layer[i].shared.{...}` — **int8 (stage 2a)**
- `model.layers.{0..11}.{input,post_attention}_layernorm.weight` → `decoder.layer[i].{norm1,norm2}` — **f32 RMSNorm, keep**
- `model.norm.weight` → `decoder.final_norm` — **f32 RMSNorm**
- `lm_head.weight` (1280→129280, untied) → `decoder.lm_head` — **int8 (gated, stage 2c, FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD)**

Validate against the WeightsManifest census (all 2710 tensors). MISSING / SHAPE-MISMATCH / EXTRA → loud named diff, refuse to proceed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **The policy column is the quant recipe (doctrine 2) made executable**: only decoder FFN/expert/dense/attention/lm_head linears are int8 candidates; vision + projector + embed + router + ALL norms are high precision. The router gate (`mlp.gate.weight`) is f32 always — it picks experts, low-bit routing flips token routing and wrecks OCR.
- **Single shard** (`model-00001-of-000001.safetensors`, 6.67 GB, 2710 tensors) — no multi-shard reassembly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Note the misspelled `view_seperator` (sic) in the HF state dict — match it exactly.
- `first_k_dense_replace=1` means layer 0 is DENSE (`mlp.{gate,up,down}_proj`, intermediate 6848); layers 1–11 are MoE (`mlp.experts.*` + `mlp.shared_experts.*` + `mlp.gate`). The remap must treat layer 0 and layers 1–11 differently.
- Do NOT quantize the router, embeddings, projector, or any norm — even though they are linears/params, the recipe forbids it.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every one of the 2710 source tensors maps to exactly one internal name with the correct policy; census passes; loud diff on any missing/extra/mis-shaped tensor.
- Router, embeddings, projector, vision, norms are tagged high-precision (never int8).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit test: feed the full expected name set (synthetic shapes) → assert the remap produces the expected internal names + policies; feed a corrupted set (missing layer, extra tensor, wrong shape) → assert loud census failure. Covered for real by P2-convert-int8-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: the WeightsManifest census (expected names/shapes) comes from the truth-pack token/shape census; the exact `lm_head` tied/untied + router shape are pinned there.
- **E-P1**: Phase 1 already loads these tensors for the f32 forward; the internal-name scheme must match what the f32 forward consumes.

title: Per-output-channel symmetric int8 weight quantizer (zero-point 0)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The core quant primitive: turn an f32 weight matrix into int8 weights + per-output-channel f32 scales. frankentorch already ships this exact op (`quantize_per_output_channel_i8(w, out, in_) -> (Vec<i8>, Vec<f32>)`); we reuse it as-is (the crown asset) and wrap it for the converter. This is what makes the decoder weights half-size (bf16→int8) and feeds the bit-exact int8 GEMM.

## WHAT TO DO
Wrap/consume frankentorch's `quantize_per_output_channel_i8` in the converter:
- **Scheme:** symmetric, **per-output-channel** (one scale per output row), **zero-point 0**, `scale = max|w_row| / 127`.
- Input: an f32 weight `[out, in]`. Output: `Vec<i8>` weights (`round(w / scale)`, clamped to [-127, 127]) + `Vec<f32>` of `out` scales, stored inline in the `.focrq` per the format spec.
- Apply ONLY to the int8-policy tensors from the remap (attention q/k/v/o, dense layer-0 gate/up/down, expert gate/up/down, shared-expert gate/up/down, lm_head when its gate is on).
- The quantizer is a **pure function of the input bytes** — no RNG, no calibration data (data-free PTQ, validated by NVFP4 being data-free and OCR-identical).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Per-output-channel symmetric, zero-point 0** mirrors ONNX `DynamicQuantizeLinear`/`MatMulInteger` and is the scheme frankentorch's bit-exact GEMM expects. Per-channel (not per-tensor) preserves accuracy where channel magnitudes differ.
- **Reuse, don't rebuild** — this op is in production in the frankensearch reranker; reimplementing it risks a numerics divergence for no gain.
- **Data-free** — no calibration set in v1; NVFP4 proved data-free PTQ is OCR-identical for this model class.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A row of all-zeros gives `scale = 0` → division guard (emit scale 0 → all-zero int8, handled by the dequant as 0).
- The scales are f32 and stored inline; they MUST be the same scales the GEMM uses post-accumulation (`dequant = i32_acc · scale_w · scale_a`).
- Round-to-nearest-even vs round-half-away must match frankentorch's implementation exactly so the converter is deterministic and matches the kernel's expectation.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- For every int8-policy tensor, produces `out` per-channel scales + `[out,in]` int8 weights, all within [-127,127].
- Deterministic: same input → identical (i8, scales) every run.
- Dequant of the int8+scales reconstructs the f32 within the per-channel quant error (round-trip error bounded by `scale/2` per element).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit tests: (a) quantize a known matrix, assert scales == max|row|/127 and int8 == round(w/scale); (b) all-zero row → scale 0, no NaN; (c) dequant-reconstruction max abs error ≤ scale/2 per element; (d) determinism (two runs byte-equal). Covered end-to-end by P2-convert-int8-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: confirms the exact weight shapes (q_proj/down_proj/expert dims) so the per-channel axis is correct.
- **P2-tensor-remap**: supplies which tensors are int8-policy.

title: focr convert --quant int8 (full converter pipeline → .focrq)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the user-facing converter: `focr convert --quant int8 [--arch ...]` turns the 6.67 GB bf16 HF safetensors into an int8 `.focrq`. It is the §5.1 pipeline end-to-end: download (out-of-band) → reference-parity load → quantize → (pre-pack stub in Phase 2, real packing in Phase 3) → write → round-trip gate. It serves the 'no network at inference time' goal (convert once, run offline) and the 'custom quantized form' mandate.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the `convert` subcommand orchestration:
1. Load the source safetensors via `ft-serialize::load_safetensors_from_bytes` (BF16→f32) into `BTreeMap<String, DenseTensor>`.
2. Validate against the WeightsManifest census (all 2710 tensors); loud diff on MISSING/SHAPE-MISMATCH/EXTRA.
3. Remap HF paths → internal layout + policy (P2-tensor-remap).
4. For int8-policy tensors: quantize per-output-channel symmetric (P2-quantize-per-channel-i8). For high-precision tensors: pass through as **BF16 verbatim**. Gate inclusion of attention/lm_head into the int8 set by the stage flags (default 2a = FFN/expert only; `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` adds 2b; `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` adds 2c).
5. Compute `source_sha256`; embed the MIT/Baidu `license_notice`; embed the frozen `model_config`.
6. Write the `.focrq` (P2-focrq-writer) for the chosen `--arch` (Phase 2: `Generic` packing; per-arch pre-pack is Phase 3).
7. Print the realized per-section footprint (vision bf16, embed bf16, lm_head bf16-or-int8, decoder int8 set) — a demonstrated number, not assumed.
8. Robot/JSON output of the conversion summary (sizes, hashes, stage flags active).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Staged by env flag** so the default convert produces the validated-set (FFN/expert) int8 and the risky tensors (attention, lm_head) are opt-in — each its own parity gate.
- **Prints realized footprint** — the plan forbids assumed sizes; the converter measures and reports.
- **`--arch {aarch64-smmla,x86-vnni,x86-amx,generic}`** flag wired now (Phase 2 only emits `generic` packing meaningfully; the real interleave is Phase 3) so the CLI surface is stable.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- This handles 6.67 GB — stream/load carefully; do not hold both the full f32 expansion and the int8 output if avoidable.
- The census MUST run before quantizing (catch a wrong checkpoint early).
- Determinism: the whole pipeline is a pure function of (source bytes, flags) → byte-identical `.focrq` (P2-roundtrip-determinism-gate asserts this).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr convert --quant int8` on the real checkpoint produces a loadable int8 `.focrq` of Q8_0-class size for the decoder set, vision/embed/router/norms bf16.
- Census passes; license_notice present; `source_sha256` matches the input.
- Stage flags correctly include/exclude attention (2b) and lm_head (2c).
- Prints realized per-section footprint + a JSON summary.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See P2-convert-int8-tests (the dedicated test bead). At minimum the CLI must: produce a valid artifact, refuse a mismatched checkpoint loudly, and respect the stage flags.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-focrq-writer**: serializes the result.
- **P2-tensor-remap**: name/policy mapping + census.
- **P2-quantize-per-channel-i8**: the int8 primitive.

title: TEST — convert int8 end-to-end: census, policy, footprint, determinism
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The converter is load-bearing: every downstream parity gate runs on its output. This test proves the converter does the recipe correctly (right tensors int8, right tensors bf16, census enforced) without needing the full 6.67 GB weights in CI.

## WHAT TO DO
Integration test (model-gated where it needs the real checkpoint; synthetic-mini where it can):
1. **Synthetic-mini path (always runs):** a tiny fake model with the same NAME structure (a couple decoder layers, an expert, a router, a norm, embed, projector, lm_head). Run `convert --quant int8`; assert: int8-policy tensors are QInt8PerChan with inline per-channel scales; router/embed/projector/norms are BF16 verbatim; census passes; loud failure if a tensor is dropped/extra/mis-shaped.
2. **Stage-flag tests:** default (2a) leaves attention + lm_head bf16; `FOCR_INT8_ATTN=1` makes q/k/v/o int8; `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD=1` makes lm_head int8. Assert the directory dtypes change accordingly.
3. **Footprint assertion:** the printed per-section footprint matches the emitted byte sizes.
4. **Determinism:** two converts of the same source → byte-identical `.focrq` + identical content hash.
5. **Model-gated full path (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights):** convert the real checkpoint, assert size ≈ Q8_0-class, census green; point any fallback at `/nonexistent` to prove the native path ran.
Structured logging: emit `{tensor, internal_name, dtype, bytes, scale_count}` per tensor and a final `{stage_flags, total_bytes, source_sha256, content_hash}` summary line.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Census enforcement proven (loud diff on tamper).
- Right tensors int8, right tensors bf16, per the recipe.
- Stage flags toggle the int8 set correctly.
- Determinism byte-equal.
- Model-gated test skips green without weights, runs the native path with them.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-convert-int8-cli**: the thing under test.

title: i32-accumulator overflow proof + worst-case-K unit test (K=6848, every arch)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
int8 GEMM accumulates in i32. If that accumulator overflows `i32::MAX = 2,147,483,647` the result is silently wrong (UB-free in Rust but numerically corrupt). AGENTS doctrine 6 makes this a **PROOF OBLIGATION, not an assumption**, and explicitly forbids inheriting frankensearch's `k≤1536` bound — THIS model's worst case is far larger. The proof gates every int8 GEMM kernel.

## WHAT TO DO
Prove, and encode as a unit test, that the i32 accumulator cannot overflow for any GEMM in the int8 decoder, on every kernel/arch path. The per-GEMM worst-case `|acc|` (transcribed from §5.4):
- **S8S8** (signed×signed, SMMLA/SDOT path): `|acc| ≤ K · 127²`.
- **U8S8** (unsigned activation [0,255] × signed weight [-127,127], VNNI path): `|acc| ≤ K · 255 · 127`.

The K values and resulting bounds:
- attention `q/k/v/o_proj`, expert/dense `gate`/`up`, `lm_head`: **K = 1280** → S8S8 ≤ 20.6M, U8S8 ≤ 41.4M.
- expert `down_proj`: **K = 896** → S8S8 ≤ 14.5M, U8S8 ≤ 29.0M.
- **dense layer-0 `down_proj`: K = 6848 (THE WORST CASE)** → S8S8 ≤ **110.4M**, U8S8 ≤ **221.7M**.

All fit i32 with ≥ 9× headroom (221.7M vs 2,147M). **Encode the unit test:** multiply worst-case *saturated* operands at **K = 6848** (S8S8: all weights ±127, all activations ±127; U8S8: all activations 255, all weights ±127) through every kernel/arch path (scalar, SDOT, SMMLA, VNNI/AVX2 where available) and assert the i32 result equals an **i64 reference** AND that no path silently saturates.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **K=6848 dense layer-0 down_proj is the documented worst case** (not the expert K=896 nor the K=1280 projections). The test must use 6848, not a smaller convenient K.
- **i64 reference comparison** is the oracle; the i32 path must equal it exactly (integer add is associative → SIMD == scalar bit-identically, no tolerance).
- **If a future fused/grouped/accumulating path approaches i32::MAX, switch THAT path to i64/blocked accumulation** — the proof is per-path, re-checked when a new accumulation shape is introduced.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The U8S8 path needs the documented +128 unsigned-correction handled BEFORE the bound is computed — the test must exercise the actual VNNI `_mm512_dpbusd_epi32` correction path, not a simplified model.
- Do NOT assume the expert-only K bounds; the dense layer-0 down_proj (K=6848) is what makes this model different from the reranker.
- The test must cross-compile/run its scalar fallback on every target (the bit-identical fallback is the floor).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A written proof (in the bead/docs) that max |acc| (221.7M) < i32::MAX (2147M) with ≥9× headroom for all GEMMs.
- The unit test passes on every arch path: i32 result == i64 reference at K=6848, no silent saturation.
- The test is part of the E-VERIFY e-process for 'int8 i32 accumulator never overflows'.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead (the overflow unit test). It must run the scalar + every available SIMD path and compare to i64.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: confirms the exact K dims (the 6848 intermediate of dense layer-0, the 896/1280 of experts/projections) so the worst-case K is the real one, not assumed.

title: Per-row dynamic int8 activation quant at forward (S8S8 / U8S8 per arch)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
int8 GEMM needs int8 *activations* too, quantized dynamically per forward (the input distribution is data-dependent). frankentorch's `linear_int8_dynamic_f32` already does per-row dynamic activation quant + i32-accumulate GEMM + dequant + bias (mirrors ONNX `DynamicQuantizeLinear` + `MatMulInteger`). We reuse it as the decode-GEMV path and ensure the activation-quant convention matches the per-arch weight packing.

## WHAT TO DO
Wire per-row dynamic int8 activation quant into the int8 decoder forward:
- For each activation row, compute `scale_a = max|x_row| / 127` (symmetric) for the **SMMLA/SDOT (signed×signed)** path; for the **VNNI/AMX (U8S8)** path, produce **uint8 activations with a zero-point** (since `vpdpbusd` wants unsigned activations × signed weights), using the documented +128 sign-flip correction.
- The converter (weight side) and the kernel (activation side) AGREE on the convention per arch (signed×signed for SMMLA/SDOT, U8S8 for VNNI/AMX).
- Post-accumulation: `dequant = i32_acc · scale_w[chan] · scale_a[row]`, then +bias, then activation (SiLU for FFN/experts, none for projections/lm_head). Requantize for the next chained layer where applicable.
- Reuse `linear_int8_dynamic_f32` as the baseline/oracle; do NOT hand-roll a wide-SIMD scalar loop (NE-INH-1: ~5× slower than LLVM autovectorization).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Per-row dynamic** (not static/calibrated) activation quant is data-free and matches the data-free weight recipe.
- **int8 activations, NOT int4** — int4 activations are too lossy for exact-token OCR (§6.3); activations stay int8 even when weights go int4 in Phase 4.
- **Convention per arch** — the scales applied post-accumulation are identical logically; only the signed-vs-U8S8 packing differs, handled by the +128 correction on the VNNI path.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The +128 unsigned-correction on VNNI must be exact or the U8S8 result drifts from the S8S8 result (they must dequantize to the same f32 within int8 error).
- The activation scale and weight scale order of application matters for the dequant — follow `i32_acc · scale_w · scale_a` exactly.
- A row of all-zero activations → `scale_a = 0` → all-zero int8 row, guard the division.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Per-row dynamic activation quant produces int8 (or U8S8) activations matching the per-arch convention.
- The S8S8 and U8S8 paths dequantize to the same f32 result within int8 rounding (cross-checked).
- Reuses `linear_int8_dynamic_f32` as the bit-exact baseline; no hand-wide-SIMD scalar loop.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit tests: (a) a known activation row → assert scale_a = max|x|/127 and int8 = round(x/scale_a); (b) S8S8 vs U8S8 dequant agree within int8 error on the same input; (c) the +128 VNNI correction yields the same dot as the signed path. Covered by the stage parity gates below.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-P1**: provides the f32 activations the int8 path quantizes (and the f32 forward to compare against).
- **P2-quantize-per-channel-i8**: the weight scales that combine with the activation scales at dequant.

title: Stage 2a — int8 the FFN / expert / dense-layer-0 GEMMs (the validated set)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the **first, validated, lowest-risk** int8 stage — the exact set GGUF and NVFP4 quantize and prove OCR-safe: the decoder FFN/expert GEMMs. These are also the **bulk of the 3B params and the decode hot path** (per-token expert GEMVs dominate decode), so this is the biggest correctness-safe footprint+bandwidth win.

## WHAT TO DO
Run int8 (weight-only, per-output-channel symmetric + per-row dynamic activations) on EXACTLY these decoder linears, keeping everything else as in Phase 1:
- Dense layer-0 MLP: `decoder.layer[0].dense.{gate,up,down}` (intermediate 6848; down_proj is the K=6848 worst case).
- MoE routed experts (layers 1–11): `decoder.layer[i].expert[e].{gate,up,down}` for e in 0..63 (1280↔896 SiLU-gated MLP).
- MoE shared experts (layers 1–11): `decoder.layer[i].shared.{gate,up,down}` (2 always-on per layer).
- Each expert/dense MLP: int8 `gate_proj` + `up_proj`, SiLU gate, int8 `down_proj`. i32-accumulate, scales applied post-accumulation.

Keep HIGH PRECISION (unchanged from Phase 1): router gate (f32), attention q/k/v/o (bf16/f32 — that's stage 2b), lm_head (bf16/f32 — that's stage 2c), embeddings, projector, vision, all norms.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **This set is validated by prior art** (NVFP4/GGUF) → lowest CER risk, do it first as the parity oracle for the riskier stages.
- **Router gate stays f32** — it selects the top-6 experts; quantizing it can flip routing and corrupt OCR.
- **MoE dispatch unchanged** — router → softmax → top-6 greedy → norm_topk_prob → gather 6 routed + 2 shared experts; only the expert GEMM weights become int8.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The K=6848 dense layer-0 down_proj is the overflow worst case — its kernel path must be covered by the P2-i32-overflow-proof test.
- Expert weights should be packed cache-contiguous for the per-token active experts (Phase 3 does the arch pre-pack; Phase 2 uses generic packing).
- A drift here reverts JUST stage 2a (source reverted, NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The FFN/expert/dense GEMMs run int8; everything else high precision.
- Passes the stage-2a parity gate (P2-int8-ffn-parity-gate): logits within measured int8 tolerance, decoded text exact where deterministic, CER within ledgered budget.
- i32 accumulator proven safe (P2-i32-overflow-proof).

## TESTS REQUIRED
P2-int8-ffn-parity-gate (the differential gate). Plus per-kernel unit tests: an expert MLP int8 vs f32 cosine on a synthetic input; the dense layer-0 MLP int8 vs f32.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-convert-int8-cli**: produces the int8 `.focrq` with the FFN/expert set quantized.
- **P2-dynamic-act-quant**: the activation-quant + i32 GEMM path.
- **P2-i32-overflow-proof**: must be green for the K=6848 dense down_proj.
- **E-P1**: the f32 forward this stage is measured against.

title: TEST — stage 2a differential parity gate (FFN/expert int8 vs f32 vs reference)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
No int8 stage lands without its own parity gate (the staged-de-risk rule). Stage 2a (FFN/expert) is the validated set, so its gate is the strictest: int8 here must be within noise of f32 (NVFP4's 'identical to BF16' bar). This is the differential int8-vs-f32-vs-reference proof for stage 2a.

## WHAT TO DO
A differential parity gate over the golden corpus comparing THREE engines: int8(2a), f32 (Phase 1), and the E-VERIFY reference oracle. Run the L0–L5 ladder for the int8 path:
- **L3 logits:** int8 logits within the **measured** int8 tolerance (NOT the inherited frankensearch 0.055 — measure THIS model's int8-vs-f32 max_diff and put it in the ledger), and **argmax must match** f32 where the reference is deterministic.
- **L4 token:** decoded token sequence **exact** vs f32 (and vs reference over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically, per the oracle nondeterminism floor).
- **L5 end-to-end:** CER / TEDS / Formula-CDM within the ledgered budget on the dense numeric/table corpus.
- **Determinism gate:** same input twice → byte-identical output.
Structured logging per doc: `{doc, max_logit_diff, argmax_match_rate, first_divergence_token, CER, stage_flags}`. Record the measured int8 max_diff in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` (with kill-switch = revert stage 2a).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Measured tolerance, not inherited** — the int8-vs-f32 budget for THIS model is a new number, derived here.
- **Three-way differential** (int8/f32/reference) so we know whether an int8 divergence is inside the oracle's own bf16 noise (then it's not a bug).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must use the oracle's pre-measured nondeterminism floor (from E-VERIFY) to define 'exact where deterministic'.
- Dense numbers/tables/code are the failure surface — the corpus must stress them.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L3 within measured tolerance + argmax match; L4 exact where deterministic; L5 CER within ledgered budget; determinism byte-equal.
- Measured int8 max_diff recorded in the ledger; gate is part of the E-VERIFY conformal release ratchet.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the gate (the test bead) for stage 2a.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **P2-int8-ffn-experts**: the thing under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: supplies the reference oracle, the nondeterminism floor, and the L0–L5 / conformal-ratchet machinery.

title: Phase 1 — fp32 reference-parity forward (Vision + Ingest + Decoder)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 1 builds the FIRST end-to-end forward of Baidu Unlimited-OCR in pure Rust, **in f32 only**, whose ONLY job is to be **bit/cosine-faithful to the bf16 reference oracle**. No quantization yet, no SIMD micro-kernels yet — those are Phase 2/3/4. Correctness outranks speed always (G1 > G2): a faster kernel that drifts OCR output is reverted. This epic is the foundation every later speed phase stands on; if f32 parity is wrong, every int8/int4 number built atop it is meaningless.

This epic spans TWO halves, both nesting under THIS epic bead (E-P1):
- **VISION + INGEST half (owned here):** tokenizer, image preprocess/tiling, bicubic pos-embed interp, SAM-ViT-B vision tower, CLIP-L vision tower, feature fusion + linear projector, connector (structural tokens + masked-scatter fusion).
- **DECODER half (owned by the sibling decoder agent):** embed lookup, RMSNorm, RoPE, R-SWA attention, MoE dispatch, dense layer-0 MLP, final norm, lm_head, sampler, postprocess.

The model (one paragraph): DeepEncoder vision tower (SAM-ViT-B → 16× conv token-compressor → CLIP-L/14) → single linear projector (2048→1280) → DeepSeek-V2 MoE decoder (12 layers, hidden 1280, 10 MHA heads, use_mla=false, 64 routed + 2 shared experts, top-6, vocab 129280) with R-SWA attention. bf16, 6.67 GB single safetensors shard, MIT license.

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver an f32 forward that passes the L0–L5 parity ladder (§8.2) against the frozen reference oracle from E-PM1. The VISION+INGEST tasks produce, in order: tokenized prompt, preprocessed/tiled image tensors, SAM features, CLIP features, fused 2048→1280 projected vision embeds, and the connector-assembled embedding stream (vision embeds masked-scattered into the text-embed sequence with image_newline/view_seperator inserted) — i.e. exactly the `inputs_embeds` the decoder consumes.

Spec-first discipline (`/porting-to-rust`): FIRST the spec `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` is extracted from the pinned reference source (E-PM1 truth pack), THEN each kernel is implemented FROM THE SPEC, not by line-translating Python. No kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN]/OQ.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **f32 everywhere in Phase 1.** Vision tower stays high-precision in ALL phases (the validated quant recipe — both prior-art quants keep vision unquantized; quantizing vision wrecks OCR). So the vision half built here is essentially the FINAL vision tower, just in f32 storage; later phases keep it bf16/f32.
- **Reuse frankentorch f32 building blocks** (conv2d_forward_f32, sdpa_forward_f32, layer_norm_forward_f32, gelu/silu, matmul_tensor_contiguous_f32, linear_tensor_f32) rather than hand-rolling. BUILD only the genuine gaps: pure-Rust BPE tokenizer, image decode/resize/pad/normalize/tile front end, f32 bicubic pos-embed interpolation, windowed attention mask, quick_gelu, masked-scatter fusion.
- **Mat { rows, cols, data: Vec<f32> } as the in-engine currency** with a thin frankentorch facade (nn.rs).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **A wrong tokenizer corrupts every downstream gate** — tokenizer parity (OQ-16) is an L0/L4 prerequisite gated before any decoder parity work.
- **A wrong SAM window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme silently corrupts vision features** (surfaces only as a fuzzy L2 cosine) — OQ-15 must be resolved from `deepencoder.py` first.
- **The exact SAM⊕CLIP concat order producing the 2048-dim projector input is [OPEN] (OQ-6)** — must be read line-by-line from `deepencoder.py` (variants `low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu` / `hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu`).
- Establish the oracle's OWN nondeterminism floor first; L4 'exact' is defined only over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically; L3 tolerance derived from measured oracle variance, NOT the imported frankensearch 0.055.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L0 preprocessing exact (gray pad 127, ratio selection, [-1,1] normalize, tile geometry).
- L1 per-op cosine ≥ 0.9999 (f32) vs oracle activations for every vision kernel.
- L2 per-vision-stage output (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector) cosine ≥ 0.9999; max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Tokenizer token-id-exact vs LlamaTokenizerFast on the conformance corpus.
- WeightsManifest census passes; no [OPEN] blocks any implemented kernel.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Every task carries unit tests (happy/edge/error) AND a per-stage differential parity test against the oracle fixture, with structured JSON-line logging (stage name, shapes, cosine, max-abs-diff, pass/fail).
- A dedicated vision parity-ladder harness bead (P1-vision-parity-harness) wires L0–L2 for the whole vision+ingest path.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on E-PM1 (truth pack: pinned source, census, resolved OQs, oracle fixtures) and E-P0 (scaffold: crate, native_engine module skeleton, Mat currency, nn facade, weights loader).

title: Pure-Rust byte-level BPE tokenizer over tokenizer.json (encode/decode)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The tokenizer is the FIRST stage of ingest and an L0/L4 PARITY PREREQUISITE: a single wrong token id corrupts every downstream gate (logits, decoded tokens, end-to-end OCR). It must reproduce HF `LlamaTokenizerFast` byte-level BPE exactly over the model's `tokenizer.json`. This is a known GAP (frankentorch ships no tokenizer); we BUILD a pure-Rust HF-JSON BPE — no SentencePiece, no FFI, preserving the no-FFI single-binary story (G3).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/tokenizer/mod.rs` (+`special.rs`) — a dependency-free reader of the model's `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB) implementing:
- **Vocab 129280** (the DeepSeek vocab), `LlamaTokenizerFast`, byte-level BPE.
- **encode(text) -> Vec<u32>** and **decode(ids) -> String**, both byte-level-exact.
- The HF byte-level pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, merge ranks from `tokenizer.json`.
- **Special tokens**: bos=0, eos=1, pad=2; `<image>` image_token; layout specials `<|ref|>` `<|/ref|>` `<|det|>` `<|/det|>`; the DeepSeek glyph specials (e.g. the end-of-sentence glyph `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`); ~512 special tokens total. `add_special_token=false` per processor config (do NOT auto-prepend specials unless the reference does).
- Read all rules from `tokenizer.json`; do NOT hardcode merges in source.

Transcribed facts: tokenizer = `LlamaTokenizerFast`, byte-level BPE, `tokenizer.json` 9.98 MB, vocab 129280, bos/eos/pad = 0/1/2, image_token=`<image>`, ignore_id=-100, `add_special_token=false`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Drive everything from `tokenizer.json`** (merges, vocab, pre-tokenizer, special-tokens map) so a tokenizer change is a data change, not a code change. AGENTS doctrine: revise in place, no tech debt.
- **Byte-level (not char-level)** so CJK/math/code/Unicode round-trip exactly — these are the corpus classes Unlimited-OCR is strong on.
- **Resolve OQ-16 before relying on it**: the pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token and DeepSeek-glyph handling are confirmed against `LlamaTokenizerFast` on a round-trip corpus (the OQ-16 deliverable, owned by E-PM1).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- DeepSeek glyph specials use unusual full-width bar/triangle glyphs — exact byte sequences matter; a mismatched glyph desyncs the whole stream.
- Special tokens must NOT be BPE-split — they are atomic; the special-token splitter runs before BPE.
- Byte-fallback path for bytes with no merge must match HF exactly.
- This is a tokenizer **mismatch** risk flagged HIGH in the plan: a wrong id corrupts every downstream gate.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Token-id-EXACT vs `LlamaTokenizerFast` on the OQ-16 conformance corpus (diverse Unicode, CJK, math, code, DeepSeek specials, `<image>`/`<|ref|>`/`<|det|>`).
- Round-trip `decode(encode(text)) == text` for all non-lossy inputs in the corpus.
- All special tokens map to their exact reference ids.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: encode/decode happy path; empty string; lone special token; mixed text+special; multi-byte boundary; byte-fallback case.
- See P1-tokenizer-tests for the full frozen-golden conformance corpus (token-id-exact vs reference), with structured logging of first-divergence position per sample.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-16 (E-PM1 must resolve byte-level-BPE encode/decode parity vs LlamaTokenizerFast — pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, specials) and E-PM1 (pinned `tokenizer.json` + frozen reference id sequences).

title: Tokenizer conformance test bead (token-id-exact golden corpus)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Tokenizer conformance is the gate that makes every downstream parity gate meaningful. It must be token-id-exact vs `LlamaTokenizerFast`; a wrong id corrupts L0/L4 and everything after.

## WHAT TO DO
Build a dedicated tokenizer conformance harness (`tests/tokenizer_conformance.rs`) that round-trips `encode`→`decode` against frozen reference id sequences (from E-PM1/OQ-16) over a corpus spanning: diverse Unicode, CJK, math/LaTeX, code, the DeepSeek glyph specials, and `<image>`/`<|ref|>`/`<|/ref|>`/`<|det|>`/`<|/det|>`. Freeze the reference id sequences as golden fixtures under `tests/fixtures/`. Emit structured JSON-line logs: per sample {name, byte_len, expected_ids_len, got_ids_len, first_divergence_index, pass}.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Golden fixtures are committed reference id sequences (provenance recorded: tokenizer.json sha256, generator command). Differential vs the reference is the gold standard; XFAIL (never SKIP) any intentional divergence with a DISC-NNN entry.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must cover the special-token-inside-text case and the empty/whitespace edge cases.
- CI must run without the 6.67 GB weights (tokenizer needs only tokenizer.json) — keep this test always-on, not model-gated.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- 100% token-id-exact across the corpus; any divergence is a logged failure (or an explicitly ledgered XFAIL).
- Round-trip identity holds for all non-lossy samples.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead. It must produce a machine-readable pass/fail report with first-divergence diagnostics.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-tokenizer (the thing under test) and OQ-16 (the frozen reference id sequences + parity rules).

title: Feature fusion (SAM⊕CLIP → 2048) + linear projector (2048→1280)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The SAM features and CLIP features are concatenated to **2048 dims** and a **single linear projector** maps **2048 → 1280** to produce vision embeds in the decoder's hidden space. The EXACT concat order producing the 2048-dim input is [OPEN] (OQ-6) — get it wrong and the projector mixes the wrong channels. frankentorch supplies `linear_tensor_f32`.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/native_engine/vision_bridge.rs`:
