- Form the **2048-dim** fused feature from SAM-1024 ⊕ CLIP-1024 (the exact concat order/path per OQ-6 — variants `low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu` / `hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu` in `deepencoder.py`).
- Apply the **single linear projector** 2048 → 1280: weights `model.projector.layers.{weight,bias}` (internal `vision.projector`), `projector_type=linear`, `input_dim=2048`, `n_embed=1280`.
Transcribed config: `model_type=mlp_projector`, `projector_type=linear`, `input_dim=2048`, `n_embed=1280`. SAM compressed = 256 tokens × 1024; CLIP = its token set × 1024.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Resolve OQ-6 FIRST** (line-level from `deepencoder.py`): SAM-1024 ⊕ CLIP-1024 vs interleaved, and whether any GELU/split happens in the hybrid path. No kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN].
- Keep projector f32/high-precision (validated recipe keeps the projector unquantized; both prior-art quants keep it).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The token-alignment between SAM (256) and CLIP token sets must be consistent before concat — if CLIP runs over a different token count, the fusion path (OQ-6) defines how they align.
- Concat axis (feature dim → 2048) vs token dim is load-bearing.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs oracle post-projector vision embeds (tokens × 1280).
- The fused intermediate matches the oracle's 2048-dim feature (cosine ≥ 0.9999) where the oracle dumps it.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: concat shape (→2048); projector output shape (→1280); projector vs a known SAM⊕CLIP input.
- See P1-vision-bridge-tests for the L2 differential gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-6 (exact SAM⊕CLIP concat order/path), E-PM1 (oracle 2048-feature + post-projector fixtures + projector weights), P1-sam-neck-compression + P1-clip-tower (the two feature sources).

title: Vision bridge + projector L2 parity test bead
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The concat order (OQ-6) is a silent-corruption point; prove the fused feature and the projected embeds match the oracle.

## WHAT TO DO
Differential harness (`tests/vision_bridge_parity.rs`): compare our 2048-dim fused feature and our 1280-dim projected vision embeds against the oracle fixtures. Structured JSON-line logging: {stage(fused|projected), shape, cosine, max_abs_diff, pass}. Model-gated.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 ≥ 0.9999 for both the fused 2048 feature and the projected 1280 embeds.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-vision-bridge-projector (under test) and E-PM1 (oracle fused + projected fixtures).

title: Connector: image_newline/view_seperator insertion + masked-scatter vision fusion
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The connector assembles the final embedding stream the decoder consumes: it inserts the two learnable structural tokens (`image_newline`, `view_seperator`) into the vision-token stream and **masked-scatters** the projected vision embeds into the text-embed sequence at the `<image>` placeholder positions. This is true end-to-end fusion — NO cross-attention resampler / Q-former. It is the last vision+ingest stage before the decoder.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/native_engine/connector.rs`:
- Build the vision-token stream with structural tokens: per OQ-18, a 1024 view yields the placeholder structure `(16+1)·16 + 1 = 273` = 256 image-feature tokens + 1 `image_newline` per row × 16 rows + 1 `view_seperator`. Insert the learnable params `model.image_newline` and `model.view_seperator` (internal `connector.image_newline`, `connector.view_sep`) at exactly the reference positions.
- **masked-scatter fusion** (BUILD, small kernel): replicate `inputs_embeds.masked_scatter_(images_seq_mask, vision_embeds)` — scatter the (structural-token-interleaved) vision embeds into the prompt's `inputs_embeds` at the positions flagged by the `<image>`-derived `images_seq_mask`.
Transcribed facts: structural tokens are two learnable nn.Parameter `model.image_newline` and `model.view_seperator` (sic); fusion via `masked_scatter_`; no cross-attention. Census 273 placeholder slots at 1024 (Base).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Token-class layout from the OQ-18 census is the source of truth** — every class (image-feature, image_newline, view_seperator) placed exactly, because the R-SWA reference block `m` and all KV/buffer sizing depend on the exact assembled length.
- Keep the structural params and embeds f32/high-precision.
- For Gundam mode, assemble the global-view + per-crop token streams in the reference order (global_view_pos='head').

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The `images_seq_mask` construction from the `<image>` placeholder ids must match the reference exactly — an off-by-one in mask positions misplaces every vision embed.
- The number of `<image>` placeholder ids in the prompt must equal the number of vision embeds being scattered (a length-mismatch is a hard failure to assert loudly).
- The structural-token positions (one image_newline per row, one view_seperator at the end) must be exact.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs the oracle post-connector `inputs_embeds` (the full assembled embedding stream the decoder consumes).
- The assembled length and per-class token counts match the OQ-18 census exactly.
- masked-scatter places every vision embed at the correct position (positional exactness, not just cosine).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: structural-token insertion (273-slot Base layout); masked-scatter into a synthetic prompt; length-mismatch error path; Gundam global+crops ordering.
- See P1-connector-tests for the L2 differential gate vs the oracle inputs_embeds.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-18 (token-class census + placeholder structure), E-PM1 (oracle post-connector inputs_embeds fixture + structural param weights), P1-vision-bridge-projector (provides the projected vision embeds), P1-tokenizer (provides the prompt ids + `<image>` placeholder positions).

title: Connector + masked-scatter L2 parity test bead (assembled inputs_embeds)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The connector is the hand-off boundary to the decoder; if the assembled `inputs_embeds` is wrong (positions or values), the decoder parity is meaningless. This bead proves the boundary.

## WHAT TO DO
Differential harness (`tests/connector_parity.rs`): compare our assembled `inputs_embeds` against the oracle post-connector fixture — BOTH the per-class token counts/positions (exact) and the embed values (cosine ≥ 0.9999). Include a metamorphic check: the structural-token count derived from the census matches the assembled stream. Structured JSON-line logging: {assembled_len, n_image_feat, n_newline, n_viewsep, cosine, max_abs_diff, positions_match, pass}. Model-gated.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Assembled length + per-class counts EXACT vs census; embed values cosine ≥ 0.9999; vision-embed positions exact.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-connector-fusion (under test) and E-PM1 (oracle post-connector inputs_embeds fixture + OQ-18 census).

title: Vision+ingest L0–L2 parity-ladder harness (end-to-end vision path gate)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Individual stage tests catch per-kernel drift; this harness proves the WHOLE vision+ingest pipeline (decode → preprocess/tile → tokenize → SAM → bridge/compress → CLIP → concat/project → connector) is parity-faithful end-to-end as one chained path, against the oracle's per-stage dumps. It is the vision half's exit gate for E-P1 and the hand-off contract to the decoder half.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/vision_parity_ladder.rs` driving the full vision+ingest forward on the golden image corpus and asserting at every checkpoint: L0 preprocessing EXACT; L1 per-op cosine ≥ 0.9999; L2 per-stage (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector) cosine ≥ 0.9999 with max-abs-diff ledgered. Establish the oracle's nondeterminism floor first (run the oracle twice / two thread counts) and define cosine thresholds relative to that floor. Emit one structured NDJSON line per checkpoint per image: {image, stage, shape, cosine, max_abs_diff, oracle_floor, pass}.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Chained (not isolated) comparison catches integration bugs (e.g. a token-alignment error between SAM and CLIP that each isolated test misses).
- Model-gated e2e: skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran (point fallbacks at /nonexistent). Tokenizer + preprocess sub-gates stay always-on (no weights needed).
- Fixtures carry PROVENANCE (torch==2.10.0, transformers==4.57.1, git ref, command).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A green isolated stage with a red chained stage means a layout/alignment bug at a boundary — the harness must localize WHICH boundary.
- Cosine thresholds must be ledgered relative to the measured oracle floor, NOT a hand-guessed epsilon or the imported frankensearch 0.055.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L0 exact; L1/L2 all green (cosine ≥ 0.9999) across the golden corpus; every max-abs-diff ledgered.
- The post-connector inputs_embeds (the decoder hand-off) matches the oracle within L2 tolerance.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the integration test bead (the vision+ingest parity ladder).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on every vision+ingest test bead (P1-preprocess-tests, P1-tokenizer-tests, P1-sam-tests, P1-clip-tests, P1-bicubic-posembed-tests, P1-vision-bridge-tests, P1-connector-tests) since it chains all of them, and E-PM1 (oracle per-stage fixtures + nondeterminism floor).

title: Extract decoder+output spec from modeling_deepseekv2.py / modeling_unlimitedocr.py into EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 1 is `/porting-to-rust` spec-first: *extract spec -> implement from spec -> never translate line-by-line* (plan §8.6, §10 Phase 1). Before any decoder/output Rust is written, the verbatim semantics of the DeepSeek-V2 MoE decoder + R-SWA + sampler + postprocess must be transcribed from the **pinned** reference source (Phase -1 truth pack) into the decoder/output portion of `docs/EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` (THE SPEC). This serves the over-arching goal of fidelity-to-this-one-model: a wrong masking rule or routing tie-break corrupts every downstream parity gate and is far cheaper to catch on paper than in a fuzzy L2 cosine.

## WHAT TO DO
Produce the **decoder + output** sections of `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md`, extracted verbatim (with quoted source lines) from the pinned `modeling_deepseekv2.py` (esp. `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`, `DeepseekV2MoE`, `DeepseekV2MLP`, `DeepseekV2RMSNorm`, `DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding`), `modeling_unlimitedocr.py` (`infer`/`infer_multi`, `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`, placeholder/structural-token assembly), and `conversation.py` (prompt taxonomy). Transcribe the VERIFIED config facts so the implementer never reopens the plan:
- hidden_size 1280; num_hidden_layers 12; num_attention_heads 10; num_key_value_heads 10 (**no GQA**); v_head_dim 128 (10·128 = 1280); `use_mla=false` (MLA/latent attention DISABLED -> plain MHA); kv_lora_rank/q_lora_rank null; qk_nope_head_dim/qk_rope_head_dim 0 (Q/K head dim **not** directly given by config -> read from q_proj weight shape, almost certainly 128, **OQ-4**).
- intermediate_size 6848 (dense MLP, layer 0); moe_intermediate_size 896 (per expert); n_routed_experts 64; n_shared_experts 2; num_experts_per_tok 6 (top-6); topk_method `greedy`; norm_topk_prob true; n_group/topk_group 1/1 (no group routing); first_k_dense_replace 1 -> **layer 0 dense, layers 1-11 MoE**.
- max_position_embeddings 32768; vocab_size 129280; RMSNorm (LLM); SiLU (MLP/experts); RoPE `DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding` (theta default 10000 assumed, YARN/NTK present but likely inactive -> **OQ-5**); lm_head tied false; bos/eos/pad 0/1/2.
- R-SWA: `config.sliding_window = 128`, `config.sliding_window_size = 128`; ring-buffer KV width W=128; `slot = prefill_len + ring_pos`, `ring_pos = (ring_pos+1) % W`; reference tokens = frozen global KV (never evicted, excluded from state transitions).
- Sampler: greedy/low-temp argmax; strip trailing EOS `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`; `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with sliding `ngram_window` **128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image)** (OQ-18); `infer(...)` signature `(tokenizer, prompt='', image_file='', output_path='', base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True, test_compress=False, save_results=False, eval_mode=False, max_length=32768, tps_interval=0, no_repeat_ngram_size=0, ngram_window=0, temperature=0.0)`.
- Output: structured Markdown, HTML tables, LaTeX, `<|ref|>...<|/ref|><|det|>...<|/det|>` tags parsed via regex, bbox 0-999 (`/999 -> ×W/H`), `<page>` separators.

Resolve, with a quoted line-backed answer recorded in the truth-pack OQ register, every kernel-blocking OQ in this half: OQ-1 (reference set `m`: visual-only vs visual+prompt; exact mask boundary prompt vs generated), OQ-2 (is attention uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers incl. layer 0; any full-attention layer), OQ-3 (warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during first 128 decode steps), OQ-4 (Q/K head dim), OQ-5 (rope_theta + YARN/NTK active?), OQ-8 (prompt-mode taxonomy + whether bboxes emitted in all modes), OQ-13 (does the multi-page reference block span ALL pages -> page N depends on 1..N-1), OQ-18 (token census per mode + ngram_window per mode).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Spec-first, not line-by-line translation** (plan §8.6): implementing from the Python directly reproduces its bugs and its CUDA-isms; we want the *math contract*. 
- **No kernel ships against an unresolved OQ** (AGENTS.md hard rule): this doc is the gate that converts each OPEN into a design assumption with a citation.
- This is the load-bearing input for every other P1 decoder/output bead; all of them depend on it.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The model changed on 2026-06-24 (post-cutoff); read ONLY the Phase -1 pinned/hashed source, never live HF.
- The reference `infer()` path is CUDA-oriented (OQ-17 owned by E-PM1); the spec is for *semantics*, independent of device.
- Do NOT assert multi-page = sum of single-page parses (OQ-13): R-SWA likely makes pages cross-dependent; record the actual span.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` decoder+output sections complete, each load-bearing claim carrying a `claim_id -> source-line` citation into the pinned source.
- OQ-1,2,3,4,5,8,13,18 each have a quoted, line-backed answer recorded.
- Reviewed against the config census; no contradictions with the VERIFIED config table.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A `docs/truth-pack` consistency check (or CI lint) asserting every decoder/output claim_id resolves to a real pinned-source line; covered by the truth-pack CI guard owned by E-PM1, extended here for the decoder claims.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the pinned/hashed source + census + OQ register live there) and on the named OQs (OQ-1..5,8,13,18) because their answers ARE the spec content. Every other P1 decoder/output bead depends on THIS bead.

title: Token embedding lookup (bf16-preserving, 129280×1280 index_select)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The decoder's first op turns input token ids into hidden vectors via a lookup into `model.embed_tokens.weight` (129280×1280). It is on the f32 reference-parity critical path: the connector masked-scatters vision embeds INTO this embedding stream (vision agent), so any drift here poisons the whole decode. `embed_tokens` is in the **keep-high-precision** set (NEVER quantized; plan §2/§5.3) — the over-arching goal is fidelity to this one model, and the embeddings are cheap to keep exact.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `decoder.embed` in `src/native_engine/decoder.rs` (or a small helper in `nn.rs`): given a `&[u32]` of token ids and the loaded `embed_tokens` weight (stored **BF16 verbatim** on disk per §5.2/§5.3, dequantized BF16->f32 at load), produce a `Mat { rows = seq_len, cols = 1280, data: Vec<f32> }` by row gather (`index_select` pattern). Preserve f32 throughout — do NOT materialize f64 (plan §4.3 'token embedding lookup (f32-preserving): preserve f32, don't materialize f64'). Vocab 129280, hidden 1280, bos/eos/pad 0/1/2.

The BF16->f32 dequant must be **byte-exact** to the reference's bf16->f32 widening (zero-extend mantissa, no rounding): bf16 is (1-8-7); widening to f32 places the 7 mantissa bits in the high mantissa and zero-fills — a pure bit operation, lossless. Reuse `ft-serialize`'s BF16 decode (`load_safetensors_from_bytes` already decodes BF16->f32) as the oracle for the widening.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **BF16-preserving, not F16**: the checkpoint is bf16 (1-8-7); narrowing to f16 (1-5-10) is lossy and not a byte-identical round-trip (plan §5.2). High-precision storage is BF16 verbatim; any f16 narrowing would be a ledgered DISCREPANCIES.md divergence, never the default.
- **Keep embeddings unquantized** (plan §2 doctrine 2): both prior-art quants keep embeddings high precision; quantizing them risks the OCR cliff for marginal size.
- **No autograd / no f64**: inference-only, plain `&[f32]` slices (plan §3.1).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Out-of-range id (>= 129280) must error loudly, not index OOB.
- The connector (vision agent) will overwrite image-placeholder rows via masked-scatter; this op must produce the full padded embedding stream first so those positions exist to be scattered into.
- Determinism: pure gather, no RNG.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 per-op parity vs the oracle's `embed_tokens(ids)` activation: cosine >= 0.9999 and max-abs-diff 0 for the BF16->f32 widening (it is exact) on a fixed id sequence.
- Round-trip: the widened f32 of each looked-up row equals `ft-serialize`'s BF16 decode of that row, bit-for-bit.
- No f64 materialization (code review + no f64 buffers in the path).

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-token-embed-tests`. Unit: lookup of a small id list returns expected rows; OOB id errors; BF16->f32 widening matches `ft-serialize` for adversarial bf16 patterns (±inf, denormals, signed zero). Integration: feed the golden-corpus prompt ids and assert L1 cosine vs the oracle embedding dump, with structured per-token max-diff logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (pinned config gives vocab/hidden + the embed_tokens shape census) and **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (confirms embed is unquantized + the exact widening rule). The connector/masked-scatter consumer is owned by the vision half (E-P1 connector task); this op only produces the embedding stream.

title: Tests: token embedding lookup parity + BF16->f32 widening exactness
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The embedding lookup is the decoder's entry point; a silent BF16->f32 widening bug or a wrong gather row would corrupt every downstream parity gate. This bead provides the unit + integration tests with the comprehensive structured logging the project mandates (AGENTS.md Testing Policy).

## WHAT TO DO
Add to `tests/` (and `#[cfg(test)]` units in the embed module):
1. **Unit — gather correctness**: build a tiny synthetic `embed_tokens` (e.g. 8×4), look up `[0,3,7,3]`, assert exact rows; assert OOB id (8) returns a typed error, no panic/OOB.
2. **Unit — BF16->f32 widening exactness**: for adversarial bf16 bit patterns (signed zero, +inf, -inf, NaN, smallest denormal, 1.0, -2.5), assert our widening == `ft-serialize` BF16 decode, bit-for-bit (compare raw u32 of the f32).
3. **Unit — no-f64**: assert (via code structure / a type-level check) the path holds only `f32`.
4. **Integration (model-gated)**: load the real `embed_tokens` (skip-with-SUCCESS if 6.67 GB weights absent, per AGENTS.md model-gated policy; point fallback at `/nonexistent` to prove the native path ran); feed the golden-corpus prompt ids; assert L1 cosine >= 0.9999 and max-abs-diff 0 vs the oracle embedding `.npy` dump from `gen_reference_fixtures.py`.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
Emit one NDJSON line per checked token: `{test, token_id, cosine, max_abs_diff, exact}` so a failure pinpoints the token and value. Aggregate `{n_tokens, min_cosine, max_abs_diff}` at the end.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All units pass; widening is bit-exact for every adversarial pattern.
- Integration: min cosine >= 0.9999, max_abs_diff == 0 over the golden ids (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-token-embed** (the thing under test).

title: RMSNorm kernel (decoder input/post-attention norms + final norm, f32)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Every decoder layer applies RMSNorm twice (input_layernorm before attention, post_attention_layernorm before MLP/MoE) and the model applies a final `model.norm` before lm_head. Norms are in the **keep-f32** set (plan §2 doctrine 2: ALL norms stay high precision — quantizing them drifts the OCR output). This op is the glue between the big GEMMs; getting it bit-faithful to the reference is required for f32 parity.

## WHAT TO DO
Wire `decoder.layer[i].norm1/norm2` and `decoder.final_norm` to frankentorch's existing `rms_norm_forward_f32` (plan §4.3: RMSNorm EXISTS, reuse). RMSNorm over hidden_size 1280: `y = x / sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps) * weight`. Confirm `rms_norm_eps` from the pinned config (record in spec) and the **reduction precision**: DeepseekV2RMSNorm computes the variance in f32 even when activations are bf16 — our f32 path matches by construction, but the `mean(x^2)` accumulation must use f64 (or Kahan/pairwise) accumulation where the reference does, to avoid drift at 1280-wide reduction (plan §6.2: scalar loops with f64 accumulation where precision matters; LLVM autovectorizes, do NOT hand-SIMD).

Weights `model.layers.{0..11}.{input,post_attention}_layernorm.weight` and `model.norm.weight` are stored BF16 verbatim, dequantized to f32 at load (§5.3, kept f32).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Reuse the frankentorch kernel** (§4.3) — it already does row-band `std::thread::scope` parallelism gated by element count, and autovectorizes the elementwise math. Do NOT hand-roll wide SIMD (NE-INH-1: 5× slower than LLVM autovectorization).
- **Keep norms f32** (doctrine 2) — non-negotiable; the eps and the reduction order are the only correctness levers.
- **f64 accumulation for `mean(x^2)`** where the reference does, to keep L1 cosine >= 0.9999.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Verify `eps` matches the reference exactly (a wrong eps shifts every norm output — fuzzy L2 cosine, no hard crash).
- Variance computed on the **pre-weight** vector; weight is applied after the reciprocal-sqrt scale — confirm ordering vs DeepseekV2RMSNorm in the spec.
- Determinism: same input -> same output (no atomics, fixed reduction order).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 per-op parity vs the oracle per-norm activation dump: cosine >= 0.9999, max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Matches `rms_norm_forward_f32` semantics with the confirmed eps; determinism gate green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-rmsnorm-tests`. Unit: known small vector vs hand-computed RMSNorm with the real eps; zero-vector / large-magnitude stability; weight=1 reduces to pure normalization. Integration: per-layer norm outputs vs oracle dumps with structured per-position max-diff logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (eps + hidden dim from pinned config) and **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (confirms norm ordering + reduction precision + that norms stay f32). Consumed by `P1-decoder-loop`, `P1-rswa-attn`, `P1-moe`, `P1-dense-mlp0`, `P1-lmhead`.

title: Tests: RMSNorm correctness + per-layer parity + determinism
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
RMSNorm runs 25 times per token (2×12 layers + final); a wrong eps or reduction order silently shifts every hidden state. These tests pin it.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit**: RMSNorm of `[1,2,3,4]` (and a 1280-wide vector) vs a hand/f64 reference using the real eps; assert max-abs-diff < 1e-6.
2. **Unit — stability**: all-zeros (eps prevents NaN), very large values (no overflow), weight all-ones (pure normalization).
3. **Unit — determinism**: same input twice -> bit-identical output.
4. **Integration (model-gated)**: for each of input/post-attn norms of layers 0..11 and the final norm, compare against the oracle `.npy` dumps; assert cosine >= 0.9999.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per norm site: `{layer, site, cosine, max_abs_diff}`; final aggregate `{min_cosine, max_abs_diff_overall, eps_used}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
All units pass; integration min cosine >= 0.9999 across all 25 norm sites (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-rmsnorm**.

title: RoPE kernel (theta from OQ-5, head_dim 128, applied to Q/K)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Rotary position embedding is applied to the Q and K projections in every decoder attention layer before the score computation. It is a BUILD item (plan §4.3: 'RoPE (theta 10000) ... BUILD ... confirm theta first'). Correct RoPE is required for f32 parity — a wrong theta or a wrong rotate-half convention silently corrupts attention everywhere (fuzzy L2 cosine, no crash). Position indexing also interacts with R-SWA: reference tokens carry their absolute positions; generated tokens continue the sequence.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement RoPE in `src/native_engine/decoder.rs` / `rswa.rs` over Q,K with head_dim = 128 (10 heads, hidden 1280; head dim confirmed 128 per **OQ-4** from q_proj shape). Use `DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding` semantics from the spec:
- `rope_theta` value: DeepseekV2 default 10000 is assumed but MUST be confirmed from the pinned `configuration_deepseek_v2.py` / config `language_config` (**OQ-5**). Use the confirmed value; do not hardcode 10000 until OQ-5 is recorded.
- Whether YARN/NTK scaling is active at 32K-native (OQ-5): the classes are present but likely inactive; if inactive, plain RoPE; if active, implement the exact scaling factor recorded in the spec.
- Match the reference's rotate-half / interleave convention exactly (DeepseekV2 uses the `rotate_half` (split-in-half), NOT GPT-NeoX interleaved — confirm in spec): `q_rot = q*cos + rotate_half(q)*sin`.
- `inv_freq = 1 / theta^(2i/head_dim)` for i in 0..head_dim/2; `cos`/`sin` indexed by absolute position. Precompute the cos/sin tables up to max_position_embeddings = 32768 once.

Fuse opportunity (Phase 3, not now): RMSNorm->RoPE in one pass (§6.10); v1 keeps them separate for parity clarity.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Confirm theta before building** (§4.3, AGENTS.md no-kernel-against-unresolved-OQ): the entire attention parity depends on it.
- **Autovectorize the elementwise rotation** (§6.2): tight scalar loops, LLVM vectorizes; do NOT hand-SIMD (NE-INH-1).
- **Precompute cos/sin tables** to 32768 to avoid per-step transcendental cost; deterministic, no per-token trig.
- **f64 for the inv_freq table** construction where precision matters, materialized to f32 (the table is computed once).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Wrong convention (interleave vs split-half) is the classic silent RoPE bug — the unit test must lock the convention against the oracle.
- Position indexing across the R-SWA reference block (m) + generated window must be absolute and monotonic (OQ-1/OQ-13 affect WHICH positions reference tokens hold, not the rotation math); coordinate with `P1-rswa-attn`.
- If YARN is unexpectedly active (OQ-5), the per-dim frequency scaling and the attention-scaling `mscale` must be replicated exactly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 per-op parity: roped Q/K vs the oracle's post-RoPE Q/K dumps, cosine >= 0.9999, max-abs-diff ledgered.
- theta + scaling decision matches the recorded OQ-5 answer; convention matches the oracle.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-rope-tests`. Unit: position 0 is identity (cos=1,sin=0); a known (theta, pos, head_dim) triple vs a hand/oracle computation; rotate-half convention lock. Integration: per-layer post-RoPE Q/K vs oracle dumps.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** + **P1-spec-decoder-extract**, and explicitly on **OQ-4** (head dim 128) and **OQ-5** (theta + YARN/NTK active?). Consumed by **P1-rswa-attn** (RoPE is applied inside the attention block).

title: Tests: RoPE convention lock + theta + per-layer Q/K parity
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
RoPE bugs are silent (wrong convention or theta degrades attention without crashing). These tests lock the convention, the theta, and per-layer parity.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — identity at pos 0**: cos=1, sin=0 -> roped Q == Q at position 0.
2. **Unit — known triple**: for the confirmed theta and head_dim 128, a hand-computed rotation at position p matches our output (locks `inv_freq` formula).
3. **Unit — convention lock**: assert split-half (rotate_half) vs interleaved by feeding a vector where the two conventions differ and matching the oracle.
4. **Unit — YARN/NTK gate**: if OQ-5 says inactive, assert no scaling is applied; if active, assert the recorded scaling factor.
5. **Integration (model-gated)**: per decoder layer, post-RoPE Q/K vs oracle `.npy`; cosine >= 0.9999.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON: `{layer, head, cosine, max_abs_diff, theta_used, yarn_active}`; aggregate min cosine.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration min cosine >= 0.9999 across all 12 layers (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-rope**.

title: R-SWA decode attention: fixed ring buffer + frozen reference block + online softmax (the centerpiece)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
R-SWA (Reference Sliding Window Attention) is the model's ONE architectural novelty and the project's centerpiece kernel (`src/native_engine/rswa.rs`). It replaces EVERY decoder attention layer (all 12). Each generated token attends to: (a) ALL reference tokens (visual + prompt prefix), kept as a **frozen, never-evicted global KV** (excluded from state transitions, avoiding 'progressive blurring'), PLUS (b) only the previous n=128 generated tokens via a ring-buffer KV cache. This bounds generated-token KV at `L·(m + min(n,T)) <= L·(m+n)` — CONSTANT in output length, vs MHA's `L·(m+T)`. That bounded-KV long-output decode is literally what 'Unlimited' means and is the model's pitch (plan §2.1, §2.3.3). Getting this f32-faithful is the gate for every decode parity result.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement R-SWA decode attention in `rswa.rs`, plain MHA (use_mla=false): 10 heads, head_dim 128, separate `q/k/v/o_proj` dense GEMMs (f32 in v1). Per the spec (`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`):
- **Pre-allocate, per layer, one fixed buffer sized for worst-case `m`** (~= 32768 - 128): a reference-KV block of length `m` (visual+prompt, read-only for the whole generation, never grows, never evicted) + a **128-wide ring buffer** for generated-token K,V. No realloc; trivially memory-safe.
- **Decode step t**: compute Q for the new token (post-RoPE via `P1-rope`); `scores = (Q·reference-K) ∪ (Q·ring-K)`; softmax over the union (use the online/FlashAttention-style softmax of `P1-rswa-online-softmax`); output = weighted sum of `(reference-V ∪ ring-V)`; write the new K,V into the ring at `slot = prefill_len + (ring_pos)`, `ring_pos = (ring_pos+1) % 128` AFTER warm-up.
- **Prefill**: build the reference KV block (visual + prompt). Whether the reference set `m` includes the running prompt or strictly the visual tokens is **OQ-1**; the warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during the first 128 decode steps is **OQ-3**; whether attention is uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers (incl. layer 0; first_k_dense_replace affects only the MLP) with no full-attention layer is **OQ-2**. Build the mask ONLY after these are recorded in the spec.
- **Scaling**: `softmax(QK^T / sqrt(head_dim))` (head_dim 128 -> 1/sqrt(128)); confirm the exact scale (and any YARN mscale) from the spec.
- **Multi-page span (OQ-13)**: in a multi-page pass, whether the reference block spans ALL pages (page N attends to 1..N-1) changes the reference-block construction and the metamorphic gate — honor the recorded answer.

KV memory is plain MHA per-layer (no MLA decompression). The matmul tiling is built around the known max KV length `m + 128` (cache-resident for small m, streamed for large m), but the f32 v1 just needs correctness; the tiled/online perf form is Phase 3.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Constant MEMORY, NOT constant per-token COMPUTE** (plan §6.4, AGENTS.md doctrine 7): the reference block `m` grows ~256+ tokens per page and is attended EVERY step, so per-token attention is `O(m+128)` and compute scales linearly with page count up to the 32K cap. Do NOT market decode as 'constant-time per token across document sizes'.
- **Pre-allocate worst-case once, reuse across pages** (§6.12): zero per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop; trivially memory-safe (no realloc).
- **f32 attention in v1 for parity** (§6.8): int8 attention is a Phase-3 perf lever behind `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` + measured-CER gate, NOT a v1 default — scores feed softmax/exp which amplify error.
- **Reference tokens excluded from state transitions** (§2.3.3): they are read-only KV, never overwritten by the ring — preserving visual-token fidelity.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **No kernel ships against an unresolved OQ** (AGENTS.md): OQ-1,2,3,13 gate the mask; do not finalize masking until recorded.
- Ring-buffer wrap arithmetic (`slot = prefill_len + ring_pos`, `% 128`) and the warm-up (cache fills to `prefill_len + W` before overwriting) are the highest-bug-density code — unit-test the wrap exhaustively.
- **INVARIANT (gauntlet e-process, §8.5)**: KV cache never exceeds `L·(m+128)`; assert in a debug check.
- Reference block read-only: a write into the reference region is a correctness bug — assert it cannot happen.
- Multi-page is cross-page DEPENDENT (OQ-13): do not assume page independence.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 per-layer parity: per-decoder-layer attention output (and hidden state) vs oracle dumps, cosine >= 0.9999 in f32, max-abs-diff ledgered.
- L4: with this attention in the full decode loop, decoded tokens exact vs the oracle (over the prefix the oracle reproduces deterministically, per §8.2 nondeterminism floor).
- Ring-buffer invariant: KV never exceeds `L·(m+128)`; reference block never written; warm-up + wrap semantics match OQ-3.
- Determinism: same input -> identical attention output.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-rswa-attn-tests` (dedicated, heavyweight). Unit: ring-wrap arithmetic over >128 steps; reference-block-read-only assertion; warm-up boundary at step 127/128/129; single-head attention vs a dense reference for small m; union-softmax over reference ∪ ring equals a non-ring full attention when T <= 128. Integration: full decode over the golden corpus, per-layer cosine + token-exactness with structured logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** + **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (mask semantics), **P1-rope** (RoPE applied to Q/K inside attention), and explicitly **OQ-1** (reference set `m`), **OQ-2** (uniform R-SWA across layers), **OQ-3** (warm-up/ring mask), **OQ-4** (head dim 128), **OQ-13** (multi-page span). Uses **P1-rswa-online-softmax** for the softmax (in f32 v1 a simple two-pass softmax is acceptable; the online form is the perf path but its numerics must already match here).

title: Online (FlashAttention-style) softmax over the R-SWA reference block
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The R-SWA score set per decode step is `Q·[reference-K (length m, up to ~32K) ∪ ring-K (128)]`. Materializing the full `1×m` score row per head (×10 heads ×12 layers, m up to 32K) is cache-hostile (plan §6.8). Online softmax (the FlashAttention recurrence) streams the reference-K/V block in cache-sized tiles maintaining running `(m_max, l_sum, acc)` per head — never materializing the full score vector, single pass. This is what makes 'constant-memory, compute-grows-with-pages' R-SWA actually cache-efficient at 40-page scale, and it composes with the int8 `Q·K^T` GEMM later (the reference block is large enough to be a real GEMM).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the online-softmax accumulator used by `P1-rswa-attn`: for each head, iterate the reference-K/V block in cache-sized tiles, maintaining `(running_max, running_denom, running_acc_vector)`; on each tile, rescale the accumulator by `exp(prev_max - new_max)`, add `exp(scores - new_max) · V_tile`, update denom; finalize `acc / denom`. Handle the 128-wide ring window as a tail tile. The numerics MUST equal a straightforward two-pass softmax to f32 tolerance (the online recurrence is algebraically identical; the test proves it).

In f32 v1, a simple two-pass softmax is *acceptable for correctness*, but implement the online form so its numerics are locked NOW (it becomes the Phase-3 perf path); the parity gate is identical for both.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Online softmax over two-pass** (§6.8): bounded working set (one K/V tile + accumulator), single pass, GEMM-composable. Independent of the OQ-1..3/13 mask answers (those choose WHICH tokens are in the reference set; the recurrence is the same).
- **f64 accumulation for the denom** where the reference accumulates in higher precision, to keep L3 logit drift within the measured tolerance.
- **Polynomial vectorized exp is a Phase-3 lever** (§6.11) behind a measured A/B gate — v1 uses a faithful `exp` for parity.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The rescale-on-new-max step is the bug-prone part; an incorrect rescale silently shifts the softmax — the equivalence test catches it.
- Numerical stability: subtract running max before exp (standard); never exp raw scores.
- Determinism: fixed tile order -> fixed reduction order.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Online softmax output == two-pass softmax output to < 1e-5 max-abs-diff on random and adversarial (large-magnitude, all-equal, single-spike) score rows of length up to 32K.
- Composes with `P1-rswa-attn` to hit L2 cosine >= 0.9999.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-rswa-attn-tests` (covers online vs two-pass equivalence). Dedicated unit: equivalence over score-row lengths {1, 127, 128, 129, 4096, 32640}, tile sizes {64, 256, 1024}, with adversarial distributions; structured logging of `{len, tile, max_abs_diff}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (the attention scale + softmax precision) and **OQ-1** (which tokens form the reference block being summed). Used by **P1-rswa-attn**.

title: Tests: R-SWA ring buffer, warm-up, reference-block invariants, online-softmax equivalence, per-layer + token parity
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
R-SWA is the centerpiece and the highest-bug-density kernel (ring wrap, warm-up boundary, frozen reference block, online softmax). It needs the heaviest test battery, with the structured logging the project mandates, and it carries load-bearing INVARIANTS the gauntlet monitors as e-processes (§8.5).

## WHAT TO DO
**Unit — ring buffer:**
1. Drive >128+prefill decode steps; assert `slot = prefill_len + ring_pos`, `ring_pos` wraps `% 128`, and the 128 most-recent generated K,V are exactly those retained.
2. Warm-up boundary: at steps 127/128/129, assert the mask transitions from 'accumulate' to 'ring-overwrite' per the OQ-3 semantics recorded in spec.
3. Reference-block read-only: assert no write ever lands in the reference region (a debug guard / poisoned canary).
4. KV-cap INVARIANT: assert total live KV per layer never exceeds `L·(m+128)` across a long run.

**Unit — attention math:**
5. Single-head, small m: R-SWA output vs a dense hand-computed reference attention (when T <= 128, union == full attention over reference ∪ all-generated).
6. Online vs two-pass softmax equivalence (from `P1-rswa-online-softmax`) over lengths {1,127,128,129,4096,32640}.
7. RoPE-position consistency: reference tokens hold absolute positions; generated tokens continue the sequence (per OQ-1/OQ-13).

**Integration (model-gated):**
8. Full decode over the golden corpus: per-decoder-layer attention-output cosine >= 0.9999 vs oracle dumps (L2); decoded tokens exact vs oracle over the deterministic prefix (L4, per the §8.2 nondeterminism floor).
9. Determinism: same image+args twice -> identical attention outputs and tokens.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per step/layer/head: `{step, layer, head, ring_pos, slot, kv_len, cosine, max_abs_diff, first_divergence_pos}`; aggregate `{min_cosine, max_kv_len, token_exact_prefix_len}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
All units pass; ring/warm-up/read-only/KV-cap invariants hold; online==two-pass; integration L2 min cosine >= 0.9999 and L4 token-exact over the deterministic prefix (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-rswa-attn** and **P1-rswa-online-softmax** (the things under test).

title: MoE router: gate GEMV (1280->64, f32) -> softmax -> top-6 greedy -> norm_topk_prob
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Layers 1-11 are MoE. Per token, the router picks which 6 of 64 experts run; a wrong selection drifts the whole layer (and the OCR output) catastrophically. The router gate is in the **keep-f32, NEVER-quantized** set (plan §2 doctrine 2, §6.7: 'quantizing the gate drifts expert selection -> cliff'; NVFP4 keeps it f32). This op is on the f32-parity critical path and is also the gateway to the single biggest decode cost center (expert FFNs).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the router in `src/native_engine/moe.rs`: for each token's hidden vector (1280):
1. **Gate GEMV** `1280 -> 64` in **f32** (`model.layers.{i}.mlp.gate.weight`, kept high precision) -> 64 logits.
2. **softmax** over the 64 logits (DeepseekV2: `topk_method='greedy'`, `n_group/topk_group = 1/1` so no group routing).
3. **top-6 greedy** selection: select the 6 highest-probability experts. Use partial selection (`select_nth_unstable` / `select_nth`, NOT a full sort — §6.7) but the **tie-break MUST match the reference exactly** (confirm from spec: DeepseekV2 greedy uses `torch.topk` which is stable by index on ties — record the exact tie rule).
4. **norm_topk_prob = true**: renormalize the 6 selected gate weights to sum to 1 (divide by their sum), confirm the epsilon/ordering vs the reference.

Output: the 6 expert ids + their normalized routing weights, per token.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Router stays f32, never quantized** (doctrine 2, §6.7): expert selection is discrete; a quantized gate flips which experts run -> cliff. Non-negotiable.
- **Partial selection not full sort** (§6.7): top-6 of 64 via `select_nth` is O(64), cheaper than sorting; but correctness (exact expert set + tie-break) outranks speed.
- **norm_topk_prob renormalization** (config true): the 6 weights are renormalized to sum 1 — a wrong renorm scales the expert contributions.
- **Greedy, n_group=1**: no group routing path needed (simpler than full DeepSeek-V2).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Tie-break determinism**: if two gate logits tie, the selected expert set must match the reference's index-order tie-break exactly — a flipped expert is an L4 token divergence. This is the subtlest correctness point; lock it in the test.
- softmax over 64 is tiny; f64 accumulation is cheap and removes any reduction-order ambiguity.
- Layer 0 has NO router (it is dense, first_k_dense_replace=1) — the router runs only for layers 1-11.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 parity: router gate logits vs oracle dump, cosine >= 0.9999; **the selected top-6 expert id set and their normalized weights are EXACT vs the oracle** for every token on the golden corpus.
- Determinism: same hidden -> same expert set + weights.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-moe-router-tests`. Unit: known gate logits -> expected top-6 + normalized weights; tie-break case matches the reference rule; norm_topk_prob sums to 1. Integration: per-token expert-id-set exactness vs oracle router dump, with NDJSON logging of any mismatch (`{token, expected_set, got_set}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (n_routed_experts 64, top-6, norm_topk_prob, gate shape from census) and **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (exact greedy tie-break + renorm). Feeds **P1-moe-experts**.

title: Tests: MoE router top-6 exactness + tie-break + norm_topk_prob
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
A flipped expert selection is a hard L4 token divergence; the tie-break and renorm are the subtle correctness points. These tests pin exact expert-set selection.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — selection**: known 64-logit vector -> expected top-6 expert ids and softmax probs; renormalized weights sum to 1.
2. **Unit — tie-break**: construct exact-tie logits; assert the selected set matches the recorded reference tie rule (index order).
3. **Unit — norm_topk_prob**: assert the 6 selected weights are divided by their sum (compare to hand computation).
4. **Unit — determinism**: same logits twice -> same set.
5. **Integration (model-gated)**: for every token over the golden corpus and every MoE layer (1-11), assert the selected expert-id set + normalized weights are EXACT vs the oracle router dump; log any mismatch.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON: `{layer, token, expected_set, got_set, weight_max_abs_diff}`; aggregate `{n_tokens, n_mismatches}` (must be 0).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration mismatch count == 0 over the golden corpus (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-moe-router**.

title: MoE expert dispatch: 64 routed + 2 shared experts (1280<->896 SiLU-gated MLP), gather + combine
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The MoE expert FFNs are the bulk of the 3B params and the dominant decode cost (plan §6.1 HIGHEST priority, §6.7). In f32 v1 we need them CORRECT first; the int8/int4 + token-grouping perf forms come in Phases 2-3. Per MoE layer (1-11): 6 routed experts (selected by `P1-moe-router`) + 2 always-on shared experts, each a 1280->896 SiLU-gated (SwiGLU) MLP, combined by the routing weights.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement expert dispatch in `src/native_engine/moe.rs` (f32 v1):
- **Each expert** is a SwiGLU MLP: `down_proj( silu(gate_proj(x)) * up_proj(x) )` with `gate_proj`/`up_proj`: 1280->896, `down_proj`: 896->1280. Tensors `model.layers.{i}.mlp.experts.{0..63}.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` (int8/int4 later; f32 in v1 via `linear_tensor_f32`).
- **Routed experts**: for the 6 expert ids from the router, run the expert MLP on the token's hidden vector, scale each output by its normalized routing weight, sum.
- **Shared experts**: `model.layers.{i}.mlp.shared_experts.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` — 2 always-on experts (1280->896 SwiGLU), run on every token, ADDED to the routed sum. Confirm from spec whether shared experts are weighted by a fixed factor or simply added (DeepseekV2 adds shared-expert output directly; record).
- **Combine**: `output = sum(weight_e * expert_e(x) for e in top6) + sum(shared_expert_k(x) for k in 2)`.
- SiLU via `P1-silu`. Use `silu(gate) * up` (NOT `gate * silu(up)` — confirm the SwiGLU gate ordering from spec; DeepseekV2 applies act_fn to gate_proj).

Leave a clean seam for Phase-3 token-grouping (§6.7: prefill sorts tokens by expert into dense GEMMs) and fused SwiGLU; v1 is the scalar/per-token correct form.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Correct before fast** (G1>G2): f32 per-token dispatch first; the grouped-GEMM (§6.7) and int8/int4 (Phase 2/4) are layered on after parity.
- **Shared experts always on** (config n_shared_experts=2): they are not routed, run every token — a frequent omission bug; the test asserts their contribution.
- **SwiGLU gate ordering matters**: `silu(gate_proj(x)) * up_proj(x)` per DeepseekV2 — swapping gate/up silently corrupts output.
- **Cache-contiguous expert packing** is a Phase-2 concern; v1 just indexes the experts.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Omitting shared experts, or weighting them wrong, is a silent L2 drift.
- The down_proj K=896 (i32-overflow proof obligation is a Phase-2 concern; f32 v1 has none).
- moe_intermediate_size 896 is the per-expert hidden; the dense layer-0 uses 6848 (different op, `P1-dense-mlp0`).
- Determinism: fixed expert order in the sum (sum routed in id order, then add shared) to keep reduction order stable.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 per-layer parity: MoE-layer output (post-residual) vs oracle dumps for layers 1-11, cosine >= 0.9999 in f32, max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Shared-expert contribution present and correct (ablation test: removing it diverges from oracle).
- Determinism green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-moe-experts-tests`. Unit: single expert SwiGLU vs hand computation; combine of 6 routed weights + 2 shared matches a reference combine; gate/up ordering lock. Integration: per-MoE-layer output cosine vs oracle, with NDJSON per-layer logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (expert count/dims), **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (SwiGLU ordering + shared-expert combine rule), **P1-moe-router** (provides the 6 expert ids + weights), and **P1-silu** (the activation).

title: SiLU activation (LLM/expert MLPs) wired to frankentorch
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The decoder MLPs (dense layer-0 and all MoE experts) are SiLU-gated (SwiGLU). SiLU `x*sigmoid(x)` is the LLM-family activation (plan §2.7: SiLU for LLM MLP/experts; quick_gelu/GELU are vision-family, kept separate). It is a small but exact-required op on the decode path.

## WHAT TO DO
Wire SiLU to frankentorch's existing `silu` tensor op (plan §4.3: GELU/SiLU EXISTS, reuse). `silu(x) = x * sigmoid(x) = x / (1 + exp(-x))`. Keep vision-family and text-family activations separate (§2.7). In v1, faithful scalar/autovectorized `exp`; a vectorized polynomial `sigmoid` is a Phase-3 lever (§6.11) behind a measured A/B — NOT v1.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Reuse the frankentorch kernel** (§4.3); do NOT hand-SIMD (NE-INH-1).
- **Faithful exp in v1** for parity; vectorized poly-exp is Phase-3 behind a gate (§6.11).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Numerical form: `x/(1+exp(-x))` is stable for large +x; for large -x it underflows cleanly to 0. Match the reference's formulation.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 parity: SiLU output vs oracle, cosine >= 0.9999; matches `silu` semantics.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: `silu(0)=0`, `silu(large +)~x`, `silu(large -)~0`, vs a hand/f64 reference over a sweep; determinism. Covered alongside `P1-moe-experts-tests` and `P1-dense-mlp0-tests`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (confirms SiLU is the LLM activation). Used by **P1-moe-experts** and **P1-dense-mlp0**.

title: Tests: MoE expert SwiGLU correctness + shared-expert presence + per-layer parity
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Expert dispatch is the dominant decode op; common bugs are omitted shared experts and swapped gate/up. These tests pin per-layer parity.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — single expert**: SwiGLU `down(silu(gate(x))*up(x))` vs hand/f64 computation on a small synthetic expert.
2. **Unit — gate/up ordering lock**: feed inputs where `silu(gate)*up != gate*silu(up)` and match the recorded reference ordering.
3. **Unit — combine**: 6 routed (with weights) + 2 shared summed matches a reference combine; ablation removing shared experts diverges.
4. **Unit — determinism**: fixed expert sum order -> identical output.
5. **Integration (model-gated)**: per MoE layer (1-11) output (post-residual) cosine >= 0.9999 vs oracle dumps.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON: `{layer, cosine, max_abs_diff, shared_contrib_norm}`; aggregate min cosine.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration min cosine >= 0.9999 across layers 1-11 (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-moe-experts**.

title: Image decode → resize → pad(gray 127) → normalize([-1,1]) → CHW front end
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The preprocess front end turns a raw document image into the exact normalized tensor the SAM patch-embed consumes. It is the L0 parity gate — and L0 must be EXACT (any pixel mismatch changes OCR output). This is a known GAP (frankentorch has no image I/O); we BUILD it with the `image` / `fast_image_resize` crates.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/preprocess/{mod.rs,resize.rs,normalize.rs,pad.rs}`:
- **decode** (image crate) PNG/JPG/etc → RGB. v1 is IMAGE-ONLY (PDF is out of scope, rasterized out-of-band).
- **resize** keeping aspect ratio (the per-mode target; see tiling bead for which target).
- **pad to square with gray (127,127,127)** = `tuple(int(image_mean*255))` since image_mean=0.5 → 127; aspect preserved, equivalent to PIL `ImageOps.pad`.
- **normalize**: ToTensor → pixels to [0,1], then `(x-0.5)/0.5` → **[-1,1]**, since `image_mean = image_std = [0.5,0.5,0.5]`.
- **layout**: produce CHW f32 tensor.
Transcribed facts: `image_mean = image_std = [0.5,0.5,0.5]`; pad color gray (127,127,127); `patch_size=16`; `downsample_ratio=4`; `num_queries = ceil((image_size//patch_size)/downsample_ratio)`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Exact match to PIL `ImageOps.pad` and the reference ToTensor/normalize** — the resize filter and rounding must match the reference; a half-pixel offset blows L0.
- Keep this stage pure-Rust/no-FFI (image + fast_image_resize are pure Rust).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The reference resize **interpolation filter** (and any antialias flag) must be identified from the processor and reproduced exactly — bicubic vs bilinear, antialias on/off, changes pixels.
- Padding origin (centered vs head) and the gray fill value (exactly 127) must match.
- Channel order (RGB) and value range ([-1,1], not [0,1] or [0,255]) are load-bearing.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L0 EXACT: the resized/normalized/padded CHW tensor matches the oracle's preprocessed tensor exactly (bit-exact where the reference is deterministic; tile geometry exact).
- Gray pad is exactly 127; normalize maps to [-1,1]; aspect ratio preserved.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: square image (no pad); wide image (pad top/bottom); tall image (pad left/right); 1×1 and odd-size edge cases; verify [-1,1] range and gray-127 fill.
- See P1-preprocess-tests for the differential L0 gate vs the oracle preprocessed-tensor fixture.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-18 (full token census + per-mode sizing depends on the resolved preprocessing rules) and E-PM1 (oracle preprocessed-tensor fixtures + exact resize/pad/normalize rules from the pinned processor).

title: Dense layer-0 MLP (SwiGLU, intermediate 6848) for first_k_dense_replace=1
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Config `first_k_dense_replace = 1` -> **decoder layer 0 is a DENSE `DeepseekV2MLP`** (no router, no experts), while layers 1-11 are MoE. Layer 0's MLP has the model's widest intermediate dimension, **6848** (vs 896 per expert). It is the i32-overflow worst case later (K=6848 down_proj, plan §5.4) but in f32 v1 it is just a 3-GEMM SwiGLU that must match the reference exactly.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the dense layer-0 MLP in `src/native_engine/decoder.rs`: `down_proj( silu(gate_proj(x)) * up_proj(x) )` where `gate_proj`/`up_proj`: 1280->6848, `down_proj`: 6848->1280. Tensors `model.layers.0.mlp.{gate,up,down}_proj.weight` (f32 via `linear_tensor_f32` in v1; int8 in Phase 2). SiLU via `P1-silu`, same SwiGLU gate ordering as the experts (`silu(gate_proj(x)) * up_proj(x)`, confirm from spec). Layer 0 still uses R-SWA attention (only the MLP differs from layers 1-11; OQ-2 confirms attention is uniform).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Layer 0 is dense** (first_k_dense_replace=1): a frequent mistake is running MoE on layer 0; the manifest census + this dedicated op prevent it.
- **Intermediate 6848** is the widest GEMM (and the K=6848 i32-overflow worst case in Phase 2 — down_proj S8S8 <= 110.4M, U8S8 <= 221.7M, fits i32 with >= 9x headroom but is a proof obligation later, §5.4/doctrine 6). v1 f32 has no overflow concern.
- **Same SwiGLU ordering** as experts for consistency and correctness.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Do NOT route layer 0 through the MoE path; it has no `mlp.gate`/`experts` tensors — a wrong path is a load-time census failure or a crash.
- Determinism: fixed reduction order in the GEMMs.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 per-layer parity: layer-0 output (post-residual) vs oracle dump, cosine >= 0.9999 in f32, max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Determinism green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-dense-mlp0-tests`. Unit: SwiGLU at intermediate 6848 vs hand/f64 on a small slice; gate/up ordering lock. Integration: layer-0 output cosine vs oracle with NDJSON logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (first_k_dense_replace=1 + intermediate 6848 from census), **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (SwiGLU ordering), **P1-silu**.

title: Tests: dense layer-0 MLP SwiGLU parity (intermediate 6848)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Layer 0 being dense (not MoE) and having the widest intermediate (6848) are the two facts that make it a distinct op; these tests pin both.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit**: dense SwiGLU `down(silu(gate(x))*up(x))` at a small synthetic intermediate vs hand/f64.
2. **Unit — path**: assert layer 0 uses the dense MLP path, not the MoE router/expert path (no `mlp.gate` tensor consumed).
3. **Unit — ordering lock**: gate/up ordering matches the recorded reference.
4. **Integration (model-gated)**: layer-0 post-residual output cosine >= 0.9999 vs oracle.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON: `{layer:0, cosine, max_abs_diff, intermediate:6848}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration cosine >= 0.9999 (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-dense-mlp0**.

title: Final RMSNorm + lm_head GEMV (1280->129280, f32)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
After the 12 decoder layers, the model applies the final `model.norm` (RMSNorm) then the `lm_head` GEMV (1280->129280) to produce the per-token logits over the 129280 vocab. These logits are the L3 parity gate (within measured tolerance, argmax must match) and feed the greedy sampler. lm_head is huge and per-token (plan §6.1 HIGH priority). In f32 v1 it is `linear_tensor_f32`; int8 is a Phase-2 lever behind `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` (lm_head is high-value/high-risk, OQ-14, gate behind a measured-CER kill-switch).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement in `src/native_engine/decode.rs`:
1. **Final RMSNorm** via `P1-rmsnorm` over `model.norm.weight`.
2. **lm_head GEMV** `1280 -> 129280` (`lm_head.weight`, tied=false, kept f32 in v1) -> logits. Use `linear_tensor_f32`.
Return the full f32 logit vector (length 129280) per token for the L3 gate; the sampler (`P1-sampler`) consumes it (argmax-only under greedy — no full softmax over vocab, §6.10 fusion).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **lm_head stays f32/bf16 by default** (doctrine 2, §5.3): int8 lm_head goes BEYOND the validated NVFP4/GGUF set (OQ-14) and only behind `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` + a measured-CER gate — v1 is f32.
- **lm_head not tied** (config lm_head tied false): use the separate `lm_head.weight`, not `embed_tokens.weight`.
- **argmax-only under greedy** (§6.10): the sampler does not need a full softmax over 129280; lm_head produces logits, sampler takes argmax. (Full softmax only if temperature>0.)
- f64 accumulation in the GEMV where precision matters to keep L3 within the measured tolerance.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- 129280-wide argmax is tie-sensitive (bf16 oracle nondeterminism, §8.2): the L3 gate's 'argmax must match' applies over the prefix the oracle reproduces deterministically; the L3 tolerance is derived from the MEASURED oracle variance, NOT the inherited frankensearch 0.055.
- Determinism: fixed reduction order in the GEMV -> identical logits.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L3 parity: logits vs oracle pre-lm_head/pre-sampling dump, within the MEASURED quant/oracle tolerance; **argmax matches** where the oracle is deterministic.
- Final-norm + lm_head determinism green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-lmhead-tests`. Unit: small synthetic lm_head GEMV vs hand computation; argmax tie handling. Integration: per-token logits cosine + argmax-match vs oracle, with structured per-token max-diff + first-divergence logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (vocab 129280, lm_head shape, tied=false from census), **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (final-norm + lm_head contract), **P1-rmsnorm** (the final norm). Feeds **P1-sampler**.

title: Tests: lm_head logits parity (L3) + argmax match + determinism
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The lm_head logits are the L3 gate and the input to greedy sampling; an argmax flip is an L4 token divergence. These tests pin logit parity and argmax match against the measured oracle tolerance.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit**: small synthetic final-norm + lm_head GEMV vs hand/f64; argmax on a known logit vector with a deliberate tie.
2. **Unit — determinism**: same hidden -> identical logits.
3. **Integration (model-gated)**: per token over the golden corpus, logits cosine vs oracle pre-sampling dump >= the L3 threshold and within the MEASURED tolerance; argmax matches the oracle over the deterministic prefix; record first-divergence position.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per token: `{token_pos, cosine, max_abs_diff, argmax_ours, argmax_oracle, argmax_match}`; aggregate `{min_cosine, max_abs_diff, argmax_match_prefix_len, first_divergence_pos}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration logits within the measured L3 tolerance and argmax-match over the deterministic prefix (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-lmhead**.

title: Greedy sampler + no_repeat_ngram(size=35, window 128 single / 1024 multi) + EOS handling
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The sampler turns per-token logits into the output token sequence and enforces the model's generation semantics. Critically, `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with a sliding `ngram_window` that DIFFERS by mode — **128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image)** — are **first-class generation semantics, NOT just CLI flags** (plan §2.4, §6.4): the `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor` bans repeated 35-grams within the window. Replicating the EXACT window per mode is required for L4 token-exactness. This lives in `src/native_engine/decode.rs` with the frozen `DecodeParams`/`DecodeOutput` contract and the AR loop.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement in `decode.rs`:
1. **Greedy/argmax** under `temperature=0.0` (default): pick `argmax(logits)` (no full softmax — §6.10). For `temperature>0`, low-temp softmax+sample (confirm sampling RNG/seed semantics from spec; v1 default is greedy/deterministic).
2. **no_repeat_ngram_size=35**: before argmax, find all 35-grams whose first 34 tokens match the current suffix and set the logit of the banned continuation token to `-inf`. Track n-grams ONLY within the sliding `ngram_window` (**128 single-image / 1024 multi-image**, per OQ-18 — confirm the exact window per mode from `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor` in the spec).
3. **EOS handling**: stop on EOS id 1 (`<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`); strip the trailing EOS from the output token sequence (plan §2.4).
4. **AR loop**: `max_length=32768` cap; feed each sampled token back into the decoder (embed -> 12 layers -> lm_head) until EOS or cap; `infer(...)` defaults: `no_repeat_ngram_size=0, ngram_window=0, temperature=0.0` (the CLI/engine sets the OCR defaults).
5. **Frozen contract**: `DecodeParams{ max_length, no_repeat_ngram_size, ngram_window, temperature, eos_id }` and `DecodeOutput{ tokens, stop_reason }`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **ngram window is mode-dependent and a first-class semantic** (§2.4, OQ-18): 128 vs 1024 — wiring the wrong window changes output; it is NOT just a CLI flag.
- **argmax-only under greedy** (§6.10): skip the 129280-wide softmax under temperature 0.
- **Deterministic under fixed sampling** (§7.3 determinism gate): temperature 0 -> byte-identical output for same input+args.
- **EOS strip** matches the reference's trailing-EOS removal.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 35-gram ban with a 1024 window over a 129280 vocab must be efficient (a rolling hash / suffix index of the last `ngram_window` tokens), but correctness (exact banned set per the reference) outranks speed in v1.
- Banning to `-inf` then argmax: if ALL continuations are banned (rare), match the reference's fallback (likely no ban applies if it would empty the set — confirm from spec).
- Single vs multi-image mode selection drives the window (128/1024) — the engine must pass the correct mode.
- Determinism: argmax tie-break must match the reference (lowest index on ties — confirm).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L4: decoded token sequence EXACT vs the oracle over the deterministic prefix (§8.2), including the EOS strip and the ngram-ban behavior, for both single- and multi-image modes.
- Determinism gate: same image+args twice -> byte-identical token sequence.
- ngram window selected correctly per mode (128 single / 1024 multi).

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-sampler-tests` (dedicated). Unit: argmax tie-break; 35-gram ban with synthetic repeats at the window boundary (127/128/129 single; 1023/1024/1025 multi); EOS stop + strip; max_length cap. Integration: full decode token-exactness vs oracle for single- and multi-image golden docs, with NDJSON per-step logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (eos id, defaults), **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (exact ban semantics + argmax tie-break), **P1-lmhead** (provides logits), and **OQ-18** (the ngram_window per mode, and the token census that defines 'single' vs 'multi').

title: Tests: greedy sampler, no_repeat_ngram(35) window boundaries, EOS strip, determinism, token-exactness
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The sampler is the last gate before output; the ngram window (128/1024) and EOS strip are subtle and mode-dependent. These tests pin L4 token-exactness and the determinism gate.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — argmax + tie-break**: known logits -> expected token; tie -> reference tie-break (lowest index).
2. **Unit — ngram ban (single)**: synthetic token stream that would repeat a 35-gram; assert the banned continuation is set to -inf within the 128-token window and NOT banned outside it; test boundary at 127/128/129.
3. **Unit — ngram ban (multi)**: same at the 1024-token window boundary (1023/1024/1025).
4. **Unit — EOS**: stop on id 1 and strip the trailing EOS; max_length cap stops at 32768.
5. **Unit — all-banned fallback**: if every continuation is banned, behavior matches the recorded reference fallback.
6. **Unit — determinism**: same logits stream twice -> identical tokens.
7. **Integration (model-gated)**: full decode token sequence EXACT vs oracle for a single-image and a multi-image golden doc over the deterministic prefix; determinism gate (same input twice -> byte-identical).

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per step: `{step, mode, argmax, banned_count, eos, token_match}`; aggregate `{token_exact_prefix_len, first_divergence_pos, deterministic}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration token-exact over the deterministic prefix for both modes; determinism gate green (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-sampler**.

title: Postprocess: EOS strip, <|ref|>/<|det|> tag regex, bbox /999 rescale, markdown/<page> assembly
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The decoded token sequence is detokenized to text, then parsed into the final structured OCR output: Markdown with HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, normalized 0-999 bounding boxes, reading order, and `<page>` separators for multi-page (plan §2.4). This is the L5 end-to-end output that the user/agent consumes and the conformance corpus compares (`focr ocr --json`). Lives in `src/native_engine/postprocess.rs`.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement in `postprocess.rs`:
1. **Detokenize** the token sequence (via the tokenizer, vision/E-P1-tokenizer half) to text; the trailing EOS is already stripped by `P1-sampler` (belt-and-suspenders strip here too).
2. **Layout tag parsing**: parse `<|ref|>...<|/ref|><|det|>...<|/det|>` via regex (plan §2.4, §4.1 postprocess.rs) — `<|ref|>` holds the recognized text/label, `<|det|>` holds the bbox coords. Confirm the exact tag grammar from the spec.
3. **bbox rescale**: coords are normalized 0-999; rescale `coord/999 × W` (x) and `coord/999 × H` (y) to pixel space for the original image dimensions (plan §2.4: 'bbox coords /999 -> ×W/H'). Confirm the coord order (x1,y1,x2,y2) and rounding from spec.
4. **Markdown assembly**: assemble the Markdown (HTML tables, LaTeX preserved verbatim, reading order from the token stream).
5. **Multi-page**: split/join on `<page>` separators for multi-page docs.
6. **Prompt-mode taxonomy (OQ-8)**: whether bboxes are emitted in ALL modes (free OCR vs layout vs grounding vs markdown) is OQ-8 — honor the recorded answer (some modes may emit text-only, no `<|det|>`).

Return a structured `OcrResult` (markdown text + parsed bbox list + per-page split) so `--json` and the markdown human output both derive from it.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Derive both --json and markdown from one structured result** (§7.3): machine and human output never diverge.
- **bbox /999 rescale** is the documented coord contract; a wrong divisor or axis swap silently misplaces every box.
- **Prompt-mode-dependent bbox emission** (OQ-8): do not assume bboxes in every mode.
- **LaTeX/HTML preserved verbatim**: do not 'normalize' formula/table markup — exact-match is the L5 gate.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Regex must handle the DeepSeek glyph specials and malformed/truncated tags gracefully (a truncated `<|det|>` at max_length must not panic).
- bbox coord order + rounding must match the reference exactly (OQ-8 / spec).
- `<page>` handling for multi-page must align with the multi-image decode (cross-page dependence, OQ-13, is an attention concern; the output is still per-page-segmented).
- Determinism: pure transform of the token sequence.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L5 end-to-end: `focr ocr --json` output exact-match vs the frozen oracle reference (`tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json`) where deterministic; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the documented budget.
- bbox coords rescale correctly (round-trip a known normalized box to pixels and back).
- Tag parsing handles malformed/truncated input without panic.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-postprocess-tests`. Unit: tag-regex on synthetic `<|ref|>/<|det|>` strings (incl. malformed/truncated); bbox /999 rescale for known W/H; markdown/<page> assembly; mode-dependent bbox emission (OQ-8). Integration: golden-corpus `--json` exact-match + CER/TEDS with structured diff logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1**, **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (tag grammar + bbox coord contract), **P1-sampler** (provides the token sequence), and **OQ-8** (prompt-mode taxonomy + whether bboxes are emitted per mode). Detokenization uses the tokenizer (E-P1 vision/tokenizer half).

title: Tests: postprocess tag parsing, bbox /999 rescale, markdown assembly, L5 golden exact-match
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Postprocess produces the user-facing output and the L5 conformance artifact; tag/bbox/markdown bugs are visible to the end user. These tests pin the structured output.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — tag regex**: parse well-formed `<|ref|>text<|/ref|><|det|>coords<|/det|>`; malformed/truncated tags do not panic and degrade gracefully.
2. **Unit — bbox rescale**: a normalized box (e.g. `[100,200,800,900]` /999) rescales to pixels for known (W,H); axis order + rounding match the spec.
3. **Unit — markdown/<page>**: assembly preserves LaTeX/HTML verbatim; multi-page splits on `<page>`.
4. **Unit — mode-dependent emission (OQ-8)**: a mode that emits text-only produces no bboxes; a grounding mode emits bboxes.
5. **Integration (model-gated)**: golden-corpus `focr ocr --json` exact-match vs frozen oracle reference (canonicalized: strip timing, sort bbox); aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within budget.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per doc: `{doc, cer, teds, exact_match, bbox_count, first_diff}`; aggregate `{mean_cer, exact_match_rate}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration exact-match where deterministic and CER/TEDS within budget (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-postprocess**.

title: Decoder orchestration: 12-layer loop wiring (embed -> [RMSNorm->R-SWA->residual->RMSNorm->MLP/MoE->residual]×12 -> final norm -> lm_head)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the glue that assembles the per-op kernels into the full DeepSeek-V2 MoE decoder forward (`src/native_engine/decoder.rs`), driving both prefill (build reference KV from visual+prompt) and the AR decode loop. It is the f32-parity integration point: it must reproduce the reference's exact layer structure, residual connections, and the dense(layer0)/MoE(layers1-11) split. It consumes the vision features (via the connector) and produces logits for the sampler.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the 12-layer decoder loop:
- **Input**: the fused embedding stream (token embeds via `P1-token-embed` with vision embeds masked-scattered in by the connector — vision half).
- **Per layer i (0..11)**: `h = h + RSWA_attn( RMSNorm_input(h) )` (RoPE applied to Q/K inside attention); then `h = h + MLP_i( RMSNorm_post_attn(h) )` where `MLP_0` = dense (`P1-dense-mlp0`) and `MLP_{1..11}` = MoE (`P1-moe-router` + `P1-moe-experts`). Confirm the exact residual + pre/post-norm placement (DeepseekV2: pre-norm, residual around attn and around MLP) from the spec.
- **Attention is uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers** (OQ-2, incl. layer 0; first_k_dense_replace affects ONLY the MLP). Honor the recorded OQ-2 answer.
- **Prefill vs decode**: prefill builds the reference KV block (visual+prompt) for all layers; decode runs one token at a time through the ring + reference block. Pre-allocate all per-forward buffers once and reuse across pages (§6.12, zero per-token allocation).
- **Final**: `RMSNorm_final(h)` -> `lm_head` (`P1-lmhead`) -> logits -> sampler (`P1-sampler`).
- **Concurrency**: the forward fans out across cores via the kernel's own rayon pool; the outer page/document loop is SEQUENTIAL (§6.5, AGENTS.md doctrine 5: never nest rayon under a lock, exactly one live forward).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **dense layer-0 / MoE layers-1-11 split** (first_k_dense_replace=1): the loop must branch the MLP by layer index; attention is uniform R-SWA (OQ-2).
- **Pre-norm + residual** per DeepseekV2: a wrong norm/residual placement silently shifts every layer.
- **Pre-allocate + reuse buffers** (§6.12): no per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop.
- **Sequential outer loop, internal fan-out** (doctrine 5): the deadlock-proof template; a `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (owned by E-TEST) guards regression.
- **f32 v1** (Phase 1): no quant, no hand-SIMD; correctness first.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Buffer aliasing between the residual stream and the norm scratch is a subtle bug; keep distinct buffers or prove the in-place op is safe.
- The KV-cap INVARIANT (`L·(m+128)`) and 'exactly one live forward' are gauntlet e-process invariants (§8.5) — assert them.
- Cancellation: thread a `checkpoint()` into the per-decode-step loop so Ctrl+C / per-page timeout aborts cleanly (§3.3) — cooperative cancellation, token goes INTO the closure.
- Determinism: fixed layer/reduction order -> identical hidden states and logits.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 per-layer parity: every decoder-layer hidden state vs oracle dumps, cosine >= 0.9999 in f32; L3 logits within measured tolerance; L4 tokens exact over the deterministic prefix; L5 end-to-end CER ~= reference.
- Determinism gate green; KV-cap invariant holds; no per-token allocation in the hot loop (verified).
- No `[OPEN]` blocks any wired kernel (OQ-1,2,3,4,5,13,18 resolved).

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-decoder-loop-tests` (the end-to-end decoder integration test). Plus this bead is the convergence point for the L2-L5 parity ladder across the decoder; it depends on every per-op test passing first.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1**, **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (layer structure/residual placement), and ALL the per-op decoder beads it wires: **P1-token-embed**, **P1-rmsnorm**, **P1-rope**, **P1-rswa-attn**, **P1-moe-router**, **P1-moe-experts**, **P1-dense-mlp0**, **P1-lmhead**, plus **OQ-2** (uniform R-SWA across layers). Consumes the connector/masked-scatter fused embedding stream from the vision half of E-P1.

title: Tests: full decoder integration L2-L5 parity ladder + determinism + KV-cap invariant + e2e CER
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the decoder's end-to-end parity gate — the convergence test that proves the whole decoder+output half matches the bf16 reference within f32 tolerance on the golden corpus. It exercises the full L2-L5 ladder with the comprehensive structured logging the project mandates.

## WHAT TO DO
Add a `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` decoder path (model-gated; skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights, prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`):
1. **L2 per-layer**: every decoder-layer hidden state vs oracle `.npy` dumps, cosine >= 0.9999; max-abs-diff ledgered.
2. **L3 logits**: per-token logits within the MEASURED L3 tolerance; argmax matches over the deterministic prefix.
3. **L4 tokens**: decoded token sequence EXACT vs oracle over the deterministic prefix (per §8.2 nondeterminism floor — establish the oracle's own nondeterminism envelope first).
4. **L5 end-to-end**: `focr ocr --json` text + bbox vs frozen oracle reference; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the documented budget.
5. **Determinism gate**: same image+args twice -> byte-identical output.
6. **KV-cap invariant**: assert KV per layer never exceeds `L·(m+128)` over the run.
7. **No-realloc**: assert zero per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop (instrument the allocator or a counter).

Run over the per-regime corpus {sparse page, dense page, 10-page, 40-page} so cross-page (OQ-13) behavior is exercised, and over both single- and multi-image modes (ngram window 128/1024).

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per layer/token/doc: `{doc, regime, mode, layer, token_pos, cosine, max_abs_diff, argmax_match, token_match, kv_len}`; aggregate `{min_layer_cosine, l3_tol, token_exact_prefix_len, first_divergence_pos, mean_cer, teds, deterministic, max_kv_len}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 min cosine >= 0.9999; L3 within measured tolerance + argmax match; L4 token-exact over the deterministic prefix; L5 CER/TEDS within budget; determinism green; KV-cap invariant holds; no per-token allocation. (Skips green without weights.)

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-decoder-loop** (the integrated forward), and on **P1-sampler** + **P1-postprocess** (needed for the L4/L5 end-to-end output). This is the decoder half's exit-gate test for the E-P1 epic.

title: Base vs Gundam tiling: dynamic_preprocess / find_closest_aspect_ratio
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Unlimited-OCR retains TWO resolution modes (DeepEncoder natively had 5). Tiling decides how many image tensors and how many tokens each page produces — which drives ALL downstream KV/buffer sizing and the R-SWA reference block `m`. Wrong tiling = wrong token count = wrong everything. Part of the L0 exact gate (tile geometry must match).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/preprocess/tile.rs`:
- **Base mode**: 1024×1024, `crop_mode=false` — multi-page/PDF; **256 visual (image-feature) tokens/page** (16×16 compressed grid).
- **Gundam mode**: `base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `crop_mode=true` — dynamic-resolution tiling for a single page; a global padded 1024 view (`global_view_pos='head'`) PLUS 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()` / `find_closest_aspect_ratio()` over `i*j ∈ [min_num, max_num]`; each 640 crop ≈ **100 tokens**. `tile_tag='2D'`, `candidate_resolutions=[[1024,1024]]`.
Transcribed `infer()` defaults: `base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True`.
The token CLASS census (OQ-18): a 1024 view yields **256 image-feature tokens (16×16) PLUS structural tokens** → placeholder structure `(16 + 1)·16 + 1 = 273` (16 image tokens + 1 image_newline per row × 16 rows + 1 view_seperator).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
