3. **Assert no oversubscription**: log + assert that at most ONE live forward fans out at a time (the §6.5 invariant) and that `workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical-core budget` (the §6.9 capacity-certificate invariant). Emit these as structured `stage` log lines with `simd_tier` + thread counts.
4. **Model-gated**: if weights are present, run real forwards; if absent, run a faithful STUB forward that reproduces the SAME concurrency topology (kernel rayon fan-out called from the sequential loop, the engine owning one runtime) so the deadlock SHAPE is exercised in CI without the 6.67 GB model. The stub must use the real orchestrator/`OcrEngine` concurrency code, not a fake — only the math is stubbed.
5. **Regression-detector self-test**: a `#[ignore]`-by-default variant that INJECTS a nested-rayon-under-lock (or a nested second runtime) and asserts the watchdog TIMES OUT — proving the watchdog actually catches the regression (run manually, not in the green CI path).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Pages ≫ pool** is the exact condition the frankensearch saga proved exposes the deadlock — fewer pages can mask it.
- **Stub-but-real-topology** so CI (no weights) still guards the concurrency architecture; the deadlock is in the ORCHESTRATION, not the math, so stubbing the math is sound and necessary.
- **The hang IS the signal**: a deadlock cannot be caught by an assertion that never runs; the wall-clock timeout converts a hang into a deterministic CI failure.
- **Capacity-certificate invariants logged** (§3.3/§6.9): no nested-runtime, no rayon-pool oversubscription, one live forward — these are the soak-test's pass conditions.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Cancellation is cooperative**: `spawn_blocking` keeps running on drop, so the watchdog timing out does not necessarily reclaim the thread — the test process may need to be killed; in CI the job timeout handles it, but the test should still report FAIL via the watchdog thread before the job timeout where possible.
- The stub forward must NOT accidentally serialize everything (which would mask a real deadlock) — it must reproduce the real fan-out-under-the-sequential-loop topology.
- NUMA/affinity features (`FOCR_NUMA`, hwloc) are behind a feature flag; the watchdog runs the no-NUMA fallback by default and the NUMA path when the feature is enabled.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` completes within its wall-clock budget on a healthy tree (CI green).
- The injected-regression self-test TIMES OUT (proving the watchdog catches a nested-rayon-under-lock / nested-runtime regression) — demonstrated once and documented.
- Structured logs assert: ≤1 live forward at a time; `workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical cores`; no nested runtime.
- Runs model-gated (real forwards with weights; real-topology stub without).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The watchdog itself (the soak).
- The `#[ignore]` regression-detector that proves the watchdog times out under an injected violation.
- A capacity-certificate log assertion test (p95/p99 queueing evidence + no-oversubscription proof, §3.3).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs `OcrEngine` owning the one asupersync Runtime, the sequential page-loop facade, and the kernel rayon pool wiring from the scaffold.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the concurrency-topology + capacity-certificate log lines.

title: Phase -1: Source/Oracle Truth Pack — pin sources, hash everything, resolve every OPEN/OQ before any kernel
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the executable foundation of franken_ocr and **the highest-EV epic in the project**. Per the `/idea-wizard` review transcribed in plan §10 Phase -1: *"the single highest-EV move is NOT a kernel — it is an executable truth pack that stops the project from optimizing the wrong model semantics (the model changed on 2026-06-24)."* The target model `baidu/Unlimited-OCR` was released ~2026-06-22, **post knowledge-cutoff**, so every model-specific claim in the master plan is tagged [VERIFIED] (confirmed from a primary source), [REPORTED] (paper/card, not line-verified), or [OPEN] (must be code-confirmed). This epic converts all [OPEN]/OQ items into pinned, line-backed answers and freezes the exact sources so every downstream kernel is built against a known model version.

The over-arching goal franken_ocr serves: **max CPU speed for THIS one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels, correctness before speed, a portable single binary, agent-ergonomic**. None of that is possible if the model semantics are wrong. AGENTS.md hard rule: *"no kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN]. A phase exit gate cannot pass while it depends on an unresolved OQ."* This epic is what makes that rule satisfiable.

## WHAT TO DO (the Phase -1 deliverable, plan §10)
Produce, all under `docs/truth-pack/` with a machine-readable `claim_id → source-line` index:
1. **Pin the exact sources**: HF repo commit + GitHub commit. Codex-reported candidates to VERIFY against the live repos: `HF 3a7f4dbbbffcc6f9282712c5b0d7cc31b3812da5`, `GitHub 7e98affeacba24e95562fbaa234ddb89b856874a` — record the *verified* hashes, not the reported ones.
2. **Snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source**: `config.json`, `tokenizer.json`, `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`, `modeling_deepseekv2.py`, `deepencoder.py`, `conversation.py`, `model.safetensors.index.json`, `LICENSE`.
3. **Generate the machine-readable token/shape/buffer census** from the pinned config (§2.4, §8.6), CI-guarded against source drift.
4. **Extract the spec** `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` (§8.6 / §10 Phase 1) — every data structure, exact preprocessing/tiling rules, `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` semantics, projector concat order, tokenizer rules — verbatim from the pinned `modeling_*.py`/`deepencoder.py`/`conversation.py`.
5. **Resolve every [OPEN]/OQ-1..OQ-18 (§13)** with a quoted, line-backed citation from the pinned source.
6. **Decide the oracle strategy (OQ-17, CPU-vs-GPU)** — prove CPU HF runs, else GPU-correctness + CPU-perf split (§8.1) — and capture one smoke fixture.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Spec-first porting** (`/porting-to-rust`, §8.6): *extract spec → implement from spec → never translate line-by-line.* The four documents are PLAN_TO_PORT (the master plan), EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md (THE SPEC, this epic produces it), PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md. Kernels are implemented from the spec, which is what resolves the §13 [OPEN]s.
- **Pin + hash before anything**: the model is freshly released and may be re-uploaded; a moving reference silently invalidates every fixture and every measured ratio. The negative-evidence ledger schema (docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) *requires* a `model source commit + fixture hash` field on every entry — that field is sourced from this truth pack.
- **CI-guarded census**: plan §2.4 — *"A machine-readable token/shape/buffer census (§8.6, generated from the pinned config) is the source of truth and CI fails if the source/config changes."*

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Plan §11 risk *"[OPEN] model details wrong"* (MED-HIGH): hard rule no kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN]; this epic gates Phase 1. R-SWA/RoPE/projector/SAM-window must read the actual source first.
- The token budget is **NOT just 256** (§2.4): a 1024 view yields 256 image-feature + structural tokens ⇒ `(16+1)·16 + 1 = 273` slots; the census must count every token CLASS or KV/buffer sizing is wrong.
- OQ-17 gotcha: `infer()` is CUDA-oriented; a CPU bf16 HF oracle is not guaranteed to run, which decides the meaning of G2 (perf claim).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase -1 exit gates, plan §10)
- Every OQ (OQ-1..OQ-18) answered with a pinned-source citation (file + line range + quoted text).
- All source hashes recorded (SHA-256 of every load-bearing file + verified HF/GitHub commits).
- Census generated + CI-guarded against source drift.
- Oracle strategy decided + smoke fixture captured.
- **No kernel work begins until this phase is green.**

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A CI guard test that re-hashes the pinned sources and fails on drift (the census generator's drift guard).
- A `claim_id → source-line` index that is itself validated (every OQ answer references a real, in-range line of a hashed file).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
This epic depends on nothing — it is the root of the dependency graph. Every kernel epic (E-P1, E-P2, E-P3, E-P4) and verification epic (E-VERIFY) depends on E-PM1 and on specific OQ beads it owns. The OQ beads here are the foundation every kernel depends on.

title: Pin + verify the HF repo commit and GitHub commit for baidu/Unlimited-OCR
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The target model was released ~2026-06-22 and could be re-uploaded at any time; HF model repos are mutable. Every golden fixture, every measured perf ratio, and every OQ answer is only meaningful relative to an EXACT immutable commit. Plan §10 Phase -1 makes pinning the first task. The negative-evidence ledger (docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) and DISCREPANCIES.md both require a `model source commit + fixture hash` provenance field — that value comes from here.

## WHAT TO DO
1. Resolve and VERIFY the HF repo commit for `baidu/Unlimited-OCR`. Codex reported `HF 3a7f4dbbbffcc6f9282712c5b0d7cc31b3812da5` — do NOT trust the reported value; query the live HF repo (e.g. `huggingface_hub` revision listing / `git ls-remote https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR`) and record the *verified* full-length commit SHA, the resolution date, and the method used.
2. Resolve and VERIFY the GitHub commit. Codex reported `GitHub 7e98affeacba24e95562fbaa234ddb89b856874a` — verify against the live GitHub repo (`git ls-remote`) and record the verified SHA.
3. Write both verified commits into `docs/truth-pack/PINNED_SOURCES.md` (or equivalent) with: repo URL, verified commit SHA, resolution timestamp, and a note if the verified hash DIFFERS from the Codex-reported one (which would itself be evidence the model moved).
4. Record the runtime pin from the README that the oracle must use: `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, `Pillow==12.1.1`, `pymupdf` — NOT the `4.46.3` recorded in config.json (plan §8.1, §11 reference-version-drift risk).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Verify, don't trust the reported hash**: the whole point of pinning is to detect a moved model; copying Codex's number defeats it.
- **Pin runtime to README, not config**: plan §8.1 explicitly warns config records `4.46.3` while README pins runtime `4.57.1`/`torch 2.10.0`; the oracle and all fixtures use the README runtime.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If the live HF hash differs from the Codex-reported one, the model HAS changed since the plan was written — flag loudly; all [REPORTED] facts must be re-verified.
- HF repos can have separate revisions for LFS pointers vs the resolved blob; record the commit that resolves the safetensors + config + tokenizer together.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/truth-pack/PINNED_SOURCES.md` contains verified full-length HF + GitHub commit SHAs, resolution timestamps, and the verification method.
- Runtime pin (`transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`) recorded.
- Any divergence from the Codex-reported hashes is explicitly flagged.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A check (script or CI step) that the recorded commit still resolves the expected file set; a divergence is a loud failure (this feeds PM1-hash-sources and PM1-census-generator drift guards).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on E-PM1 (parent). Blocks PM1-hash-sources (you hash files at a pinned commit) and PM1-oracle-strategy (the oracle uses the pinned runtime).

title: OQ-4: Q/K head dim when use_mla=false and qk_nope/rope_head_dim=0 (likely 128 from q_proj shape)
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-4)
What is the Q/K head dimension when `use_mla=false` and `qk_nope_head_dim=0` / `qk_rope_head_dim=0`? Plan §2.3.2 notes config does not directly give it in this configuration, but it is *"almost certainly 128 from q_proj shape."* Confirm from the actual q_proj weight shape.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_deepseekv2.py` (how q/k head_dim is computed when MLA is off) AND the `q_proj` weight shape from `model.safetensors.index.json` / the tensor census: q_proj is `[out, 1280]`; out = num_attention_heads(10) × head_dim. If out=1280 ⇒ head_dim=128 (consistent with v_head_dim=128, 10·128=1280).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Attention shapes — the q/k/v/o_proj GEMM dimensions and the RoPE rotation dim (`decoder.rs`, `rswa.rs`). A wrong head_dim mis-shapes every attention matmul.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.2: `v_head_dim=128`, `num_attention_heads=10`, `num_key_value_heads=10` (no GQA), `use_mla=false` (plain MHA, no latent decompression). Plan §6.4: *"Plain MHA (no MLA decompression): q/k/v/o_proj separate dense GEMMs, 10 heads, head_dim 128."* Pinning head_dim from the actual tensor shape removes the last [OPEN] in the attention shape set.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: Q/K head_dim value (expected 128), cited to both the modeling code path and the q_proj tensor shape in the census/index.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources and PM1-census-generator (q_proj shape). Feeds the E-P1 attention kernels and the RoPE kernel (OQ-5).

title: OQ-5: rope_theta value (default 10000 assumed); is YARN/NTK active at 32K?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-5)
What is the `rope_theta` value? Plan §2.3.2 assumes the DeepseekV2 default 10000 but flags it as not surfaced. And: is YARN/NTK scaling active at the 32K-native context, or are those classes present-but-inactive?

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`configuration_deepseek_v2.py` and the config `language_config` (the hashed `config.json`). Read `rope_theta`, any `rope_scaling` dict (YARN/NTK type + factor), and whether the active `DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding` class uses scaling at `max_position_embeddings=32768`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The RoPE kernel (a BUILD item, plan §4.3: *"RoPE (theta default 10000; YARN present but likely unused). new kernel (confirm theta first)."*). A wrong theta or an unexpectedly-active YARN scaling rotates Q/K incorrectly → attention drift across all layers.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.2: *"RoPE DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding; YARN/NTK classes present but likely inactive at 32K-native. [OPEN] rope_theta not surfaced (DeepseekV2 default 10000 assumed) — read directly before kernel work."* RoPE feeds the R-SWA attention every layer; getting theta wrong is a silent, pervasive error.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: exact `rope_theta`, the presence/absence/type of `rope_scaling`, and whether YARN/NTK is active at 32K, cited to config + configuration_deepseek_v2.py.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds the E-P1 RoPE kernel and the RMSNorm→RoPE fusion (§6.10). Coupled to OQ-4 (head_dim defines the rotation dim).

title: OQ-6: Exact SAM⊕CLIP feature-fusion / concat order producing the 2048-dim projector input
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-6)
What is the exact SAM⊕CLIP feature-fusion / concat order producing the 2048-dim projector input? Plan §2.3.1 flags it [OPEN]: *"SAM-1024 ⊕ CLIP-1024 vs interleaved; deepencoder.py mentions low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu / hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu variants — MUST be read line-by-line from deepencoder.py before implementing the projector."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`deepencoder.py` (line-level). Read how SAM features (1024-dim after neck) and CLIP-L features (1024-dim) are combined into the 2048-dim input to the single linear projector (`projector_config`: input_dim=2048, n_embed=1280, projector_type=linear). Determine: plain concat order (SAM then CLIP vs CLIP then SAM), interleaving, and which `*_hybrid_split_*` code path is actually taken.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The projector + vision bridge (`vision_bridge.rs`). A wrong concat order feeds the 2048→1280 linear the wrong channel layout → every downstream decode token is built on corrupted vision features (catastrophic, but only visible as L1/L2 vision-stage cosine drop). On the Phase-1 critical path per plan §13.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.1: SAM features + CLIP features concatenated to 2048 dims → single linear projector 2048→1280. The 16× compression bridge (64×64→16×16=256 tokens) precedes this. The concat order is the kind of detail that is invisible in the architecture diagram but corrupts everything if wrong.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the exact fusion/concat order and the specific `*_hybrid_split_*` path taken, cited to `deepencoder.py` line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (projector section) and the E-P1 `vision_bridge.rs`. On the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

title: OQ-7: Gundam-mode vision-token count per aspect ratio; dynamic_preprocess min_num/max_num defaults
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-7)
What is the exact Gundam-mode vision-token count per aspect ratio, and what are the `dynamic_preprocess` `min_num`/`max_num` defaults? Plan §2.4 reports the bounds as 2..32 but flags them [OPEN]: *"i*j ∈ [min_num, max_num] (reported 2..32). Each 640 crop ≈ 100 tokens."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` — `dynamic_preprocess()` and `find_closest_aspect_ratio()`. Read the literal default `min_num`/`max_num`, how `i*j` tile combinations are enumerated, and the per-tile token count (each 640×640 crop ≈ 100 tokens; confirm exact). Cross-check against `candidate_resolutions=[[1024,1024]]`, `base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `global_view_pos='head'`, `tile_tag='2D'`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Preprocess tiling (`tile.rs`) and buffer sizing (the census, PM1-census-generator). Gundam multi-tile token totals feed the R-SWA reference-block `m` worst case and the prefill GEMM token counts.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.4: Gundam = dynamic-resolution tiling for a single page; global padded 1024 view + 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()`. The exact min/max tile bounds determine the maximum token count a single page can produce — which sizes the prefill buffers and the reference block.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: literal `min_num`/`max_num` defaults, the per-aspect-ratio tile enumeration, and the per-tile token count, cited to `dynamic_preprocess`/`find_closest_aspect_ratio` line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-census-generator (Gundam token-class cells) and the E-P1 preprocess `tile.rs`. Coupled to OQ-18 (the full token census).

title: OQ-8: Full prompt-mode taxonomy and whether bboxes are emitted in all modes
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-8)
What is the full prompt-mode taxonomy (free OCR / layout / grounding / markdown), and are bounding boxes emitted in ALL modes or only some? Plan §2.4 flags it [OPEN]: *"Full prompt-mode taxonomy ... and whether bboxes are emitted in all modes is not documented — read the processor/conversation.py."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`conversation.py` and the `UnlimitedOCRHFProcessor`. Read the prompt templates (e.g. `'<image>document parsing.'`, `'<image>Multi page parsing.'`) and the conversation/mode taxonomy. Determine which modes emit the `<|ref|>…<|/ref|><|det|>…<|/det|>` layout/grounding tags (bbox coords /999) vs plain text/markdown.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Postprocess (`postprocess.rs` — EOS strip, `<|ref|>`/`<|det|>` regex, bbox /999 rescale, markdown assembly) and the CLI prompt contract (which prompts `focr ocr` exposes). If a mode does NOT emit bboxes, the postprocessor must not expect them.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.4: output is structured Markdown with HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, normalized 0-999 (×/999) bbox coords, layout tags parsed via regex. Knowing the mode taxonomy lets the CLI offer the right prompt presets and the postprocessor parse the right output shape per mode.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the enumerated prompt modes and a per-mode statement of whether bboxes are emitted, cited to `conversation.py`/processor line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (generation/output section), the E-P1 postprocess, and the E-P5 CLI prompt contract.

title: OQ-9: Exact activated-param count (500M vs 570M) — docs-only, not kernel-blocking
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-9)
What is the exact activated-parameter count per token — 500M or 570M? Plan §2.2 notes the conflict: *"paper abstract + §3.2 say 500M; Figure 2 labels '3B-MOE-A570M' → treat as ~500–570M, [OPEN] exact figure."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The paper Figure 2 (`Unlimited-OCR.pdf`) AND recompute from the safetensors tensor census: activated params = embed + per-token-active expert subset (6 routed + 2 shared of the MoE FFN) + attention + norms + lm_head. Use the census (PM1-census-generator) to recompute and reconcile with the paper's label.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Docs/claims only (NOT kernel-blocking, per plan §13). It does inform the decode roofline memory-floor estimate (plan §9.1: *"a single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token"*), so a precise figure sharpens the roofline target.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.9: decode is memory-bandwidth-bound, streaming ~500M active params/token; the USL pool-sizing (AF-5) and the roofline memory floor use this number. A precise activated count makes the decode memory-floor honest.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A recomputed activated-param count from the census, reconciled against the paper label, with the reconciliation recorded.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED] (or [REPORTED] with the recompute noted if the paper is the only source).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-census-generator (recompute from tensor census). Feeds docs/claims and the decode roofline (E-P3 §9.1). Not on the kernel critical path.

title: OQ-10: Does community GGUF (PR #17400) quantize router/experts identically to NVFP4 (router-gate-in-f32)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-10)
Does the community GGUF quantization (requiring llama.cpp PR #17400, not upstream) quantize the router/experts identically to NVFP4 — specifically, does it keep the MoE router gate in f32? Plan §2.6 notes both prior-art quants keep the vision encoder unquantized; this cross-checks the router-gate treatment.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-GGUF` repo + llama.cpp PR #17400. Inspect which tensors are quantized vs kept high-precision, focusing on the router `mlp.gate.weight` (1280→64) and the expert `{gate,up,down}_proj`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Quant-recipe cross-check (NOT blocking, per plan §13). It confirms the §6.7 decision that the router is *never* quantized: *"Router: ... Never quantized (NVFP4 keeps it; quantizing the gate drifts expert selection → cliff)."*

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.6: GGUF recommended Q4_K_M (~1.82 GiB) keeps vision/mmproj F16. If GGUF (like NVFP4) keeps the router gate f32, that is two independent prior-art confirmations of the core recipe (decoder FFN/expert weight-only quant, router/norms/vision high-precision).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A recorded answer: GGUF's router/expert quant treatment, especially whether the router gate stays f32, cross-referenced to NVFP4 (OQ-14).
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED]/[REPORTED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (the GGUF listing snapshot). Cross-checks OQ-14 (NVFP4 module set). Informs the E-P2/E-P4 quant recipe. Not blocking.

title: OQ-11: Is int8 viable for the patch-embed conv specifically (cleaner stats than mid-network ViT)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-11)
Is int8 viable for the patch-embed conv specifically — does it have cleaner activation statistics than mid-network ViT layers (which have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant)? This is an isolated later experiment (Phase 4+), not a v1 question.

## SOURCE / EXPERIMENT TO RUN
An isolated experiment (Phase 4+), NOT a source read of the model definition. Using the per-layer activation dumps from the oracle (PM1-oracle-strategy), inspect the activation distribution at the patch-embed conv output (non-overlapping k=stride, so im2col is exact) vs mid-network ViT post-LayerNorm/post-GELU outputs, and measure whether per-channel int8 + per-token dynamic activation quant on patch-embed holds CER.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
A later vision-int8 experiment ONLY (Phase 4+). Vision stays BF16/F32 in v1 (the validated recipe). Not on any v1 critical path.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.4: *"keep the vision tower BF16/F32 in v1 (the validated recipe; ViT post-LayerNorm/post-GELU activations have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant). Revisit int8 vision ... only after decoder parity is proven, as an isolated experiment."* Patch-embed (§6.4: *"non-overlapping (k=stride) ... ideal SMMLA/VNNI target"*) is the most promising vision-int8 candidate because its stats are cleanest.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A recorded hypothesis + (later) measured result on whether patch-embed int8 holds CER, gated behind decoder-parity-locked.
- [OPEN] remains a deferred experiment marker (low priority; not a Phase -1 blocker, but recorded so the lever is not forgotten).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-oracle-strategy (activation dumps). Feeds a Phase-4+ vision-int8 spike (E-P4). Lowest priority; explicitly deferred.

title: OQ-12: Confirm benchmark cells (TPS, v1.6 peers, 40+pg edit distances) from PDF tables
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-12)
Confirm the benchmark cells — throughput (TPS), the OmniDocBench v1.6 peer table, and the 40+page edit distances — from the PDF tables, vs the secondary sources the plan currently cites as [REPORTED].

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`Unlimited-OCR.pdf` tables. Verify: OmniDocBench v1.5 overall 93.23 / v1.6 93.92; peers PaddleOCR-VL 1.6 96.33, MinerU 2.5-Pro 95.69, PaddleOCR-VL 1.5 94.50, Qianfan-OCR 93.12, DeepSeek-OCR-2 91.09; long-horizon edit distance 2pg 0.0362 / 10pg 0.0526 / 40+pg 0.1069; ~5580 TPS (+12.7% vs DeepSeek-OCR, +35% at 6144 out-tokens). Tables/math: TEDS 90.93 / TEDS-S 94.07, Formula-CDM 92.61, Text-Edit 0.038 (v1.5).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Docs/claims only (per plan §13). It informs the honest non-goal framing (§1.2: *"Not leaderboard SOTA ... ~93.9% on OmniDocBench v1.6, behind PaddleOCR-VL 1.6 and MinerU 2.5-Pro"*) and the profiling regime expectations (long-output advantage grows because constant-KV avoids decode slowdown).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
The project's pitch (§1.2) is fidelity + bounded generated-token KV + speed, NOT beating the benchmark. Confirming the numbers keeps the README/CHANGELOG honest.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The benchmark cells confirmed against the PDF tables, [REPORTED] upgraded to [VERIFIED] where the table confirms.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (the PDF snapshot, if obtainable). Feeds docs/claims only. Not blocking.

title: OQ-13: Does R-SWA's reference block span ALL pages in a multi-page pass (page N depends on 1..N-1)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-13)
Does R-SWA's reference block span ALL pages in a multi-page pass, making page *N* attend to (and depend on) pages `1..N-1`? Plan §13 marks this as determining both the §8.3 metamorphic gate AND whether the §9.5 cross-page reference-KV "sharing" idea is even sound.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` + `infer_multi(...)` in `modeling_*.py` (`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` / `modeling_deepseekv2.py`). Plan §2.4: *"Multi-page infer_multi(...) concatenates pages at <image> with a dummy crop tensor to force the no-crop branch."* Read whether, in a single 32K pass, ALL pages' visual+prompt prefixes form ONE frozen reference block (so page N can attend to pages 1..N-1), or whether pages are independent.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Three things: (1) the §8.3 metamorphic gate — AGENTS.md doctrine #7 and plan §8.3 forbid asserting *"multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses"*; if cross-page dependent, the defensible property is the OPPOSITE (*"changing page order / earlier-page content MAY change later-page output"*). (2) The §9.5 cross-page reference-KV sharing optimization — *"Only sound if the multi-page reference block does NOT make page N depend on 1..N-1; if it does ... naive KV-sharing would silently corrupt later pages."* (3) Multi-page decode correctness generally.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
AGENTS.md doctrine #7: *"Multi-page decode is cross-page dependent (OQ-13) — do not assume 'multi-page = sum of single-page parses.'"* This is a correctness-and-test-design-defining question, on the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: whether the multi-page reference block is shared across all pages (page N depends on 1..N-1) or pages are independent, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` + `infer_multi` line ranges.
- The resulting metamorphic property statement and the soundness verdict on cross-page KV-sharing recorded.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (multi-page section), the E-VERIFY metamorphic gate (§8.3), the E-P3/E-P4 KV-sharing spike (§9.5), and multi-page decode correctness in E-P1. On the Phase-1 critical path. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-3.

title: Snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source file under docs/truth-pack/
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The spec extraction and every OQ answer quote specific lines of specific source files. Those quotes are only trustworthy if the files are content-addressed and frozen. Plan §10 Phase -1: *"snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source."* The hashes become the `fixture hash` provenance field every NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md / DISCREPANCIES.md entry carries (plan §8.4 artifact-graph fields: model source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, exact command + env, fallback/kill-switch state).

## WHAT TO DO
At the pinned commit (from PM1-pin-sources), snapshot and SHA-256 each load-bearing file, storing the snapshot + its hash + byte length under `docs/truth-pack/sources/` with a manifest `docs/truth-pack/SOURCE_HASHES.md`:
- `config.json` (top-level + `language_config`, `vision_config.sam_vit_b`, `vision_config.'clip-l-14-224'`, `projector_config`)
- `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB, vocab 129280, the DeepSeek vocab)
- `tokenizer_config.json`
- `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`
- `modeling_deepseekv2.py` (contains `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`, `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`)
- `deepencoder.py`
- `conversation.py`
- `configuration_deepseek_v2.py`
- `model.safetensors.index.json` (total_size 6,672,212,480 bytes, 2710 tensors)
- `LICENSE` (MIT, Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu)
- (Optional secondary oracles) the `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-NVFP4` and `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-GGUF` tensor/scale listings for OQ-14 / OQ-10.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Content-addressed sources**: a `claim_id → (file_sha256, line range, quoted text)` index makes every spec claim reproducible and re-checkable; this is the backbone of the spec-first method (§8.6).
- **Hash the LICENSE too**: plan §2.2 — the MIT `LICENSE` (Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu) is what legally permits redistributing a quantized derivative; the verbatim notice must be embedded in `.focrq` `license_notice`. Freezing its hash proves what notice we are obligated to ship.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 6.67 GB safetensors shard itself is NOT committed (too large); record its SHA-256 from `model.safetensors.index.json` metadata + an out-of-band hash of the downloaded blob (this becomes the `.focrq` `source_sha256`, plan §5.2).
- Line numbers must be recorded against the *exact* snapshot — re-indenting or reformatting the source would invalidate every cited line.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every file above has a recorded SHA-256 + byte length in `docs/truth-pack/SOURCE_HASHES.md`.
- The safetensors blob's SHA-256 is recorded (out-of-band) and matches the future `.focrq` `source_sha256`.
- The snapshots are immutable copies (or git-pinned references) the spec/OQ beads can cite by line.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A CI hash-drift guard: re-hash each pinned source; fail loudly if any hash changes (shared with PM1-census-generator's drift guard).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-pin-sources (you hash at the pinned commit). Blocks PM1-census-generator, PM1-spec-extraction, and ALL OQ beads (every OQ quotes a hashed source by line).

title: OQ-14: Enumerate the EXACT ~2196 NVFP4 linear modules and reconcile vs our ~2229 census
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-14)
Enumerate the EXACT set of ~2196 linear modules that NVFP4 quantizes (dump its scale keys) and reconcile against our ~2229-module census. Plan §2.6: the "2196" does NOT reconcile — our census of quantizable decoder linears is experts 11·64·3=2112 + attention 12·4=48 + shared 11·2·3=66 + dense-layer-0 3 (+ lm_head 1) ≈ 2229-2230, ~33 more than 2196. *"So NVFP4 is NOT quantizing the set we assume — it likely SKIPS attention q/k/v/o and/or lm_head and/or the shared experts."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-NVFP4` tensor/scale listing (the hashed snapshot). Dump every quantized module's scale key and build the exact set. NVFP4 quantizes only linear modules to FP4 (group size 16, NVFP4A16, data-free PTQ), keeping vision tower, projector, embeddings, output layer, MoE router gate, and ALL norms in BF16, ~2.93 GB, and *"Reports OCR output identical to BF16"* (on the author's docs only; no published CER).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The §2.6 recipe SCOPE and, specifically, the validation status of two choices that go BEYOND the validated set (plan §2.6, AGENTS.md doctrine #2): **int8 on attention q/k/v/o** and **int8 on lm_head**. Both are *"explicitly OUR risk to retire by measurement (OQ-14)"*, gated behind a measured-CER kill-switch (`FOCR_INT8_ATTN`, `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`). Knowing whether NVFP4 quantizes attention/lm_head/shared-experts tells us which of our choices are validated-by-prior-art vs novel-risk.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
The core recipe (quantize decoder expert/dense FFN GEMMs; keep vision/projector/embeddings/router/norms high-precision) is converged. But Phase 2 stages quant precisely to isolate the beyond-validated choices (plan §10 Phase 2): 2a FFN/experts only → 2b attention q/k/v/o behind kill-switch → 2c lm_head behind kill-switch. OQ-14 tells us which stages carry prior-art cover.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The exact NVFP4-quantized module set enumerated from its scale keys, reconciled cell-by-cell against the PM1-census-generator quantizable-linear enumeration (account for all ~33 missing).
- An explicit per-class verdict: does NVFP4 quantize attention q/k/v/o? lm_head? shared experts? — marking each of our beyond-validated choices as prior-art-validated or our-CER-risk.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (NVFP4 snapshot) and PM1-census-generator (the ~2229 denominator). Feeds the E-P2 quant staging (2b/2c kill-switches) and the E-P4 int4 tier choices. Records the int8-attention / int8-lm_head validation status that DISCREPANCIES.md tracks.

title: OQ-15: SAM windowed-attention window size (assumed 14) and the windowed-block position-embedding scheme
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-15)
What is the SAM windowed-attention window size (assumed 14) and the windowed-block position-embedding scheme? Plan §2.3.1 flags it [OPEN]: *"window=14 inherited from the SAM/DeepSeek-OCR lineage, NOT config-verified; only global_attn_indexes [2,5,8,11] is config-confirmed."* A wrong window/pos-embed silently corrupts vision features (surfaces only as a fuzzy L2 cosine).

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`deepencoder.py` (line-level). Read the SAM-ViT-B block construction: the literal window size for non-global blocks, how windows partition the 64×64 patch grid, and the windowed-block position-embedding handling (the learnable abs `pos_embed (1,64,64,768)`, bicubic-interpolated for non-1024/tiled inputs). Confirm global attention is only at blocks {2,5,8,11}.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
SAM window attention (`vision_sam.rs`) and the L2 parity gate. Plan §4.3: *"Windowed self-attention (window=14 [OPEN, OQ-15]) — confirm window size + pos-embed scheme from deepencoder.py first."* A wrong window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme silently corrupts vision features (plan §2.3.1).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.1: window attention processes the dense uncompressed tokens; global attention is reserved for the compressed set — keeps activations low for high-res. The window must partition the patch grid exactly as the source does. On the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the literal SAM window size, the partition scheme over the 64×64 grid, and the windowed-block pos-embed handling (including bicubic interpolation for tiled inputs), cited to `deepencoder.py` line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (SAM section) and the E-P1 `vision_sam.rs` windowed attention + the f32 bicubic pos-embed interpolation kernel. On the Phase-1 critical path.

title: OQ-16: Tokenizer parity — exact byte-level-BPE encode/decode vs LlamaTokenizerFast over tokenizer.json
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-16)
Establish exact byte-level-BPE encode/decode parity over `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB, vocab 129280 — the DeepSeek vocab, ~512 special tokens) vs `LlamaTokenizerFast`: the pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token + DeepSeek-glyph handling. AGENTS.md: *"Token-id-exact is an L0/L4 prerequisite (a tokenizer mismatch corrupts every downstream gate)."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`tokenizer.json` + `tokenizer_config.json` (hashed snapshots) and a `LlamaTokenizerFast` round-trip corpus. Read: the BPE merge rules, the pre-tokenizer regex pattern, byte-level mapping, byte-fallback behavior, the special-token set (`<image>`, `<|ref|>`, `<|/ref|>`, `<|det|>`, `<|/det|>`, the DeepSeek glyph specials like `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`), and bos/eos/pad = 0/1/2. `add_special_token=false`, `image_token='<image>'`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The pure-Rust tokenizer (`tokenizer/` module) and the L0/L4 parity gates. Plan §11 tokenizer-mismatch risk (MED): *"A wrong token id corrupts every downstream gate; dedicated tokenizer conformance corpus (OQ-16, §8.3), token-id-exact, frozen golden id sequences, before any decoder parity work."* On the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §8.3: a dedicated exact-match corpus — diverse Unicode, CJK, math, code, the DeepSeek glyph specials, `<image>`/`<|ref|>`/`<|det|>` — round-tripped encode→decode against `LlamaTokenizerFast` over `tokenizer.json`. Freeze the reference id sequences as golden fixtures. Pure-Rust HF-JSON BPE, no SentencePiece (plan §4.1 module layout).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed documentation of the pre-tokenizer regex, byte-level/byte-fallback rules, and the full special-token set, cited to `tokenizer.json`/`tokenizer_config.json`.
- A frozen golden corpus of reference id sequences from `LlamaTokenizerFast` (diverse Unicode/CJK/math/code/specials) committed as fixtures for the tokenizer conformance test.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (tokenizer.json snapshot) and PM1-oracle-strategy (the `LlamaTokenizerFast` reference run). Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (tokenizer section) and the E-P1 `tokenizer/` module + its conformance-test bead. On the Phase-1 critical path — a prerequisite for EVERY downstream gate.

title: OQ-17: Does the official model run on CPU at all? (infer() is CUDA-oriented) — decides the oracle split + G2
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-17)
Does the official model run on CPU at all? `infer()` is CUDA-oriented (`.cuda()` + autocast). Determine whether a CPU-patched HF run reproduces the GPU oracle's tokens — if not, perf is benchmarked against llama.cpp-GGUF / ONNX on CPU instead. This decides the oracle split (§8.1) and the meaning of G2.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` `infer()` — read the exact device placement (`.cuda()`) and autocast dtype lines. Then run a CPU smoke test (force tensors to CPU, disable autocast) and check whether it produces the same tokens as the GPU oracle within the nondeterminism floor.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The §8.1 oracle strategy, the G2 perf claim, and the entire Phase -1 oracle deliverable. Plan §8.1: golden fixtures come from the unmodified model on a CUDA host (correctness never depends on CPU HF); the CPU baseline is separate — CPU-patched HF (if PROVEN to reproduce GPU tokens) else llama.cpp/ONNX/MLAS labeled as such. G2's *"beats the CPU reference"* is only honest against whatever CPU reference is proven to run.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §11 ("End-to-end may not beat the CPU baseline"): *"first prove which CPU baseline is legitimate (CPU-patched HF, llama.cpp, or ONNX)."* This question literally defines what G2 means and what the gauntlet (§9.3) shells out to.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the `infer()` device/autocast lines and a yes/no on whether CPU-patched HF reproduces GPU tokens within the floor.
- The resulting oracle split decision recorded (this feeds PM1-oracle-strategy, which captures the smoke fixture).
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (the modeling source) and the pinned runtime (PM1-pin-sources). Directly drives PM1-oracle-strategy. Blocks E-VERIFY (the oracle) and the E-P3 perf gauntlet (the CPU baseline).

title: OQ-18: Full token census per mode (image-feature/newline/separator/prompt/generated) + ngram_window per mode
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-18)
The full token census per mode — every token CLASS (image-feature 256, `image_newline` 16, `view_seperator` 1 ⇒ 273 at 1024; prompt; generated), the exact Gundam multi-tile totals per aspect ratio, and the `ngram_window` per mode (128 single-image / 1024 multi-image). Drives all KV/buffer sizing + the sampler.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` — `dynamic_preprocess`, the placeholder-assembly code that builds the image-token structure, and the ngram-window selection (`SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`). Plan §2.4: the Base-1024 placeholder structure is `(16 + 1)·16 + 1 = 273` (16 image tokens + 1 `image_newline` per row, × 16 rows, + 1 `view_seperator`). The decoder context = image-feature + image-newline + view-separator + prompt + generated.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Preprocess (`tile.rs`), R-SWA buffer sizing (the reference block `m`), the sampler (`decode.rs` — the no_repeat_ngram window per mode), AND the census generator itself (PM1-census-generator consumes this answer). Plan §2.4: *"the R-SWA reference block m and all KV/buffer sizing must account for every class, not the 256 compression figure ... A machine-readable token/shape/buffer census (§8.6, generated from the pinned config) is the source of truth."*

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
AGENTS.md doctrine #7: *"base mode is 256 image-feature tokens plus structural/prompt tokens — 273 token slots at 1024 before prompt."* Plan §2.4: `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with a sliding `ngram_window` that DIFFERS by mode — 128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image) — *"these are first-class generation semantics, not just CLI flags ... The sampler must replicate the exact window per mode."* Getting the token-class census wrong mis-sizes every buffer; getting the ngram window wrong changes decoded output.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the per-mode token-class breakdown (confirm Base-1024 = 273 slots `(16+1)·16+1`), the exact Gundam multi-tile totals per aspect ratio, and the `ngram_window` per mode (128 single / 1024 multi), cited to the placeholder-assembly + ngram-window line ranges.
- These values feed PM1-census-generator to populate the token-class and ngram cells.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-census-generator (the source of truth) and PM1-spec-extraction. Coupled to OQ-7 (Gundam tile bounds). Blocks the E-P1 preprocess, R-SWA buffer sizing, and the sampler. On the Phase-1 critical path.

title: Build the CI-guarded machine-readable token/shape/buffer census generator
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The decoder context, R-SWA reference-block size `m`, all KV/ring-buffer allocations, the MoE token-group buffers, and the sampler's ngram window are all sized from a token/shape census that MUST be derived from the pinned config — not hand-typed. Plan §2.4: *"A machine-readable token/shape/buffer census (§8.6, generated from the pinned config) is the source of truth and CI fails if the source/config changes."* §8.6 ties it to the spec. AGENTS.md doctrine #7: the reference block `m` grows ~256 image-feature tokens per page plus structural/prompt tokens — 273 token slots at 1024 before prompt — so a wrong census silently mis-sizes every buffer.

## WHAT TO DO
Write a generator (a `focr` subcommand or a script) that reads the pinned `config.json` + `model.safetensors.index.json` and emits a machine-readable census (`docs/truth-pack/CENSUS.json` + a human `CENSUS.md`) covering:
1. **Decoder shapes** (VERIFIED from config, §2.3.2): hidden_size 1280, num_hidden_layers 12, num_attention_heads 10, num_key_value_heads 10 (no GQA), intermediate_size (dense layer-0) 6848, moe_intermediate_size (per expert) 896, n_routed_experts 64, n_shared_experts 2, num_experts_per_tok 6 (top-6 greedy), first_k_dense_replace 1 (layer 0 dense, layers 1-11 MoE), v_head_dim 128, use_mla false, max_position_embeddings 32768, vocab_size 129280, sliding_window/sliding_window_size 128.
2. **Token-class census per mode** (the OQ-18 deliverable): image-feature tokens, `image_newline` tokens, `view_seperator` tokens. Base mode 1024 view = 256 image-feature (16×16) + structural ⇒ `(16+1)·16 + 1 = 273` slots; plus prompt + generated. Gundam multi-tile totals per aspect ratio (each 640 crop ≈ 100 tokens) — populated from OQ-18's resolved answer.
3. **KV/buffer sizing**: per-layer R-SWA reference block worst case `m ≈ 32768 − 128`; the 128-wide generated-token ring per layer; the constant-KV formula `C_RSWA(T) = L·(m + min(n,T)) ≤ L·(m+n)`, L=12, n=128.
4. **Tensor census**: all 2710 tensors with (name, shape, dtype) cross-checked vs `model.safetensors.index.json` (total_size 6,672,212,480). This IS the WeightsManifest the converter validates against (plan §5.1 step 2).
5. **i32-overflow worst case** (plan §5.4, AGENTS.md doctrine #6): dense layer-0 `down_proj` K=6848 ⇒ S8S8 ≤ 110.4M, U8S8 ≤ 221.7M; the census records the worst-case K per GEMM class so the overflow proof test has a generated source of truth.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Generated, not typed**: a census drift between code and config is the silent-corruption class this guard exists to kill.
- **CI-guarded**: CI fails if the pinned source/config changes (re-hash + regenerate + diff). This is plan §2.4's hard requirement.
- **Count every token CLASS, not the 256 compression figure** (§2.4, OQ-18): R-SWA `m` and KV sizing depend on it.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Some token-class counts (Gundam per-aspect-ratio totals, ngram window per mode) are OPEN until OQ-18 / OQ-7 are resolved — the generator must consume their resolved values, so this bead depends on those OQs for full population (the config-only part can land first; the Gundam/ngram cells are filled when OQ-7/OQ-18 close).
- The `~2196 vs ~2229` NVFP4 module-count discrepancy (OQ-14) is reconciled against THIS census's quantizable-linear count: experts 11·64·3=2112 + attention 12·4=48 + shared 11·2·3=66 + dense-layer-0 3 (+ lm_head 1) ≈ 2229-2230. The census must enumerate exactly these so OQ-14 has a denominator.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `CENSUS.json` is generated purely from the pinned config + index (no hand-typed shapes).
- The tensor census matches all 2710 tensors and total_size 6,672,212,480 from `model.safetensors.index.json`.
- A CI guard regenerates the census and fails on any diff vs the committed census (source-drift detection).
- The quantizable-linear enumeration (for OQ-14) and the worst-case K table (for the overflow proof) are present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- `census_matches_index`: assert generated tensor (name, shape) set == `model.safetensors.index.json` set, count == 2710, total_size == 6,672,212,480.
- `census_drift_guard`: regenerate from pinned source, assert byte-identical to committed `CENSUS.json` (fails on source change).
- `census_token_classes`: assert Base-1024 placeholder structure == 273 slots `(16+1)·16+1` once OQ-18 is resolved.
- `census_overflow_worstcase`: assert the worst-case K table includes dense layer-0 down_proj K=6848.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (reads pinned config + index). Depends on OQ-18 and OQ-7 to populate Gundam/ngram/token-class cells. Depends on OQ-14 to confirm the quantizable-linear denominator. Blocks Phase-1 buffer-sizing and the WeightsManifest census the converter uses (E-P2).

title: Extract EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md (THE SPEC) verbatim from the pinned model source
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Spec-first porting (`/porting-to-rust`, plan §8.6): *"extract spec → implement from spec → never translate line-by-line."* The four documents are PLAN_TO_PORT (the master plan), **EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md (THE SPEC — this bead produces it)**, PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md. Plan §10 Phase 1 says this is extracted FIRST, *"resolving every kernel-blocking [OPEN]/OQ (§13)"*, and then kernels are implemented from the spec, not from the Python. This is the deliverable that makes the §13 OPENs resolvable into design assumptions.

## WHAT TO DO
Produce `docs/truth-pack/EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` extracting verbatim (with cited file + line range against the hashed snapshots) from the pinned `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`, `modeling_deepseekv2.py`, `deepencoder.py`, `conversation.py`, `configuration_deepseek_v2.py`:
1. **Every data structure** and its exact fields/shapes (decoder config, vision config, projector config).
2. **The exact preprocessing/tiling rules**: `image_mean=image_std=[0.5,0.5,0.5]` → [-1,1]; gray pad (127,127,127) via `ImageOps.pad`; Base (1024×1024, crop_mode=false) vs Gundam (base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=true, global_view_pos='head', tile_tag='2D', candidate_resolutions=[[1024,1024]]); `dynamic_preprocess()` / `find_closest_aspect_ratio()` over `i*j ∈ [min_num,max_num]`.
3. **`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` semantics** (the R-SWA centerpiece): ring-buffer width W=128, `prefill_len` recorded on first decode, warm-up until cache reaches `prefill_len+W`, in-place ring overwrite at `slot = prefill_len + ring_pos`, `ring_pos=(ring_pos+1)%W`; the reference set `m` (visual / visual+prompt); whether attention is uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers.
4. **The projector concat order** producing the 2048-dim input (SAM-1024 ⊕ CLIP-1024 vs interleaved; `low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu` / `hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu` variants) → linear 2048→1280.
5. **Tokenizer rules**: byte-level BPE over `tokenizer.json`, special tokens (`<image>`, `<|ref|>`, `<|det|>`, DeepSeek glyph specials, bos/eos/pad = 0/1/2).
6. **Generation semantics**: `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`, ngram_window 128 (single-image) / 1024 (multi-image); EOS strip `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`; greedy/low-temp argmax; the `infer(...)` and `infer_multi(...)` signatures.
7. **The masked-scatter fusion** (`inputs_embeds.masked_scatter_(images_seq_mask, vision_embeds)`) and structural-token insertion (`model.image_newline`, `model.view_seperator`).

Mark each clause MUST/SHOULD/MAY (this becomes the §8.6 conformance coverage matrix — ≥0.95 MUST coverage to claim conformance).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Verbatim + cited, not paraphrased**: the spec is the authority that resolves the OPENs; paraphrase loses load-bearing detail (e.g. exact ring slot arithmetic).
- **MUST/SHOULD/MAY tagging**: feeds `/testing-conformance-harnesses` ConformanceTest `requirement_level` and the coverage-accounting matrix (§8.6).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The spec extraction is what ACTUALLY answers OQ-1..OQ-3 (R-SWA), OQ-6 (projector concat), OQ-8 (prompt modes), OQ-13 (multi-page span), OQ-15 (SAM window), OQ-16 (tokenizer), OQ-18 (token census). Those OQ beads should cite back into this document. Treat this bead and those OQs as co-produced: the OQ answers ARE sections of this spec.
- `view_seperator` is misspelled in the source (sic) — preserve the exact tensor name.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` covers every structure/rule above, each with a (file_sha256, line range) citation into a hashed snapshot.
- Every MUST/SHOULD/MAY clause is tagged (the conformance coverage matrix denominator).
- The kernel-blocking OQs (OQ-1/2/3/6/13/15/16/18) each have their answer present as a cited section.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A link-check: every citation references a real, in-range line of a file whose SHA-256 is in `SOURCE_HASHES.md`.
- A coverage check: the MUST-clause set is enumerated so E-VERIFY can compute ≥0.95 MUST coverage.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (cites hashed snapshots). Co-produces / depends on OQ-1, OQ-2, OQ-3, OQ-6, OQ-8, OQ-13, OQ-15, OQ-16, OQ-18 (their answers are sections of this spec). Blocks Phase-1 (E-P1) which implements from this spec, not from the Python.

title: Decide the oracle strategy (CPU-vs-GPU split) and capture one smoke fixture
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The whole parity ladder (L0-L5) and the perf claim G2 hinge on what reference we compare against. Plan §8.1 warns: *"The official infer() path is CUDA-oriented (.cuda() + CUDA autocast), so a CPU bf16 HF oracle is NOT guaranteed to run as-is (OQ-17)."* The resolution (plan §8.1) is to SPLIT the oracle: **(correctness)** golden fixtures come from the unmodified official model on a CUDA host, frozen once and committed — parity NEVER depends on CPU HF; **(performance)** the CPU baseline is separate — either a CPU-patched HF run (force tensors to CPU, disable autocast) that is PROVEN to reproduce the GPU oracle's tokens within the nondeterminism floor (§8.2), or, if CPU HF is infeasible, the perf claim is stated against the best CPU reference that actually runs (llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX Runtime / MLAS) and labeled as such. This bead IS the OQ-17 decision plus the Phase -1 smoke fixture.

## WHAT TO DO
1. Read `modeling_unlimitedocr.py` `infer()` — record the exact device/autocast lines (`.cuda()`, autocast dtype). (This is the OQ-17 source read.)
2. Attempt a CPU-patched HF run (force CPU, disable autocast) of `model.infer()` on one golden image and determine whether it reproduces the GPU oracle's token sequence within the oracle nondeterminism floor.
3. **Establish the oracle's OWN nondeterminism floor FIRST** (plan §8.2): run the oracle twice and at two thread counts over a small corpus; record the per-token divergence rate + first-divergence position as a committed fixture (the bf16 129280-vocab argmax is tie-prone across BLAS reduction order). L4 "exact" is defined only over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically; the L3 logit tolerance is DERIVED from this measured variance, NOT from the imported frankensearch `0.055`.
4. Decide and record in `docs/truth-pack/ORACLE_STRATEGY.md`: which path is correctness oracle (frozen GPU fixtures), which is perf baseline (CPU-patched HF if proven, else llama.cpp/ONNX/MLAS labeled), and the resulting meaning of G2.
5. Capture one smoke fixture: per-layer activation dumps (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector, per-decoder-layer hidden states, pre-lm_head, logits) + end-to-end decoded text for one golden image, with a `PROVENANCE.md` (`transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, git ref, exact command — plan §8.6).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Correctness fixtures are GPU-frozen and committed** — parity must never depend on a CPU HF run that may not exist (§8.1).
- **Nondeterminism floor measured before tolerances** (§8.2): a franken_ocr int8 divergence inside the oracle's own bf16 noise is NOT a bug; setting tolerances before measuring the floor would either over- or under-gate.
- **G2 honesty** (§1.1, §11): G2's "beats the CPU reference" is only honest against whatever CPU reference is PROVEN to run; this decision fixes that.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If CPU-patched HF cannot reproduce GPU tokens, the gauntlet (§9.3) targets llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX / MLAS and labels every ratio with the precision (focr-int8 vs torch-bf16 vs torch-int8) — a raw cross-precision ratio is meaningless (§9.3 precision annotation rule).
- The community GGUF requires llama.cpp PR #17400 (not upstream) — if used as the CPU perf reference, record that dependency.
- Use `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)` + seeded RNG for the gauntlet oracle bridge (§8.5), but the *correctness* fixtures capture the production (non-deterministic) path so the floor is real.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `ORACLE_STRATEGY.md` records the OQ-17 decision (CPU HF runs/reproduces-or-not) with the cited `infer()` device/autocast lines.
- The oracle nondeterminism floor is measured and committed (per-token divergence rate, first-divergence position) — the source of the L3/L4 tolerances.
- One smoke fixture (per-layer + end-to-end) is captured with full `PROVENANCE.md`.
- The perf-baseline choice (CPU-patched HF vs llama.cpp/ONNX) is recorded with its proof-of-equivalence (or its labeled-as-such caveat).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A fixture-provenance check: the smoke fixture carries `PROVENANCE.md` with the pinned runtime + git ref + exact command.
- A determinism-floor sanity test: re-loading the committed floor fixture and asserting the recorded divergence envelope is well-formed (used by E-VERIFY to derive L3/L4 tolerances).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-pin-sources (uses the pinned runtime) and PM1-hash-sources (cites the hashed `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`). IS the resolution of OQ-17 (depends_on OQ-17 read). Blocks E-VERIFY (the parity ladder uses these fixtures + floor) and the perf gauntlet (E-P3) which targets the chosen CPU baseline.

title: Build the claim_id → source-line index tying every truth-pack assertion to a hashed source line
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §10 Phase -1 requires everything *"under docs/truth-pack/ with a claim_id → source-line index."* This index is what makes the truth pack auditable: every [VERIFIED] fact, every OQ answer, and every spec clause maps to (file_sha256, line range, quoted text). It is also the spine of the artifact-graph ledger (§8.4): NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md / DISCREPANCIES.md entries carry `claim_id`/`evidence_id` fields that resolve into this index.

## WHAT TO DO
Create `docs/truth-pack/CLAIM_INDEX.json` (+ a rendered `CLAIM_INDEX.md`) mapping each `claim_id` to: the source file path, its SHA-256 (from `SOURCE_HASHES.md`), the line range, the verbatim quoted text, and the [VERIFIED]/[REPORTED]/[OPEN] tag. Seed it with: all §2.2 identity/format/license facts; all §2.3.2 decoder config values; the R-SWA §2.3.3 mechanism; the §2.4 input/output contract; and a `claim_id` for each OQ-1..OQ-18 answer (each pointing at the line resolved by the corresponding OQ bead). Assign stable `claim_id`s (e.g. `CL-CONFIG-HIDDEN-1280`, `CL-RSWA-RING-W128`, `CL-OQ16-TOKENIZER`).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Stable claim_ids**: ledger entries (§8.4) reference them; renaming breaks provenance, so freeze the scheme.
- **Tag preserved**: [OPEN] claims start unresolved and flip to [VERIFIED] when their OQ closes — the index is the single status board for "is this still open?"

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A `claim_id` whose tag is still [OPEN] must block any kernel bead depending on it (the hard rule); the index is queried to enforce that gate.
- Every quote must be re-checkable against the hashed snapshot (line numbers are against the frozen file).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `CLAIM_INDEX.json` exists with one entry per VERIFIED fact and one per OQ answer, each with (file_sha256, line range, quote, tag).
- A query "list all still-OPEN claim_ids" returns empty at Phase -1 exit (every OQ resolved).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- `claim_index_links_valid`: every entry's (file_sha256, line range) resolves to real text in a hashed snapshot.
- `no_open_claims_at_exit`: assert no [OPEN]-tagged claim remains (the Phase -1 green gate).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (line citations), PM1-spec-extraction (spec clauses), and ALL OQ beads (each contributes one resolved claim_id). This is the aggregation point proving every OQ is answered with a pinned-source citation (the top E-PM1 exit gate).

title: OQ-1: R-SWA reference set m — visual-only or visual+prompt? Exact mask boundary
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-1)
R-SWA reference set `m`: does it include the running **prompt** tokens, or strictly the **visual** tokens? And what is the exact mask boundary between prompt vs generated tokens? Plan §2.3.3 notes the conflict: *"Paper says 'all reference tokens (visual tokens in OCR)' but Figure 2 caption says 'visual+prompt'."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` in `modeling_deepseekv2.py` (the hashed snapshot from PM1-hash-sources). Read how the reference (never-evicted) KV block is constructed: does it span only the vision-token positions, or vision+prompt-prefix positions? Determine the exact index boundary where the sliding 128-window begins.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The R-SWA attention kernel (`rswa.rs`) and prefill (building the reference KV block). The attention mask cannot be finalized without this — a wrong `m` boundary attends to the wrong tokens and silently corrupts decode (surfaces only as an L2/L4 parity drift). Plan §6.4: *"resolve the R-SWA boundary questions (§2.3.3 / OQ-1..3 and the multi-page span OQ-13) from SlidingWindowLlamaAttention before finalizing the mask."*

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
R-SWA is the one architectural novelty (plan §2.3.3): `o_t = Σ_{j∈N(t)} α_tj v_j` where `N(t) = {all reference tokens} ∪ {last n=128 generated tokens}`. The reference tokens are a frozen global KV — never evicted, excluded from state transitions. Whether `m` = visual-only or visual+prompt changes the reference-block length used for KV/buffer sizing (PM1-census-generator) and per-token attention compute `O(m+128)`.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A quoted, line-backed answer in EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md + CLAIM_INDEX.json: `m` = visual-only OR visual+prompt, with the exact reference-block index span and the sliding-window start boundary, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` line range.
- The [OPEN] tag for the R-SWA reference set flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (cites the hashed `modeling_deepseekv2.py`). Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (R-SWA section), PM1-census-generator (m sizing), and the future E-P1 `rswa.rs` kernel. Closely coupled to OQ-3 (warm-up/ring semantics) and OQ-13 (multi-page span).

title: OQ-2: Is attention uniformly R-SWA across all 12 decoder layers (incl. layer 0)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-2)
Is attention uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers (including layer 0)? Is there any layer that retains full (non-sliding) attention? Plan §2.3.3 hypothesis: *"attention is expected R-SWA everywhere, including layer 0 — the first_k_dense_replace=1 split affects only the MLP, not attention."* Confirm this from source.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_deepseekv2.py` — the decoder layer construction loop. Verify whether every one of the 12 layers instantiates `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` (vs some layers using a full-attention class), and confirm that `first_k_dense_replace=1` (layer 0 dense MLP) does NOT also make layer-0 attention full.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The decoder attention dispatch (`decoder.rs` 12-layer loop). If even one layer is full-attention, the R-SWA constant-KV property and the per-layer ring-buffer allocation differ for that layer — a wrong assumption breaks both correctness and the KV-bound invariant the gauntlet monitors (§8.5: *"KV cache never exceeds L·(m+128)"*).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.3: R-SWA *"replaces ALL decoder attention (every one of the 12 layers)."* The KV formula `C_RSWA(T)=L·(m+min(n,T))` uses L=12 uniformly; if a layer is exempt, L is effectively smaller for the ring and the buffer math changes.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: all 12 layers are R-SWA (or the explicit list of any exceptions), cited to the layer-construction line range.
- Confirmation that `first_k_dense_replace=1` affects only the MLP, not attention.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction and the E-P1 decoder loop. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-3.

title: OQ-3: Exact warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during the first 128 decode steps
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-3)
What are the exact warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during the first 128 decode steps? Plan §2.3.3 reports the mechanism: *"records prefill_len on first decode, warms up until cache reaches prefill_len+W, then in-place ring overwrites at slot = prefill_len + ring_pos, ring_pos = (ring_pos+1) % W"* (W=128). Confirm and pin the exact arithmetic and the mask during warm-up.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` in `modeling_deepseekv2.py`. Read: how `prefill_len` is recorded; the warm-up condition (cache length < prefill_len+W); the in-place overwrite slot formula; the `ring_pos` update; and crucially the attention MASK applied during the first 128 steps (does a partially-filled ring mask out unfilled slots, or attend to stale data?).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The R-SWA ring-buffer KV cache implementation (`rswa.rs`) — the single most important kernel implication (plan §2.3.3: *"This is the single most important kernel implication."*). Getting the warm-up mask wrong attends to garbage ring slots before the ring fills, corrupting the first ≤128 generated tokens.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.4: *"Write the new K,V into the ring at slot = prefill_len + (t % 128) (after warm-up)."* The pre-warm-up behavior (steps 0..127, ring not yet full) is exactly where a mask bug hides — it only manifests on the early output tokens and is easy to miss in aggregate CER.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: exact `prefill_len` recording, warm-up condition, slot formula, `ring_pos` update, and the warm-up-phase mask semantics, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (the SlidingWindowLlamaAttention section) and the E-P1 `rswa.rs` ring buffer. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-2.

title: End-to-end CLI test script: focr ocr <golden image> --json → compare to frozen reference, full structured logging
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the headline e2e the user asked for: an end-to-end CLI test that runs the REAL `focr` binary on a golden image, emits `--json`, and compares against a frozen reference — with full detailed logging at every stage. It is the integration test that proves the whole pipeline (decode→preprocess→tokenize→vision→connector→prefill→decode→postprocess→emit) actually works end-to-end on the native path, not just per-op parity. No mocks: real binary, real golden image, real frozen reference JSON (per `/testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks`).

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` (the Rust e2e, model-gated) AND `scripts/run_e2e.sh` (the standalone CLI script):
1. **`scripts/run_e2e.sh`**: resolves the model (env `FOCR_MODEL_DIR`), runs `focr ocr tests/fixtures/golden_corpus/<doc>.png --json` for each golden doc, pipes output to a comparator that diffs against `tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json` after canonicalization (scrub timing/run-id/durations, sort bbox, normalize line endings). Emits a structured NDJSON summary per doc and a final pass/fail. Exit 0 on all-pass; non-zero on any diff. Skips-green (exit 0, logged) if no model resolvable.
2. **`tests/native_engine_e2e.rs`**: the same flow as a Rust test using TEST-model-gated-runner — invokes the real binary or `OcrEngine::recognize`, asserts `native_path_ran=true`, compares to the frozen reference (text exact where the bf16 oracle is deterministic over its reproduced prefix; logits/continuous values within the E-VERIFY documented tolerance), and asserts determinism (same image+args twice → byte-identical under greedy).
3. **Full per-stage logging**: at EACH pipeline stage emit a `TestLog` `stage` line with inputs/shapes/dtype/simd_tier/elapsed_us — e.g. preprocess logs the tile geometry (Base 1024×1024 vs Gundam) and the [-1,1] normalize; tokenize logs token count; vision logs the 256 image-feature tokens + the 273 placeholder-token total (16 image + 1 image_newline per row × 16 rows + 1 view_seperator) at base 1024 (OQ-18 census); prefill logs reference-block length `m`; decode logs per-N-tokens TPS and the no_repeat_ngram(35) window (128 single-image / 1024 multi-image); postprocess logs EOS strip + bbox /999 rescale.
4. **Cover both modes**: Base (1024×1024, crop_mode=false, multi-page) and Gundam (base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=true, single-page tiling) — each against its own frozen reference.
5. **Multi-page e2e**: a 10-page golden, comparing to the multi-page reference (which is cross-page DEPENDENT under R-SWA, OQ-13 — the reference is a single `infer_multi` oracle run, NOT a concatenation of single-page parses).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Real binary, real fixtures, no mocks** — an e2e that stubs the engine proves nothing.
- **`--json` is the compared surface** (stable, structured) — human markdown is a rendering of it.
- **Per-stage logging makes a failure diagnosable**: if the e2e diffs, the structured log pinpoints WHICH stage's shapes/tokens diverged, not just "output differs."
- **Both modes + multi-page** because the cost center and the token census differ (Base = 256 tokens/page; Gundam crops ≈ 100 tokens each; multi-page is cross-page dependent).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Determinism only holds under `temperature=0` greedy** (§7.3) — assert byte-identical only in that mode.
- **L4 'exact' is defined only over the prefix the bf16 oracle reproduces identically** (§8.2) — compare text exactly only up to the nondeterminism-envelope boundary carried in the fixture; beyond it, fall back to CER-within-budget.
- **Do NOT assert multi-page == sum of single-page** (§8.3, OQ-13) — the metamorphic property is the opposite (page order may change later-page output).
- Robot/JSON output may carry run-id/timing → scrub before compare (canonicalization from TEST-fixture-harness).
- Script must work both in CI (model absent → skip-green) and locally (model present → real compare).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` runs the real `focr` over each golden, compares to frozen reference, exits 0 all-pass / non-zero on diff, skips-green without a model.
- `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` asserts `native_path_ran=true`, frozen-reference match (text exact over the deterministic prefix; continuous within tolerance), and determinism.
- Both Base and Gundam modes covered; a 10-page multi-page e2e present.
- Every stage emits a valid structured NDJSON log line; a failing run produces a log that localizes the diverging stage.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The e2e itself is the test; additionally a self-test that a deliberately-perturbed reference makes the comparator FAIL (proves it actually compares).
- A self-test that the script skips-green without a model.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-model-gated-runner**: uses it to gate + prove the native path ran.
- depends_on **TEST-fixture-harness**: loads the frozen `<doc>_reference.json` + canonicalizes before compare.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the per-stage structured logs.
notes: 2026-06-25 Codex correction: any phrase like 'Base = 256 tokens/page' in this generated bead means 256 image-feature tokens only. For token/KV sizing use the OQ-18 census: base 1024 has 256 image-feature tokens plus structural slots (16 image_newline + 1 view_seperator = 273 before prompt), and the R-SWA reference block grows with page/input length up to the 32K cap. Do not size buffers from 256 alone.

title: Per-module unit-test scaffolding standard (happy/edge/error + structured logging) every module author follows
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AGENTS.md Testing Policy: "Each module includes unit tests for happy path, edge cases, error handling." Without a documented, copy-pasteable standard, module authors (preprocess, tokenizer, vision_sam, vision_clip, decoder, rswa, moe, decode, postprocess, weights, robot, storage, etc.) each invent their own shape, logging drifts, and the unit-test floor erodes. This bead defines the ONE scaffolding standard + a template so every per-module test suite is uniform, richly logged, and reviewable.

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver `docs/UNIT_TEST_STANDARD.md` + a `tests/_template_module_tests.rs.txt` template:
1. **Three mandatory groups per module**: `happy_*` (nominal path on representative input), `edge_*` (boundary: empty input, max dims, the i32-overflow worst case at K=6848 for int8 GEMM modules, single-token decode, 40-page context), `error_*` (malformed input, missing weight, shape mismatch, format/version mismatch → asserts the right `FocrError` + exit-code mapping per §7.4).
2. **Structured logging in EVERY test** via `TestLog` (bead TEST-log-emitter): log `setup`, each `stage` with shapes/dtype/simd_tier/elapsed, and `result`.
3. **Determinism assertion** where applicable: same input twice → identical output (uses the shared helper from TEST-determinism-gate).
4. **SIMD-tier coverage note**: kernel modules must run their tests across available tiers (force via `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH`) and assert the scalar fallback is bit-identical to the active SIMD path (except AVX2, which carries its own overflow proof — see §6.6 ⚠).
5. **The overflow proof obligation** (AGENTS.md doctrine #6, §5.4): any module doing int8 i32-accumulation includes a test multiplying worst-case saturated operands at **K = 6848** (dense layer-0 `down_proj`) on every arch, asserting the i32 result equals an i64 reference and no path silently saturates. (Worst cases: S8S8 ≤ 110.4M, U8S8 ≤ 221.7M at K=6848; ≥9× headroom under i32::MAX=2,147,483,647 — but PROVEN, not assumed. Do NOT inherit frankensearch's k≤1536 bound.)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **A template, not just prose**, because authors copy-paste; the path of least resistance must be the correct shape.
- **Overflow proof is a UNIT-test obligation in the standard** so no int8 module ships without it (the proof is per-module, the standard makes it mandatory).
- **SIMD-tier coverage in the standard** so bit-identity (integer accumulation is exact/associative → SIMD == scalar exactly) is verified everywhere, and the AVX2 i16-saturation exception is explicitly called out as needing its own proof or a `DISCREPANCIES.md` entry.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The standard must not mandate the 6.67 GB model for unit tests — unit tests use tiny synthetic tensors; the model-gated path is for e2e only.
- `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` forcing must degrade gracefully on a host lacking an intrinsic (the scalar fallback always cross-compiles and runs).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/UNIT_TEST_STANDARD.md` + template exist; the three groups + logging + determinism + SIMD-tier + overflow-proof obligations are documented with concrete examples.
- At least one module (e.g. the int8 linear facade in `nn.rs`) ships a reference implementation of the standard as the worked example.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The worked-example module's test suite passes and emits valid structured logs (validated against TEST-log-conventions).
- The K=6848 overflow proof passes on every available arch in the worked example.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: the standard mandates logging via that emitter.

title: Phase 4 — int4 group-quant refinement (the biggest decode bandwidth lever)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 4 is the int4 refinement round of franken_ocr — the pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for Baidu Unlimited-OCR (a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA, hidden 1280, 12 decoder layers, 64 routed + 2 shared experts, top-6, moe_intermediate 896, vocab 129280, MIT-licensed). Decode is **memory-bandwidth-bound**: a single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token. Per the plan §6.3, **int4 group-quantized weights (g=16-32) halve the bytes streamed per token vs int8 → up to ~2x decode throughput**; applied to the dominant **expert FFN weights** (the bulk of the 3B params AND the decode hot path) this is **the single biggest decode lever** and the headline CPU-performance strategy of the whole project. int8 (Phase 2) ships first as the ~lossless correctness oracle; int4 (this phase) pushes quantization as far as a *measured* CER bound allows.

The over-arching goals this serves: max CPU speed for THIS one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels; **correctness before speed (G1 > G2, always)**; portable single binary; agent-ergonomic. int4's win is *bandwidth + footprint*, not extra compute.

## WHAT TO DO (the epic scope)
Deliver, as child tasks:
1. `focr convert --quant int4` — per-group symmetric int4 (group size 16-32; NVFP4 used 16), attention `v_proj` and expert `down_proj` kept one tier higher (int8), Q4_K_M-equivalent ~4.5-4.9 bpw. Emits int4 tensor records into `.focrq`.
2. **int4 GEMM/GEMV kernels** — int4 weights **unpack to int8 in-register** and feed the SAME SMMLA / VNNI / SDOT MAC built in Phase 3 (there is NO CPU int4 matmul instruction; the win is the halved weight stream). Per-arch unpack + dispatch.
3. **AF-1 rate-distortion / water-filling per-tensor bit allocator** — `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>` emitting the `bit_allocation_table` in the `.focrq` header from measured per-tensor distortion curves D_t(b).
4. **Expected-loss-guided per-layer quant** — choose tier (int8/int4-g32/int4-g16) per layer/tensor by measured CER impact, not uniform.
5. **Dense-numeric/table/code exact-token-sensitive accuracy corpus + CER curve per bit-width** (tables/code/numbers/sub-superscripts).
6. **Footprint arithmetic demonstrated** — the converter prints realized per-section bytes; the target is a measured number, not assumed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **int4 only on the expert/FFN bulk; sensitive minority stays int8.** `v_proj` and expert `down_proj` kept one tier higher (the llama.cpp `_M` discipline, derived not guessed). `lm_head` int8 only behind its `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` kill-switch. Router gate, ALL norms, vision tower, projector, embed_tokens stay BF16/F32 — quantizing them drifts expert selection / wrecks OCR (both prior-art quants keep them).
