- A test that recomputes the SHA-256 of each committed `.npy` and asserts it matches the recorded value (catches accidental fixture corruption / mismatched commit).
- A reproducibility check: given the seeded RNG capture, re-running the deterministic-prefix portion (where a CUDA host is available; skip-with-SUCCESS otherwise) yields byte-identical tokens for that prefix.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS when neither weights nor a CUDA host is present, so CI stays green without the 6.67 GB weights.
- Structured JSON-line logging on every assertion (`{check, fixture, expected_sha, actual_sha, result}`).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All committed fixtures pass provenance + SHA checks.
- Test is green in CI without the model (skip-with-SUCCESS) and green with it.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. It must log per-fixture structured results.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the thing it validates).

title: Metamorphic test suite — oracle-free invariants (resize/rotation/whitespace/determinism); multi-page CROSS-PAGE dependence (OQ-13)
description:
# Metamorphic test suite

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Metamorphic testing (`/testing-metamorphic`) proves properties that must hold WITHOUT an oracle — invaluable for inputs not in the golden corpus and for catching bugs the oracle also has. It complements differential testing: differential checks 'same as reference', metamorphic checks 'self-consistent under transformations.'

## WHAT TO DO
Implement metamorphic relations that MUST hold:
- **Identity-resize invariance**: resizing to the same size is a no-op on recognized text.
- **90°-rotation / transpose relationships on bbox coords**: rotating the page transforms bboxes predictably.
- **Whitespace-padding invariance**: padding a page with whitespace must NOT change recognized text.
- **Base-mode determinism across runs**: same image → same parse.

**⚠️ CRITICAL — multi-page (OQ-13):** Do **NOT** assert 'multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses.' Under R-SWA the multi-page decode is **cross-page DEPENDENT**: in a single 32K pass, ALL pages' visual+prompt prefixes form one frozen reference block, so page N can attend to pages 1..N-1. The output is NOT a concatenation of independent single-page parses. The defensible metamorphic property is closer to the OPPOSITE: **changing page order / earlier-page content MAY change later-page output.** Lock the precise property only after OQ-13 confirms the reference-block span from `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` / `infer_multi`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Metamorphic relations are derived from the model's documented semantics (bbox /999 normalized coords, [-1,1] normalize, R-SWA reference block).
- The multi-page relation is deliberately phrased as a dependence-existence property, not an independence property, to avoid encoding a falsehood (the most dangerous metamorphic-test failure mode here).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Whitespace-padding invariance assumes the pad gray (127) is outside the recognized content — verify against the actual pad color.
- The multi-page property is GATED on OQ-13; until resolved, only the single-page metamorphic relations are asserted.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Identity-resize, rotation/transpose bbox, whitespace-pad, and Base-mode determinism relations all hold over a generated input set.
- The multi-page relation (post-OQ-13) asserts cross-page dependence, NOT independence.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- One metamorphic test per relation, structured-logged (`{relation, n_cases, violations}`).
- A generator producing transformed input pairs.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (needs a working e2e path to transform) and **OQ-13** (decides the multi-page metamorphic property; gating the §9.5 cross-page KV-sharing idea too).

title: Golden-artifact suite — insta exact (ocr --json/help) / fuzzy logits+activations / scrubbed NDJSON / canonicalized cross-platform
description:
# Golden-artifact suite (`/testing-golden-artifacts`)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Golden artifacts freeze known-good outputs and diff forever — the cheapest, highest-signal regression catcher. They cover what differential/metamorphic do not: the exact CLI/robot surface contract and the per-layer numeric fingerprints. A snapshot diff catches a regression the moment it lands, with the exact before/after visible in the PR.

## WHAT TO DO
Apply the RIGHT pattern per artifact type (this is the load-bearing discipline of the skill):
- **exact (insta snapshots)** for `focr ocr --json` structure and CLI `--help` output.
- **fuzzy (epsilon / ULP)** for logits and per-layer activation tensors (consumes the per-op ULP table).
- **scrubbed (strip timing, run-id, durations)** for robot NDJSON event streams.
- **canonicalized (line endings, paths, sorted bbox)** for cross-platform stability.
- Frozen reference fixture `tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json` from the Python oracle is the bar.
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` workflow with **mandatory `git diff` review**; `*.actual` gitignored; **CI never auto-updates** goldens.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Wrong pattern = useless test: an exact snapshot of a timing-laden NDJSON stream flaps; a fuzzy snapshot of CLI help hides real regressions. Pattern-per-artifact is mandatory.
- Canonicalization (strip timing, sort bbox) makes the snapshot deterministic across runs/platforms.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Robot NDJSON has run-ids and durations that MUST be scrubbed or every run flaps.
- Cross-platform path/line-ending differences must be canonicalized or the 5-target matrix produces 5 different goldens.
- CI must NEVER auto-update goldens (that defeats the regression-catch); only a human-reviewed `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` + `git diff` lands a new golden.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr ocr --json` and `--help` have exact insta snapshots.
- logits/activations have fuzzy (ULP) goldens.
- robot NDJSON has scrubbed goldens; cross-platform outputs canonicalized.
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` documented; CI never auto-updates.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The golden tests themselves (insta + custom fuzzy/scrubbed comparators), structured-logged on mismatch.
- A test asserting `*.actual` is gitignored and that CI does not set `UPDATE_GOLDENS`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the reference JSON bar) and VERIFY-ladder-l1-l2 (the ULP comparator for fuzzy tensor goldens). Consumed-by VERIFY-ladder-l5.

title: ConformanceTest trait + coverage-accounting matrix (≥0.95 MUST) + DISCREPANCIES/XFAIL discipline + fixture provenance
description:
# ConformanceTest trait + coverage matrix + XFAIL discipline

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The conformance harness is the structured backbone that turns ad-hoc tests into an auditable coverage claim. `/testing-conformance-harnesses` requires every MUST/SHOULD clause of OUR spec to be enumerated and accounted, so 'we are conformant' is a measured statement (≥0.95 MUST coverage) rather than a vibe. This is what lets the release scorecard say 'conformance: green' with evidence.

## WHAT TO DO
- Define a `ConformanceTest` trait: `name / category / requirement_level{Must,Should,May} / run`.
- Build a **coverage-accounting matrix**: every MUST/SHOULD clause of our spec — the extracted `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` (THE SPEC, §10) — enumerated, with **≥0.95 MUST coverage required to claim conformance**.
- **Differential testing (Pattern 1) against the PyO3 oracle is the gold standard** registered here.
- **Fixture provenance is mandatory**: a `PROVENANCE.md` recording `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, the git ref, and the exact command.
- **Intentional divergences are XFAIL, never SKIP** — each an entry `DISC-NNN` in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` (reference behavior, our impl, MEASURED impact, kill-switch env var, resolution, review date).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- XFAIL (expected-fail, counted) vs SKIP (ignored, hides debt): a SKIP silently drops coverage; an XFAIL keeps the clause in the matrix as a known, ledgered divergence.
- The coverage matrix is computed from the SPEC, not the test list — so a missing test shows up as an uncovered MUST clause, not as a passing suite.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The spec (`EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md`) is produced in Phase 1 from the pinned source (E-PM1 + spec extraction) — the matrix cannot be complete until the spec is extracted, but the trait + matrix scaffold lands first.
- 'partial' coverage NEVER rounds up to 'present'; 'excluded' still counts as coverage debt.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `ConformanceTest` trait implemented; differential/golden/metamorphic suites register under it with categories.
- Coverage matrix enumerates every MUST/SHOULD clause; ≥0.95 MUST coverage reported to claim conformance.
- Every intentional divergence is an XFAIL with a `DISC-NNN` in `DISCREPANCIES.md`; fixture `PROVENANCE.md` present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A meta-test asserting the coverage matrix computes a MUST-coverage ratio and fails if < 0.95 (when claiming conformance).
- A test asserting every XFAIL maps to a real `DISC-NNN`.
- Structured logging of the coverage matrix as a committed artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the spec source) and VERIFY-oracle-bridge (the differential gold standard). The other suites (differential/metamorphic/golden) register under this trait.

title: Three-pillar release certification (perf / conformance / surface) with FeatureUniverse / SurfaceMatrix; no victory-on-one-while-another-regresses
description:
# Three-pillar release certification (ML-System-class gauntlet)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is an ML-System-class port (the `/running-the-gauntlet-on-your-rust-port` class shared with frankentorch / franken_whisper). The release gate is the three-pillar gauntlet — the single check that prevents shipping a fast-but-wrong, or correct-but-incomplete-surface, binary. It enforces the project's deepest rule: you cannot win on one pillar while another regresses.

## WHAT TO DO
Run the gauntlet as the release gate with **three pillars, NO victory-on-one-while-another-regresses**:
- **(a) Performance** — honest per-stage ratios vs the reference (from VERIFY-gauntlet-bench / §9).
- **(b) Conformance** — same answer as the bf16 reference (L0–L5 ladder + metamorphic + fault), via the ConformanceTest matrix.
- **(c) Surface parity** — a **FeatureUniverse / SurfaceMatrix** accounting EVERY CLI surface, robot event, and modeling feature as `present | partial | missing | n/a | excluded`. **partial NEVER rounds up to present; excluded still counts as coverage debt.**

Produce the **release-readiness scorecard**: parity (L0–L5 all green), surface-parity (CLI/contract), honest perf vs reference (gauntlet ratios), determinism, cross-platform build matrix, ledger completeness. **A release cannot ship with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence.**

**Convergence rule**: ≥10 full rounds, ≥2 consecutive clean rounds (<3 new genuine findings), every open hypothesis resolved (per-pillar hypothesis ledgers). `convergence-tracker` gates it.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The FeatureUniverse is the formal answer to 'did we drop a feature' — every modeling feature (R-SWA, Gundam tiling, multi-page, all prompt modes), every CLI surface, every robot event is enumerated and scored.
- Convergent multi-round (not single-pass) because early rounds find shallow bugs; convergence requires the deep ones to stop appearing.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A green perf pillar with a regressed conformance cell is a FAIL, not a partial win — the no-cross-regression rule is absolute.
- The capacity certificate from asupersync (p95/p99 queueing, no nested-runtime / no rayon oversubscription) is a gauntlet artifact that must be present (proves the `many_pages` soak holds).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Scorecard all-green: parity, surface, perf, determinism, build matrix, ledger.
- FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix complete; no `present` that is actually `partial`; excluded items counted as debt.
- Convergence: ≥10 rounds, ≥2 consecutive clean rounds, all hypotheses resolved.
- No red parity cell, no unledgered divergence at ship.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A scorecard generator emitting the per-pillar status as a committed artifact.
- A `convergence-tracker` integration asserting the round/clean-round criteria.
- A test asserting the SurfaceMatrix enumerates every CLI subcommand + every robot event in the frozen schema.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (conformance pillar), VERIFY-conformance-trait (the matrix), VERIFY-gauntlet-bench (perf pillar), and VERIFY-conformal-ratchet (the release decision uses its lower bound). This is the Phase-5 release gate.

title: Conformal lower-bound release ratchet — Beta-posterior × distribution-free conformal band; release decision uses the LOWER bound
description:
# Conformal lower-bound release ratchet

## BACKGROUND / WHY
A point-estimate parity score (e.g. '99.2% pass') is overconfident — it ignores sampling uncertainty and lets a noisy improvement masquerade as progress. The conformal lower-bound ratchet (the `/alien-artifact-coding` conformal family) is the FORMAL version of 'parity gate first': it makes the release decision on a statistically defensible LOWER bound, so a change only lands if it genuinely raises the floor. This is the alien-artifact math family that keeps the release honest under uncertainty.

## WHAT TO DO
- Compute the parity score as a **Beta-posterior per category × a distribution-free conformal band**.
- The **release decision uses the LOWER bound, NOT the point estimate** (`truncate_score` to 6 dp).
- **Ratchet rule**: a change may only land if it **raises the lower bound WITHOUT lowering any per-category bound.**
- Ship the galaxy-brain transparency card (equation · substituted values · plain-English intuition · validity assumptions · what would flip the decision) and an assumptions ledger.
- Provide a deterministic conservative fallback (the raw pass/fail point estimate) if the conformal machinery cannot be computed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- LOWER bound, not point estimate: prevents shipping on lucky noise; this is the load-bearing decision rule.
- Per-category bounds (not just aggregate): a change that improves overall but regresses table parity is REJECTED — the per-category no-lowering rule encodes the no-cross-regression principle at the statistics layer.
- `truncate_score` to 6 dp: deterministic, reproducible decision boundary.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Requires a calibration set sized for the conformal band to be meaningful; too-small a corpus gives a useless (very low) lower bound — document the minimum.
- No adaptive controller ships without a conservative deterministic fallback (Alien-Artifact Engineering Contract).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Parity score computed as Beta-posterior × conformal band; release decision taken on the LOWER bound (truncated to 6 dp).
- Ratchet enforced: a landing change raises the lower bound and lowers no per-category bound.
- Transparency card + assumptions ledger + deterministic fallback present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A unit test of the lower-bound computation on synthetic pass/fail counts (known Beta posteriors).
- A ratchet test asserting a per-category regression blocks the land even if aggregate improves.
- Structured logging of the per-category bounds + decision.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (the per-category parity results it ratchets over) and VERIFY-conformance-trait (the category structure). Its lower bound is consumed by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert as the release decision input.

title: E-processes (Ville) for load-bearing invariants — KV bound, int8 overflow-free, determinism, SIMD==scalar
description:
# E-processes (Ville's inequality) for the load-bearing invariants

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Four invariants, if EVER violated, silently corrupt output: the KV cache exceeding its bound (R-SWA correctness), an int8 i32 accumulator overflowing (wrong arithmetic), same-input nondeterminism (irreproducibility), and the SIMD path diverging from scalar (silent kernel bug). A fixed-sample test under-monitors an unbounded test stream. E-processes (anytime-valid, Ville's inequality) alarm on the FIRST genuine violation across an unbounded stream WITHOUT a Bonferroni penalty — the right tool for invariants that must hold forever. This is the same machinery the gauntlet uses for its statistical decisions, reused for safety invariants.

## WHAT TO DO
Monitor each invariant as an **anytime-valid e-process** (`p₀, λ, α` per hardware/software calibration), alarming on the first genuine violation across an unbounded test stream:
1. **KV cache never exceeds `L·(m+128)`** — L=12, n=128, m=reference length. The R-SWA ring buffer + reference block must satisfy `C_RSWA(T) = L·(m + min(n,T)) ≤ L·(m+n)`.
2. **int8 i32 accumulator never overflows** — the worst case is the dense layer-0 `down_proj` at **K = 6848** (U8S8 ≤ ~221.7M; it fits i32 but must be PROVEN, NOT assumed; do NOT inherit frankensearch's `k≤1536` bound).
3. **Same-input determinism** — same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
4. **SIMD path == scalar path bit-identical** — integer accumulation is associative, so SDOT/SMMLA/VNNI == scalar EXACTLY (tolerance is only for int8-vs-f32 drift, never for the SIMD path itself).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- E-process (not p-value): anytime-valid, no multiple-testing penalty across an unbounded stream — correct for 'must hold forever' invariants.
- The overflow invariant is a PROOF OBLIGATION (AGENTS.md doctrine #6): a unit test at worst-case K=6848 on EVERY arch, plus the e-process as the continuous monitor.
- SIMD==scalar is bit-identical (not cosine) because integer add is associative — any divergence is a definite bug, alarmed immediately.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Calibrate `p₀, λ, α` so a genuine violation alarms but hardware/measurement jitter does not produce false alarms.
- The KV-bound e-process must account for the logical reference block `m` GROWING with page count up to the 32K cap (R-SWA is constant generated-token KV, not page-constant memory) — the bound is `L·(m+128)` with growing `m`, not a fixed number.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Four e-processes implemented, calibrated, and wired into the test stream.
- Each alarms on an injected violation (a deliberately-broken fixture) and stays silent on the clean stream.
- The int8 overflow invariant additionally has a worst-case-K=6848 per-arch unit test (the proof obligation).
- The SIMD==scalar invariant has a bit-identical assertion per arch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- One e-process test per invariant, each with a clean-stream pass and an injected-violation alarm.
- The K=6848 overflow proof unit test (scalar + every SIMD tier).
- The SIMD==scalar bit-identical test per dispatched tier (SDOT/SMMLA/VNNI/scalar).
- Structured logging of the e-value per step (`{invariant, step, e_value, alarm}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-conformance-trait (registers under it). The overflow + SIMD==scalar invariants are consumed-by P2/P3 kernel beads (they re-prove these on every kernel change). The KV-bound invariant is consumed-by the R-SWA work in P1/P3.

title: NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE / PERF_LEDGER / DISCREPANCIES artifact-graph ledger discipline (claim_id, evidence_id, fixture hash, CPU feature, kill-switch)
description:
# Artifact-graph ledger discipline — NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE / PERF_LEDGER / DISCREPANCIES

## BACKGROUND / WHY
G7 (Honest) is a non-negotiable goal: every accepted divergence and every rejected optimization must be a reproducible, traceable ledger entry — never prose, never a remembered claim. This discipline is what stops the swarm from re-attempting a dead lever (the inherited NE-INH entries) and stops a 'win' from being declared without a head-to-head measured ratio + correctness proof. It is the conscience's record-keeping, and it serves the whole optimization program by making every speed claim falsifiable.

## WHAT TO DO
- `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md` + `docs/PERF_LEDGER.md` are **ARTIFACT-GRAPH ledgers, NOT prose.** Use the frankentorch row format: `date | WIN/NEGATIVE(reverted) | lever | MEASURED before→after vs reference (ratio) | bit-exact correctness proof | Disposition KEEP/REVERT | 'do not retry X unless Y' | per-lever W/L/N`.
- Additionally carry, per entry/row, the **FrankenSuite artifact-graph fields**: `claim_id`, `evidence_id`, **model source commit + fixture hash** (from the Phase -1 truth pack), the **CPU feature string** (the dispatched SIMD tier), the **exact command + env**, and the **fallback / kill-switch state**.
- Per-lever artifact dirs `artifacts/perf/<bead>/` with paired baseline/after gauntlet logs + SHA-256.
- `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` — every accepted numeric divergence: reference behavior, our impl, MEASURED impact, kill-switch env var, resolution, tests affected, review date.
- **A 'win' only counts with a head-to-head MEASURED ratio against a real reference AND a correctness proof.** Everything else lands in NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE, not PERF_LEDGER.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Artifact-graph (claim_id/evidence_id/fixture hash) not prose: every claim is reproducible and traceable to the EXACT model version it was measured against (the model changed 2026-06-24).
- The kill-switch / fallback state per row proves WHICH path was active when the number was measured (int8-attn on/off, SIMD tier).
- Inherited NE-INH-1..5 are PRIORS (re-confirm on this model's shapes), not franken_ocr measurements — keep them clearly separated.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A lever that does not clear its measurement bar is REVERTED, not kept; the per-lever W/L/N tally makes a thrice-failed idea visibly dead.
- No fabricated results — the franken_ocr measurements section stays empty until a real head-to-head ratio with a correctness proof exists.
- Every perf commit re-states its parity receipt (text exact, max logit diff, deterministic).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The three ledgers exist with the full artifact-graph schema (claim_id, evidence_id, fixture hash, CPU feature, command+env, kill-switch state).
- A landed perf win has a paired baseline/after artifact dir with SHA-256.
- Every accepted divergence is a DISCREPANCIES entry with measured impact + kill-switch + review date.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A linter/CI check that PERF_LEDGER rows have all mandatory artifact-graph fields and a referenced `artifacts/perf/<bead>/` dir.
- A check that every kill-switch env var named in DISCREPANCIES exists in `error.rs`/the engine.
- A check that no PERF_LEDGER 'WIN' lacks a correctness-proof reference.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the model source commit + fixture hash come from the Phase -1 truth pack). Consumed-by every P2/P3/P4 perf bead (each lands its measured row here) and by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert (ledger-completeness is a scorecard cell).

title: Head-to-head gauntlet bench (benches/gauntlet) vs the Phase -1 proven CPU reference — per-stage ratios with thread/allocator/precision fairness
description:
# Head-to-head gauntlet bench — `benches/gauntlet`

## BACKGROUND / WHY
G2 (CPU speed beats the proven CPU baseline) is meaningless without an apples-to-apples head-to-head. This bench is how G2 is PROVED — and, critically, how fake wins are PREVENTED (the hardened frankentorch lesson: never benchmark torch at @64, oversubscription inflates fake 'wins'). It shells out to the real reference and reports honest focr/reference ratios per stage, so the honest story is visible: decode faster, vision-prefill maybe slower in f32 v1.

## WHAT TO DO
`benches/gauntlet` shells out to the **Phase -1 proven CPU reference** (`FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD` / `FOCR_REFERENCE_PYTHON` env) per stage, parses elapsed, reports the **honest focr/reference ratio** (tag each row OK/warn/slower/'focr faster') — NOT a self-relative number. If CPU-patched HF cannot be proven equivalent to the CUDA oracle (OQ-17), the target is **llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX Runtime / MLAS** and is LABELED that way.

**Fairness controls — ALL MANDATORY:**
- **Thread parity**: pin `OMP_NUM_THREADS` / torch `set_num_threads(N)` EQUAL to focr's thread budget; **NEVER benchmark torch at @64** — measure at @8/@32.
- **Allocator fairness**: build focr with the SAME allocator posture used for the claim (mimalloc behind a feature), wired into the MEASURED binary, not merely mentioned.
- **Best-of-N with warmup discard**; report the min and the precision of each side.
- **Precision annotation per row**: focr-int8 vs torch-bf16 (and torch-int8 if available) — a raw ratio across different numerics is meaningless without it.
- **PER-STAGE ratios** (preprocess / vision-encode / prefill / decode-per-token): decode-per-token faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference is the GATING part of G2; vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is acceptable and recorded honestly.

Also compute the **roofline** per stage (compute floor = int8/int4 GEMM FLOPs ÷ peak int8 throughput; memory floor = bytes streamed ÷ DRAM bandwidth; take the max) and record it next to every measured ratio in PERF_LEDGER — treat any stage >~1.3× above its floor as a named, attackable lever.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Honest ratio vs a REAL reference, not self-relative — a win only counts head-to-head (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE law).
- Per-stage because the cost center MOVES with output length (sparse page = vision-prefill-dominated; 40-page = decode-dominated) — a single end-to-end ratio hides the truth.
- Roofline target makes the perf goal falsifiable: the reranker lesson is the gap is 'kernels below peak,' so each stage's distance-from-floor is the lever list.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Benchmarks must SKIP gracefully (exit 0) if the model fixture is absent.
- A benchmark-guardrail gate catches regressions vs a frozen baseline.
- OQ-17 decides whether the reference is CPU-HF or llama.cpp/ONNX — label accordingly; G2 'beats the CPU reference' is honest only against whatever CPU reference is PROVEN to run.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `benches/gauntlet` produces per-stage honest focr/reference ratios with thread/allocator/precision/best-of-N fairness controls all applied.
- Each row labeled with the reference identity (CPU-HF proven-equivalent, or llama.cpp/ONNX) and precision annotation.
- Roofline floor recorded next to each ratio in PERF_LEDGER.
- Decode-per-token ratio < 1.0 (faster) vs the proven CPU reference on the primary arches (the gating G2 part); vision-prefill recorded honestly.
- Skips gracefully without the model.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A bench-guardrail test asserting no regression vs the frozen `.bench-history` baseline (cv_pct reported; >5% = noise, ineligible; MT8 attribution to a named ≥0.1% frame).
- A fairness-control test asserting thread parity and allocator posture are actually applied (not just configured).
- Structured logging per row (`{stage, focr_ms, ref_ms, ratio, ref_identity, precision, roofline_floor, tag}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the Phase -1 proven CPU reference + pinned source), **OQ-17** (which reference is proven to run), and VERIFY-ledger-discipline (where the rows land). Feeds the performance pillar of VERIFY-three-pillar-cert. Consumed-by every P3 SIMD-kernel perf claim.

title: Determinism gate + many_pages_without_deadlock watchdog + asupersync capacity certificate
description:
# Determinism gate + concurrency watchdog + capacity certificate

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Two failure classes are catastrophic and silent: nondeterministic output (same image → different parse, breaking reproducibility and every golden) and a concurrency deadlock under many pages (the frankensearch deadlock saga burned 5 commits). This bead is the gate that proves franken_ocr is deterministic and does not hang — both are release-blocking properties distinct from oracle parity (L4 is 'match the reference', this is 'match yourself / don't hang').

## WHAT TO DO
- **Determinism gate**: a separate gate asserting same-input-twice → byte-identical output under greedy (independent of the oracle; this is self-consistency).
- **`many_pages_without_deadlock` CI watchdog**: pages ≫ pool — the test HANGS on regression. This is the durable fix the frankensearch deadlock saga converged on: single `OcrModel` behind a cache; SEQUENTIAL outer page/document loop; each forward fans out across all cores internally via the kernel's OWN rayon pool (pinned to physical cores, ONE live forward at a time). NEVER nest rayon under a held lock; NEVER nest a second asupersync runtime inside a task.
- **asupersync capacity certificate** (gauntlet artifact): p95/p99 queueing evidence + a proof of NO nested-runtime / NO rayon-pool oversubscription (the USL pool caps of §6.9 / AF-5 hold under the `many_pages` soak); streaming uses BOUNDED channels (backpressure, never unbounded growth).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Determinism is a SEPARATE gate from L4: L4 matches the (possibly noisy) oracle over its reproducible prefix; the determinism gate demands WE are byte-identical to ourselves — a stronger, oracle-independent property.
- The watchdog HANGS (rather than fails fast) on regression because a deadlock IS a hang — a timeout wrapper turns the hang into a CI failure with the offending thread dump.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- 'Streaming per-page' = streaming the OUTPUT of a sequentially-processed page, NEVER concurrent forwards (which oversubscribe rayon N×).
- The capacity certificate must prove the USL pool caps (decode GEMV peak ~8–16 effective cores bandwidth-bound; not 64) hold — oversubscribing 64 cores is β-dominated slowdown.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Determinism gate green: same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog passes (pages ≫ pool, no hang) under a timeout wrapper.
- Capacity certificate produced: p95/p99 queueing + no-nested-runtime + no-oversubscription proof under the `many_pages` soak; bounded channels verified.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A determinism test running each golden image twice and asserting byte-identical output.
- The `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (pages ≫ pool) under a wall-clock timeout.
- A capacity-certificate test emitting p95/p99 + the oversubscription/nesting proof as a structured artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on the token/decode path being runnable (L4). Consumed-by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert (determinism is a scorecard cell; the capacity certificate is a gauntlet artifact).

title: Tests: L0-L5 ladder integration runner + per-gate structured scorecard fixture
description:
# L0–L5 ladder integration runner + scorecard

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The individual ladder gates (L0..L5) need a single integration runner that executes them in order, short-circuits sensibly (a failed L0 makes L1+ meaningless), and emits one structured scorecard the release process and per-commit parity-receipt consume. This is the operational face of 'L0–L5 parity all green' that appears in every phase exit gate.

## WHAT TO DO
- An integration runner `tests/conformance_harness.rs` that executes L0→L5 in order over the golden corpus, recording per-gate pass/fail + the measured metric (exact/cosine/logit-diff/CER/TEDS).
- Emit a structured scorecard fixture (`{gate, granularity, tolerance, measured, pass}`) consumed by the parity-receipt every perf commit re-states.
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing any fallback at `/nonexistent`.
- The runner is the single entry the phase exit gates and the three-pillar conformance pillar call.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- A single ordered runner (not 6 independent tests) so the parity receipt is one artifact and a lower-gate failure is reported clearly rather than as 6 confusing failures.
- Structured scorecard so the conformal ratchet and the three-pillar cert can consume per-category results directly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must re-prove on EVERY perf commit (the §9.2 loop re-proves L0–L5 each lever) — keep it fast enough to run in the optimization loop.
- Skip-with-SUCCESS must still PROVE the native path ran (the `/nonexistent` fallback trick) so a silently-skipped suite is not mistaken for a pass.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The runner executes L0–L5 over the corpus and emits the structured scorecard.
- Per-commit parity receipt derivable from the scorecard (e.g. 'text exact, max logit diff 0.05, deterministic').
- Skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; native-path-ran proof present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the integration test bead — it composes L0..L5 and asserts the all-green condition, with full structured logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on all four ladder gates (VERIFY-ladder-l0, -l1-l2, -l3-l4, -l5). Consumed-by VERIFY-determinism-gate (needs the token/decode path), VERIFY-three-pillar-cert, and VERIFY-conformal-ratchet.

title: Oracle nondeterminism-floor characterization (run-twice / two-thread-counts envelope) — sets all downstream tolerances
description:
# Oracle nondeterminism-floor characterization

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The HF reference is FREQUENTLY non-deterministic across torch thread counts / BLAS reduction order at the logit-tie level (bf16 arithmetic, 129280-vocab argmax). If we set parity tolerances WITHOUT first measuring the oracle's own noise, we will chase phantom 'bugs' that are just the oracle disagreeing with itself — or, worse, set tolerances so loose that real drift hides inside them. This task is the keystone that makes every L1/L3/L4 tolerance defensible rather than guessed. It directly serves 'correctness before speed': a franken_ocr int8 divergence INSIDE the oracle's own bf16 noise is NOT a bug, and we must be able to prove that.

## WHAT TO DO
Before setting ANY tolerance:
- Run the oracle **twice** over the full golden corpus, and at **two thread counts** (e.g. `torch.set_num_threads(8)` and `(32)`), with `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)` where it does not error.
- Diff the runs and record the **nondeterminism envelope** as a committed fixture: per-token divergence rate, first-divergence position per document, and the per-logit max-abs spread at matching positions.
- Derive: (a) the **L4 'exact' prefix** = the longest decoded-token prefix the oracle reproduces identically across all runs/threads, per document; (b) the **L3 logit tolerance** = derived from the measured oracle logit variance (NOT the imported frankensearch `0.055`).
- Commit `tests/fixtures/oracle_nondeterminism_envelope.json` and a `tolerances.toml` consumed by the ladder gates.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- L4 exactness is defined ONLY over the reproducible prefix; beyond it, the oracle is non-deterministic and we cannot demand bit-exactness.
- The per-op ULP tolerance table (default 4 ULP f32 matmul, 2 ULP elementwise) is the L1/L2 comparator — but the END-TO-END logit/token tolerances are derived here from measured variance, not a hand-guessed epsilon.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If the oracle is wildly non-deterministic (large divergence rate), that is itself a finding to ledger — it may mean greedy temperature=0.0 still hits ties; record and pick the longest stable prefix.
- Must be re-run if the pinned model source changes (CI-guarded against drift via the E-PM1 census).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `oracle_nondeterminism_envelope.json` committed with per-document divergence rate + first-divergence position + logit spread.
- `tolerances.toml` committed: L1/L2 ULP table, L3 logit tolerance (derived), L4 exact-prefix bounds — all sourced to measured numbers.
- A short note in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` if the floor reveals any structural nondeterminism.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A regression test asserting the derived tolerances are present and internally consistent (L3 ≥ measured spread, L4 prefix ≤ document length).
- Structured logging of the envelope computation per document.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (needs the oracle to run twice/two-thread). This task BLOCKS all the L1–L5 ladder gates, which consume `tolerances.toml`.

title: Test-only PyO3/subprocess oracle bridge with deterministic-algorithms + per-op ULP tolerance table + EngineIdentity
description:
# Test-only oracle bridge (PyO3 / subprocess) — ML-System-class wiring

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Frozen `.npy` fixtures cover the committed golden corpus, but per-op differential testing (Pattern 1 of `/testing-conformance-harnesses`) is the gold standard and needs a LIVE bridge to the pinned reference for ad-hoc inputs. This bridge is what lets a kernel author diff a single op against the oracle without regenerating the whole fixture set. It serves correctness-before-speed by making oracle comparison cheap enough to run on every kernel change.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a **test-only** PyO3 or subprocess bridge to the pinned reference (`torch==2.10.0`, `transformers==4.57.1`).
- Force `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)`; capture the seeded RNG per call.
- Provide a **per-op ULP tolerance table** as the L1/L2 comparator: default **4 ULP for f32 matmul, 2 ULP for elementwise** — this is the comparator, NOT a hand-guessed epsilon.
- Define `EngineIdentity::{Subject, Oracle}` and assert them **distinct** so the oracle is never accidentally compared against itself.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **NEVER linked into the shipping `focr` inference binary** — gated behind a test-only feature/dev-dependency so G3's no-FFI runtime claim holds.
- subprocess is acceptable if PyO3 linkage is fragile across the 5 build targets; the bridge only needs to run on the dev/CI host with the reference env.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The bridge must not leak a torch runtime into the release artifact — a build test asserts `focr` links no Python.
- ULP comparisons must account for the oracle's measured nondeterminism floor (VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor): a 4-ULP table is the per-op default but end-to-end tolerances come from the measured envelope.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Bridge runs per-op diffs against the reference with the ULP table comparator.
- `EngineIdentity::Subject != Oracle` asserted in the harness.
- Build test proves `focr` (release) links no Python/torch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A smoke test diffing a trivial op (e.g. RMSNorm on a fixed input) Subject-vs-Oracle within the ULP table.
- A linkage test asserting the shipping binary is FFI-free.
- Structured logging of every op-diff (`{op, max_ulp, within_tolerance}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the reference env) and VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (tolerance derivation).

title: L0 preprocessing parity gate — resized/normalized/padded tensor + tile geometry, EXACT
description:
# L0 — preprocessing parity gate (EXACT)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L0 is the bottom rung of the parity ladder. If preprocessing diverges, every downstream stage is fed a different tensor and all higher gates become meaningless. Preprocessing is deterministic integer/float arithmetic with no quantization, so it MUST be exact. This gate is a prerequisite for P1 (fp32 parity).

## WHAT TO DO
Gate the preprocess front end output against the oracle's preprocessed tensor, **exact**:
- **Normalize**: `image_mean = image_std = [0.5,0.5,0.5]` → pixels scaled to [0,1] then `(x-0.5)/0.5` → **[-1,1]**, CHW layout.
- **Pad to square with gray (127,127,127)** = `tuple(int(m*255))`, aspect preserved (`ImageOps.pad` equivalent).
- **Tile geometry**: `patch_size=16`, `downsample_ratio=4`, `num_queries = ceil((image_size//patch_size)/downsample_ratio)`. Two modes: **Base** (1024×1024, `crop_mode=false`) and **Gundam** (`base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `crop_mode=true`, global padded 1024 view `global_view_pos='head'` + 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()` / `find_closest_aspect_ratio()` over `i*j ∈ [min_num,max_num]`).
- Ratio selection (`find_closest_aspect_ratio`), gray pad value, and the [-1,1] normalize must all match exactly.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- EXACT tolerance (not cosine): preprocessing is reproducible arithmetic, any drift is a bug.
- The tile census (token counts per mode/aspect-ratio) is asserted against the E-PM1 machine-readable census, the source of truth.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Gundam multi-tile token totals per aspect ratio are **[OPEN, OQ-18/OQ-7]** — this gate cannot finalize the Gundam geometry assertions until those are resolved.
- Bicubic pos-embed interpolation (for non-1024/tiled inputs) is a separate frankentorch GAP (f32 build) — L0 covers the resize/pad/normalize; the interp correctness is checked at L1/L2.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Base-mode preprocessed tensor exact-equal to the oracle's (bitwise on the f32 array).
- Gundam-mode tile geometry + per-tile token counts match the census once OQ-7/OQ-18 are resolved.
- Gray pad value, [-1,1] normalize, ratio selection all exact.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit tests per preprocess submodule (resize/pad/normalize/tile) with golden tensors.
- An integration test feeding the golden corpus through preprocess and diffing exact vs oracle, with structured per-image logging (`{image, mode, tile_count, max_abs_diff, exact}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden preprocessed tensors) and VERIFY-tokenizer-conformance (the prompt side of the input contract must be token-id-exact first, an L0 prerequisite). The geometry assertions consume the E-PM1 census and are gated on OQ-7/OQ-18.

title: L1 per-op + L2 per-layer parity gates — cosine ≈ 1.0, max-abs-diff ledgered
description:
# L1 (per-op) + L2 (per-layer) parity gates

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L1/L2 catch kernel drift at the finest granularity the oracle exposes. A correct end-to-end answer can hide a compensating pair of per-op bugs; per-op/per-layer cosine pins each kernel to the reference. These are the gates every kernel bead in P1/P2/P3 re-proves on each change — they are the operational form of 'every fused op must stay at peak AND correct.'

## WHAT TO DO
- **L1 per-op**: each kernel's output vs the oracle activation. Tolerance **cosine ≈ 1.0 (≥ 0.9999 in f32; documented int8 tolerance)**. Comparator uses the per-op ULP table (4 ULP f32 matmul / 2 ULP elementwise) for the bridge path.
- **L2 per-layer**: per decoder-layer hidden state (all 12) and per vision-stage output (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector). Tolerance **cosine ≈ 1.0; max-abs-diff LEDGERED** per layer.
- Both consume the frozen `.npy` dumps from VERIFY-oracle-harness AND can use the live VERIFY-oracle-bridge for ad-hoc inputs.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Cosine ≈ 1.0 (not bit-exact) because f32-vs-bf16 and int8-vs-bf16 are continuous-value divergences within a documented tolerance.
- max-abs-diff is LEDGERED (recorded) per layer so a slow drift across layers is visible, not just the final cosine.
- A wrong SAM window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme (OQ-15) surfaces ONLY as a fuzzy L2 cosine drop — L2 must therefore log per-stage cosine so this silent-corruption failure mode is caught.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- int8 tolerances must sit inside the oracle nondeterminism floor (VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor) — an int8 divergence inside the oracle's own bf16 noise is not a bug.
- The projector concat order (OQ-6) and SAM window (OQ-15) are [OPEN]; a wrong answer passes L0 but fails L2 — so L2 must run per-vision-stage, not just on the final hidden.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every implemented kernel passes L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 (f32) / within documented int8 tolerance.
- All 12 decoder-layer hiddens + all 6 vision-stage outputs pass L2 cosine ≈ 1.0 with max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Per-stage cosine logged so OQ-15/OQ-6 misimplementations are caught.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Per-op unit tests (one per kernel) diffing against the frozen oracle activation.
- A per-layer integration test producing a structured table (`{stage, cosine, max_abs_diff}`) for the full forward, committed as a golden-fuzzy artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (per-layer dumps), VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (tolerance derivation), VERIFY-oracle-bridge (live per-op diffs). Consumed-by every P1/P2/P3 kernel bead.

title: L3 logits + L4 token parity gates — logits within derived tolerance + argmax match; decoded tokens EXACT on reproducible prefix
description:
# L3 (logits) + L4 (token) parity gates

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L3/L4 are where quantization risk is actually adjudicated. L3 bounds the continuous logit divergence; L4 demands the discrete decoded token sequence be exact where the reference is deterministic. This is the gate that says 'int8/int4 did not change what the model says.' It is the heart of G1 for the decoder and the gate P2/P4 must clear.

## WHAT TO DO
- **L3 logits**: pre-sampling logits within the int8/int4 quant tolerance DERIVED from the oracle nondeterminism floor (NOT the imported frankensearch `max_diff ~0.055` — that is a precedent value, the real number comes from VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor). **argmax MUST match** at every position where the reference is deterministic.
- **L4 token**: the decoded token sequence is **EXACT under greedy where the reference is deterministic** — and 'where deterministic' is defined ONLY over the reproducible prefix established by VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor.
- Replicate the exact sampler semantics: greedy / low-temp argmax, strip trailing EOS `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`, and `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with a sliding `ngram_window` that DIFFERS by mode — **128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image)** (these are first-class generation semantics; the `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor` bans repeated 35-grams within the window).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Discrete output bit-exact, continuous (logits) only within documented tolerance — the frankensearch `parity_logits_and_ranking_match_reference` shape.
- A separate determinism gate (VERIFY-determinism-gate) asserts same-input-twice → identical output; L4 is about matching the ORACLE, the determinism gate is about matching OURSELVES.
- The ngram window per mode is a generation semantic, not a CLI flag — the sampler must replicate the exact window or L4 diverges on repetition-prone content.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 129280-vocab argmax has logit ties at the bf16 level; argmax-match is only demanded where the oracle itself is stable (the reproducible prefix).
- int8 on `lm_head` (P2 stage 2c) and int8 attention (2b) go BEYOND the validated NVFP4/GGUF set — L3/L4 are the gate that retires that risk (OQ-14); a regression reverts just that stage via its kill-switch (`FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`, `FOCR_INT8_ATTN`).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L3: logits within the derived tolerance; argmax matches on every deterministic position.
- L4: decoded tokens EXACT over the reproducible prefix under greedy.
- Every perf commit re-states its parity receipt (e.g. 'text exact, max logit diff 0.05, deterministic').

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A logits diff test producing `{position, max_logit_diff, argmax_match}` per token.
- A token-exact test over the reproducible prefix per document, structured-logged.
- An ngram-window test asserting the per-mode window (128 single / 1024 multi) bans the right 35-grams.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden logits/tokens) and VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (derives the L3 tolerance and the L4 reproducible prefix). Consumed-by P2/P4 quant beads.

title: L5 end-to-end OCR parity gate — decoded text + bbox on golden corpus; CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within documented budget
description:
# L5 — end-to-end OCR parity gate

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L5 is the top of the ladder and the only gate a non-expert can read: does franken_ocr produce the same document parse as the reference? It is the operational definition of G1. The lower gates can all be green and L5 still catch a postprocess bug (tag parsing, bbox rescale, markdown assembly). L5 is the gate that releases depend on.

## WHAT TO DO
- Compare decoded text + bbox tags on the frozen golden corpus.
- **exact-match where the reference is deterministic** (the reproducible-prefix discipline).
- Aggregate **CER / TEDS / Formula-CDM within a documented quantization budget**: target **int8 within noise** of bf16 (NVFP4's 'identical to BF16' as the bar); **int4 within a small ledgered budget**, with **no catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content**.
- Postprocess parity: EOS strip (`<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`), `<|ref|>…<|/ref|><|det|>…<|/det|>` regex parse, bbox coords `/999 → ×W/H` rescale, markdown assembly (HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, `<page>` separators for multi-page).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM are aggregate metrics with a DOCUMENTED budget, not exact — quantization is allowed a measured, ledgered tolerance, but exact-token content (dense numbers, tables, code, sub/superscripts) is held to a tighter tail bound (see VERIFY-tail-risk / AF-2).
- The metrics: CER (character error rate), TEDS / TEDS-S (table structure), Formula-CDM (LaTeX) — the reference's own reported strengths (printed text Text-Edit 0.038, tables TEDS 90.93, math Formula-CDM 92.61).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Multi-page is CROSS-PAGE DEPENDENT under R-SWA (OQ-13) — L5 multi-page comparison must be against the multi-page reference output, NEVER a sum of single-page parses.
- The full prompt-mode taxonomy (free OCR / layout / grounding / markdown) and whether bboxes are emitted in all modes is [OPEN, OQ-8] — L5 bbox assertions are gated on that.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Decoded text exact where deterministic; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the ledgered budget (int8 within noise, int4 within small budget).
- No catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content (checked via the tail-risk monitor).
- Postprocess (EOS, tag regex, bbox rescale, markdown) matches the reference.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- End-to-end test over the golden corpus producing a structured scorecard (`{doc, CER, TEDS, Formula_CDM, text_exact}`).
- A postprocess unit suite (tag regex, bbox /999 rescale, EOS strip).
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden e2e outputs), VERIFY-ladder-l3-l4 (tokens must match before text can), and VERIFY-golden-artifacts (the frozen `focr ocr --json` snapshots). Multi-page assertions gated on OQ-13; bbox-mode assertions on OQ-8.

title: Tokenizer conformance (OQ-16) — token-id-exact byte-level-BPE vs LlamaTokenizerFast, the L0/L4 prerequisite
description:
# Tokenizer conformance (OQ-16)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The tokenizer is the single highest-leverage correctness prerequisite: **a wrong token id corrupts EVERY downstream gate** (L0 input, L4 output, and everything between). The plan calls this out explicitly as an L0/L4 prerequisite that must pass before any decoder parity work. franken_ocr ships a pure-Rust byte-level BPE over `tokenizer.json` (no SentencePiece) and must be token-id-exact against `LlamaTokenizerFast`.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a dedicated exact-match conformance corpus: diverse Unicode, CJK, math, code, the DeepSeek glyph specials, and the structural specials `<image>` / `<|ref|>` / `<|det|>`.
- Round-trip `encode` → `decode` against `LlamaTokenizerFast` over `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB, vocab **129280**, ~512 special tokens). [VERIFIED]: `LlamaTokenizerFast`, byte-level BPE; bos/eos/pad = 0/1/2; `add_special_token=false`, `image_token='<image>'`, `ignore_id=-100`.
- Verify: pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token handling, DeepSeek-glyph handling (e.g. `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`).
- **Freeze the reference id sequences as golden fixtures.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Token-id-EXACT (not fuzzy) — there is no tolerance for tokenization; an off-by-one id is a different model input.
- Pure-Rust HF-tokenizers-JSON path, no SentencePiece dependency (G3 single static binary).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The pre-tokenizer regex and byte-fallback are the classic divergence sources between a hand-rolled BPE and `LlamaTokenizerFast`; the corpus must exercise multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries, combining characters, and the DeepSeek glyph specials.
- OQ-16 (the exact byte-level-BPE encode/decode semantics: pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token + glyph handling) must be resolved against the pinned `tokenizer.json` first.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- 100% token-id-exact encode AND decode round-trip vs `LlamaTokenizerFast` over the full conformance corpus.
- Frozen reference id sequences committed as golden fixtures.
- All structural specials (`<image>`, `<|ref|>`, `<|det|>`) and DeepSeek glyphs handled exactly.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A conformance test iterating the corpus, asserting `encode(text) == reference_ids` and `decode(ids) == text` for round-trippable inputs, structured-logged (`{case, len, mismatch_pos|none}`).
- Edge-case unit tests: empty string, lone surrogate-prohibited UTF-8, special-token-in-text, CJK runs, LaTeX, code with whitespace.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (pinned `tokenizer.json`) and **OQ-16** (the exact BPE semantics). This gate BLOCKS L0 and L4 (and therefore all decoder parity) — it is on the Phase-1 critical path.

title: Differential test suite — our path vs oracle, per-op + end-to-end, on golden corpus + community quant artifacts
description:
# Differential test suite

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Differential testing (`/testing-conformance-harnesses` Pattern 1) is the gold-standard conformance method: run our path and the oracle on the same inputs and diff. It catches bugs that golden snapshots miss (because golden snapshots only cover the frozen corpus, while differential testing can run on ANY input via the live bridge). It is how we prove 'same answer as the bf16 reference' for arbitrary inputs.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a differential harness diffing our path vs the oracle, **per-op AND end-to-end**, on: (a) the golden corpus, and (b) the **community quant artifacts** (NVFP4/GGUF per-layer activation dumps as secondary oracles).
- Per-op diffs go through the per-op ULP tolerance table (4 ULP f32 matmul / 2 ULP elementwise); end-to-end diffs go through the L3/L4/L5 tolerances.
- Wire into the `ConformanceTest` trait as `category=differential` tests.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Use the community NVFP4/GGUF dumps as SECONDARY oracles during bring-up — they confirm our quant path independently of the bf16 oracle, and the NVFP4 author reports 'OCR identical to BF16.'
- Differential testing is the gold standard for the ConformanceTest coverage matrix; intentional divergences become XFAIL (a `DISC-NNN`), never SKIP.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- NVFP4 quantizes only ~2196 linear modules and the exact set is [OPEN, OQ-14] — when using it as a secondary oracle, only compare the modules NVFP4 actually quantizes (its scale-key set), or the diff is apples-to-oranges.
- The bridge is test-only; differential e2e runs need the reference env (model-gated, skip-with-SUCCESS otherwise).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Per-op and e2e differential tests green against the bf16 oracle within tolerance on the golden corpus.
- Secondary-oracle (NVFP4/GGUF) diffs green on the modules they cover.
- Any intentional divergence recorded as an XFAIL + `DISC-NNN`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The differential harness itself, emitting structured per-test rows (`{op|e2e, oracle, max_diff, within_tol, xfail?}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-bridge (live diffs), VERIFY-ladder-l1-l2 (the tolerance comparators), and VERIFY-conformance-trait (the trait it registers under). OQ-14 gates the NVFP4 secondary-oracle comparison set.

title: Phase 4 integration: end-to-end int4 forward meets all exit gates
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the Phase-4 acceptance integration: it runs the full int4 forward (converter → int4 kernels → AF-1-allocated tiers → tail gate) on the golden corpus and asserts every §10 Phase-4 exit gate together, with comprehensive structured logging — the project mandate for a model-gated e2e proof that the whole int4 path works, not just its parts.

## WHAT TO DO
A model-gated e2e test (skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`) that:
- Converts to int4 (AF-1-allocated tiers + uniform-fallback both exercised), runs the full forward on the dense-numeric/table/code corpus.
- Asserts the **Phase-4 exit gates (§10):** int4 CER within the ledgered budget (NOT below the 4-bit cliff, Q4_K_M-class); CVaR_0.1 within budget (AF-2); size ≈ 1.8-2.9 GB **demonstrated** (P4-footprint-arithmetic); no catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content; every quant choice ledgered with measured impact.
- Asserts the **parity ladder still green at int4** (§8.2): L3 logits within int4 tolerance, L4 token exact where the reference is deterministic, L5 CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within budget; bit-identical SIMD-vs-scalar on the int4 kernel.
- Asserts the **decode bandwidth win** is realized (P4-int4-gemm-bench ratios cited) WITHOUT a CER regression (G1 > G2: a faster int4 kernel that drifts OCR is reverted).
- Asserts **determinism** (§7.3): same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- **Structured NDJSON logging** of every gate: per-config CER/CVaR/EVT/TEDS, realized footprint, decode bytes-streamed, bit-identical result, first-divergence token, kill-switch states.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
All Phase-4 exit gates pass together on the golden corpus; parity ladder green at int4; bandwidth win realized with no CER regression; deterministic; every quant choice ledgered. The test skips-green without weights.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the integration/e2e bead. It composes the unit/parity/bench beads above into the phase acceptance proof.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on every Phase-4 deliverable: `P4-int4-gemm` (the kernel), `P4-af1-bit-allocator` (the allocation), `P4-expected-loss-per-layer` (the tiering), `P4-tail-risk-gate` (the gate), `P4-footprint-arithmetic` (the demonstrated size), `P4-dense-corpus` (the measurement substrate). It is the convergence point that proves the epic.

title: Bench: int4 vs int8 decode GEMV bandwidth + throughput, roofline check
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
int4's entire thesis is **bandwidth**, so the win must be MEASURED, not assumed (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE doctrine: a win only counts with a head-to-head measured ratio + correctness proof). This bench quantifies the decode bandwidth/throughput win on the expert-MLP shape and checks it against the memory roofline.

## WHAT TO DO
- Criterion bench over the expert SiLU-gated MLP decode GEMV (1280↔896 ×3) and the lm_head GEMV (1280→129280), comparing int4 vs int8 weights at decode (single-token) on each arch tier (SMMLA/SDOT, VNNI/AVX2).
- **Bytes-streamed counter:** assert int4 streams ~half the weight bytes of int8 on the int4-tensor portion.
- **Roofline (§9.1):** compute the memory floor (bytes streamed ÷ DRAM bandwidth); report how close int4 decode sits to it; flag any stage >~1.3x above its floor as a named lever.
- **Fairness controls (§9.3):** best-of-N, fixed thread/allocator/precision; same git state / same `target/` / same minute; `cv_pct` reported (>5% = noise, ineligible); MT8 attribution (the win names a specific ≥0.1% frame).
- Benches skip-green (exit 0) if the model fixture is absent.
- Write the honest ratio + roofline numbers to `PERF_LEDGER.md` with the artifact-graph fields (claim_id, evidence_id, model source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, exact command/env, kill-switch state).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- int4 decode GEMV streams ≤ ~half the int8 weight bytes (measured); decode throughput improves with int4 (memory-bound win, §12); ratio + roofline ledgered; correctness proof (P4-int4-gemm-tests) cited alongside the perf claim.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the bench bead (criterion). Pairs with P4-int4-gemm-tests for the correctness half of the keep-gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-int4-gemm`: benches the kernel it depends on.

title: Phase 5 — CLI hardening + cross-platform release (production single binary, agent-ergonomics maxed)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 5 is the productionization phase of franken_ocr — the pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for Baidu Unlimited-OCR (DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). The kernels and quant recipe (Phases 1–4) are done; Phase 5 turns the working engine into a **shippable, single static binary per target** that an AI agent (the primary user, per G5) can drive with zero context, and certifies the whole thing honest via the three-pillar gauntlet.

The over-arching goals this epic serves (plan §1.1):
- **G3 Pure-Rust single static binary, cross-platform**: one `focr` binary per target (linux x86-64/arm64, darwin x86-64/arm64, windows-msvc x86-64), no Python, no FFI, no network at inference time, no GPU required.
- **G5 Agent-ergonomic**: `robot` subcommand emitting versioned NDJSON with a self-describing `robot schema`, stable exit codes, `--json` everywhere, deterministic output under fixed sampling.
- **G6 Embeddable**: library API stays synchronous/blocking; runtime is an owned detail.
- **G7 Honest**: every accepted divergence ledgered; the release-readiness scorecard must be all-green before ship.

## WHAT TO DO (the Phase 5 task set, plan §10 Phase 5)
Goals: production single-binary release, agent-ergonomics maxed. Tasks:
1. Finalize subcommands / exit codes / env overrides (§7.2, §7.4, §7.5).
2. Robot contract tests + frozen schema (§7.3, AGENTS Agent-Ergonomics Requirements).
3. `doctor` — idempotent, reversible, capability reflection (§7.2, world-class-doctor-mode skill).
4. Per-page streaming polish (mpsc + std::thread, bounded sync_channel, recv_timeout ~40ms loop; §3.3, §7.3).
5. 5-target release build + `install.sh` + SHA-256 checksums (§7.6, /release-preparations, /installer-workmanship).
6. Agent-ergonomics audit (/agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools).
7. Release-readiness scorecard (§8.4).
8. Run the three-pillar gauntlet to convergence (§8.5) and produce the certification bundle (cross-ref E-VERIFY).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Two `[[bin]]` (`focr` short + `franken_ocr` long), both → `src/main.rs`; `fn main()` is SYNCHRONOUS (clap parse → install ctrlc ShutdownController → sync `run(cli)` → exit-code mapping). The asupersync runtime is owned by `OcrEngine` below main (§3.3, AGENTS doctrine #9). This is the franken_whisper template, proven.
- Robot mode is line-oriented NDJSON with a `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` const on every line; human decoration NEVER mixed with machine output in robot mode (AGENTS).
- **v1 is IMAGE-ONLY** — PDF stays off the CLI surface (§7.7): a pure-Rust raster at byte-parity with pymupdf@300DPI is unscoped and any pixel mismatch blows the L0 preprocessing parity gate.
- The release build has a **real gotcha**: franken_ocr uses local `../` path deps (ft-kernel-cpu, ft-core, ft-serialize, asupersync, fsqlite). Path deps break standard GH-Actions CI (release-preparations OP-6) — the 5-target build likely needs Path B (dsr/rch local build) or vendoring. Plan for this.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The gauntlet is the conscience: no victory-on-one-pillar-while-another-regresses; release cannot ship with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence (§8.4, §8.5).
- Cross-compile/nightly: nightly required for stdarch i8mm/dotprod, scalar fallback must cross-compile to every target; runtime SIMD dispatch → one binary per arch.
- Determinism gate: same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy; this is a hard gate for robot mode.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 5 exit gates, plan §10)
- All 5 targets build + smoke-test.
- Robot schema stable + self-describing (`robot schema` self-describes the contract; contract test green).
- Release-readiness scorecard all-green (L0–L5 parity, surface parity, honest perf, determinism, build matrix, ledger completeness).
- `install.sh` verified (curl-pipe install, checksum-verified, idempotent).
- Agent-ergonomics score above bar.
- Three-pillar gauntlet converged (≥10 rounds, ≥2 clean) and certification bundle produced.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4** (int4 refinement — the final quant tier must be locked so the release ships the production weights and the scorecard certifies the shipped numerics) and **E-VERIFY** (the verification gauntlet/conformance machinery — Phase 5 RUNS that machinery to convergence and packages its certification bundle; we do not rebuild it). Each child task carries its own finer-grained deps.

title: Finalize CLI subcommands, exit codes, and env overrides (the stable surface)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The `focr` CLI is the agent-facing contract (G5). Before we can contract-test the robot schema, audit ergonomics, or certify the release, the **command surface, exit-code dictionary, and env-override set must be frozen and complete**. This is the foundational task all other Phase-5 work nests on. Earlier phases shipped stubs (`convert`/`ocr`/`doctor` in Phase 0); Phase 5 finalizes them.

## WHAT TO DO
Finalize the exact subcommand table (plan §7.2), wired in `src/cli.rs` (clap-derive `Cli`/`Command`/`Args`, `to_request()` validation) and dispatched from sync `src/main.rs`:

| Subcommand | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `focr ocr <image>` | Parse a document IMAGE → markdown (human default) or `--json`. v1 IMAGE-ONLY. |
| `focr ocr --robot` (or `focr robot run`) | Streaming NDJSON events for agents |
| `focr convert <safetensors> -o <.focrq> [--arch ...] [--quant int8\|int4]` | Offline weight transformation |
| `focr robot schema` | Self-describing event/contract schema (versioned) |
| `focr robot health` | Diagnostics: model present? arch features? threads? |
| `focr robot backends` | Detected SIMD tiers (SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI/AMX/scalar), core count |
| `focr runs [--id\|--limit\|--format plain\|json\|ndjson]` | Query run history (fsqlite) |
| `focr sync export-jsonl\|import-jsonl` | Audit export/import (locked, atomic, one-way) |
| `focr doctor` | Idempotent self-check/repair |

**Exit codes (stable, documented in `src/error.rs`; plan §7.4):** `0` success · `1` generic error · `2` usage/CLI error · `3` model not found / not resolvable · `4` input decode error (bad image/PDF) · `5` budget/timeout exceeded · `6` cancelled (Ctrl+C) · `7` format/version mismatch (`.focrq`). The `robot run_error` event MUST carry the same code in its payload.

**Env overrides (read once via `OnceLock`; plan §7.5):** `FOCR_MODEL_DIR`, `FOCR_THREADS`, `FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS`, `FOCR_QUANT` (int8/int4/f32 select if multiple `.focrq` present), `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` (override SIMD dispatch for testing), `FOCR_NO_REPEAT_NGRAM` (default 35). Each numerics-affecting lever is documented in its own doc-comment, defaulted by a measured A/B, and has an opt-out.

**Model resolution (`resolve_model(spec)`):** existing path OR short name searched across an ordered, env-driven list (`$FOCR_MODEL_DIR`, `~/.cache/franken_ocr/models`, …), failing with an **actionable** error listing every searched dir. `native_model_available()` does a cheap header sniff (magic bytes), no tensor load.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Exit `1` is NEVER used to mean "ran fine, no results" — that is exit `0` with an empty JSON array (agent-ergonomics Axiom 5). An agent must be able to write `case $? in 3) ...; 5) ...; esac` deterministically.
- `pdf` does NOT appear in the CLI surface in v1 (§7.7) — accepting it would over-promise; PDFs are rasterized out-of-band by the user.
- Every numerics-affecting env var is a documented, measured, opt-out-able lever (no silent numerics changes — G7).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Backwards-compat: we are pre-users with NO tech debt allowed (AGENTS) — finalize the RIGHT surface now, no compatibility shims.
- Stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics (Axiom 4) — no log line may leak into `--json`/NDJSON stdout. Honor `NO_COLOR`/non-TTY for any human decoration.
- `--version` must embed the MIT "Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu" license notice (license risk mitigation, §11).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every subcommand in the table is wired, parses, and returns the correct documented exit code on each error class.
- `error.rs` defines the exit-code enum; a unit test asserts the numeric value of each variant matches §7.4.
- All 6 env vars resolve through a single `OnceLock`-backed config path with a doc-comment each.
- `resolve_model` failure lists every searched directory verbatim in the error.
- `pdf` is absent from `--help` output.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: exit-code numeric stability test (asserts 0..7 mapping); env-override parsing (valid/invalid/unset → default); `resolve_model` happy/missing/ambiguous paths with the actionable-error assertion.
- Integration (P5-subcommands-tests): drive each subcommand via the built binary with structured logging of (argv, exit code, stdout/stderr split), asserting the exit-code contract end-to-end.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4**: the final `--quant int4` path and the `.focrq` format-version must be locked so the `convert` subcommand and `FOCR_QUANT`/exit-7 (format/version mismatch) reflect the shipped reality.

title: Integration tests for CLI subcommand surface + exit-code contract
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The exit-code dictionary and subcommand surface are the part of the contract agents branch on. A regression here silently breaks every downstream agent script, so it needs an end-to-end test that drives the actual built binary (not just the parser), with the comprehensive structured logging this project mandates.

## WHAT TO DO
In `tests/` add a CLI-surface integration test that, for each subcommand and each documented error class, invokes the compiled `focr` binary and asserts:
- the exit code matches §7.4 (`0`/`1`/`2`/`3`/`4`/`5`/`6`/`7`),
- stdout is pure data (parses as JSON when `--json`/robot; no diagnostic lines),
- stderr carries diagnostics only,
- the actionable model-not-found error (exit `3`) lists every searched dir.
Emit one NDJSON log line per case `{case, argv, exit_code, stdout_bytes, stderr_first_line, pass}` so failures are diagnosable from logs alone.

Cover at minimum: `ocr` (missing image → 4; missing model → 3), `convert` (bad/old format → 7), usage error (unknown flag → 2), `robot schema`/`health`/`backends` (→ 0, valid JSON), `doctor` (→ 0 healthy), cancellation (SIGINT → 6, via a budgeted long run), budget exceeded (`FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_*_MS=1` → 5).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Model-gated: cases needing the 6.67 GB weights skip-with-SUCCESS when absent; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent` (AGENTS Testing Policy).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every documented exit code is exercised by at least one case and asserted exact.
- stdout/stderr discipline asserted for every `--json`/robot case.
- Test is green in CI without weights (model-gated cases skip-green).

## TESTS REQUIRED
This bead IS the test deliverable. It must produce the per-case NDJSON log and a summary line `{total, passed, skipped}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env** — it tests exactly that surface.

title: Release-readiness scorecard (all-green ship gate) tying every Phase-5 deliverable together
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The release-readiness scorecard is the single all-green ship gate (plan §8.4, §12). It is the convergent honest evaluation that a release cannot ship past with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence. It aggregates every Phase-5 deliverable into one go/no-go.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §8.4, §10 Phase 5 exit gates, §12 success metrics)
Produce the scorecard with these cells, each green/red with cited evidence:
- **Parity (L0–L5)** all green — per-layer cosine ≥0.9999 (f32), decoded text exact where reference deterministic, int8/int4 CER within ledgered budget (CVaR/tail bound per AF-2, not just mean), determinism gate green. (from P5-gauntlet-to-convergence)
- **Surface parity** — CLI/contract FeatureUniverse dashboard; PDF `excluded`, every other surface `present`. (from P5-gauntlet + P5-robot-contract-tests)
- **Honest perf vs reference** — gauntlet per-stage ratios in `PERF_LEDGER.md` with §9.3 fairness controls; decode-per-token beats the Phase −1 proven CPU reference. (from P5-gauntlet)
- **Determinism** — same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- **Cross-platform build matrix** — all 5 targets build + smoke-test. (from P5-release-build-matrix)
- **Installer** — install.sh verified, checksums present. (from P5-install-sh-checksums)
- **Agent-ergonomics** — audit score above bar. (from P5-agent-ergonomics-audit)
- **Robot schema** — stable + self-describing + contract-tested. (from P5-robot-schema-frozen / P5-robot-contract-tests)
- **Doctor** — idempotent + reversible + ≥700 rubric, fixtures round-trip. (from P5-doctor-fixtures)
- **Deadlock watchdog** — `many_pages_without_deadlock` green. (from P5-streaming-deadlock-watchdog)
- **Ledger completeness** — every accepted divergence in `DISCREPANCIES.md`, every rejected lever in `NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`, certification bundle present. (from P5-certification-bundle)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The scorecard uses the conformal LOWER bound for the parity cell (not the point estimate) — the formal version of "parity gate first" (§8.5).
- A release cannot ship with ANY red cell or an unledgered divergence (G7) — the scorecard is a hard gate, not advisory.
- It binds the whole epic: it depends on every other Phase-5 deliverable so it cannot be green until they all are.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Correctness outranks speed (G1 > G2): a red parity cell blocks ship even if every perf cell is green.
- Vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is GREEN (acceptable per G2 reframing) as long as it's recorded honestly — the scorecard must encode that nuance, not flag it red.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (the Phase 5 exit gate)
- Every scorecard cell green with cited evidence (artifact path / test name / fixture).
- No unledgered divergence; certification bundle present and re-verifies.
- The scorecard is committed and reproducible (a script regenerates it from the artifacts).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A scorecard-validation script that reads each cell's evidence artifact and asserts green; fails if any evidence is missing/stale (structured log `{cell, evidence, status}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
This is the capstone: depends on **P5-gauntlet-to-convergence** + **P5-certification-bundle** (parity/perf/surface/ledger cells), **P5-install-sh-checksums** (installer cell), **P5-agent-ergonomics-audit** (ergonomics cell), **P5-robot-contract-tests** (robot-schema cell), **P5-doctor-fixtures** (doctor cell), and **P5-streaming-deadlock-watchdog** (deadlock cell). It is green only when all are.

title: Freeze the robot NDJSON event schema + self-describing `robot schema`
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Robot mode is the primary agent interface (G5). For agents to parse it durably it must be a **stable, versioned, self-describing** contract. This task freezes the event schema and makes `focr robot schema` emit the machine-readable contract that downstream agents (and the contract test) validate against.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.3, AGENTS Agent-Ergonomics Requirements)
- Define the NDJSON event stream in `src/robot.rs`: one JSON object per line, **every line carrying `schema_version`** (the `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` const). Event types and their fields:
  - `run_start` — run id, input path, mode (base/gundam), args summary, schema_version.
  - `stage` — stage name, seq, elapsed_ms, budget_ms (one of the §4.2 pipeline stages: load/preprocess/tokenize/vision_encode/connector/prefill/decode/postprocess/emit).
  - `page` — per-page text + bbox as it completes (the streaming unit).
  - `run_complete` — totals, output summary.
  - `run_error` — message + the §7.4 exit code in payload.
- Implement `focr robot schema` to emit the self-describing, machine-readable contract for ALL event types (compact single-line JSON, parseable by agents). This is the source of truth the contract test pins.
- Bump `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` deliberately; document the version policy.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Single-line compact JSON even for `robot schema` so simple agent parsers handle it (§7.3).
- Robot mode NEVER mixes human decoration with machine output (AGENTS): stdout is the event stream, stderr is diagnostics.
- The schema is frozen as a fixture so any drift is a CI failure, not a silent break (the `🧪 Pin-The-Contract-Test` operator from agent-ergonomics Axiom 8).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- `page` events carry decoded text + bbox coords (`/999 → ×W/H`); under multi-page R-SWA the pages are cross-page dependent (OQ-13) — the event stream must NOT imply pages are independent; it just reports them as they complete in the single sequential pass.
- Timing fields (`elapsed_ms`) make output non-deterministic — they must be scrubbed in golden/contract comparison (see P5-robot-contract-tests).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every emitted line carries `schema_version == ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- `focr robot schema` emits a complete, self-describing, single-line-JSON contract covering all 5 event types and their fields.
- A frozen JSON schema fixture (`tests/fixtures/robot_schema.json`) is committed and matches the `robot schema` output.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: schema-completeness test (every event type the engine can emit is present in `robot schema`).
- The contract test itself lives in P5-robot-contract-tests (separate bead).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env**: `run_error` must carry the finalized §7.4 exit codes, and the `robot run`/`robot schema`/`robot health`/`robot backends` subcommands must be finalized first.

title: Robot contract test: validate emitted NDJSON against the frozen schema fixture
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
A frozen schema is only enforced if a test fails when emitted events drift from it. This `robot_contract_tests.rs` is the enforcement (AGENTS: "a contract test validates emitted events against a frozen JSON schema fixture"). It is what lets agents trust the contract across versions.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.3, §8.6 golden-artifact: scrubbed NDJSON)
- In `tests/robot_contract_tests.rs`, run an actual `focr ocr --robot` (or `focr robot run`) over a small golden image, capture every NDJSON line, and validate each against the frozen schema fixture (`tests/fixtures/robot_schema.json`):
  - every line is valid JSON, exactly one object per line;
  - every line has `schema_version == ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`;
  - the event sequence is well-formed: `run_start` … `stage`* / `page`* … (`run_complete` XOR `run_error`);
  - each event's fields match the declared types in the schema.
- Apply the **scrubbed** golden pattern (§8.6): scrub timing (`elapsed_ms`), run-id, and durations before comparison so the snapshot is stable; keep structural fields exact.
- Also assert `run_error` payload carries a valid §7.4 exit code on a forced error case (e.g. `/nonexistent` model → 3).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Scrubbed (not exact) golden for NDJSON because timing/run-id are inherently non-deterministic; structure must be exact (§8.6).
- Model-gated: when weights are absent, drive the well-formedness checks with a forced-error run so the test still exercises the schema path and stays green (skip-with-SUCCESS for the full-OCR case).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Test fails if any emitted event field, type, or the event ordering drifts from the fixture.
- Test fails if a line lacks `schema_version` or carries the wrong version.
